summary: Router 4 - G10 Shutdown MOP
description: Shutdown of GigabitEthernet10 interfaces (DMZDNS subnets) on rouvty04 and roufkt04 following confirmation from Loic that no services depend on these interfaces. Current state: 0 packets/sec traffic, incomplete ARP entries, no active devices detected. Subnets being decommissioned: rouvty04: 10.100.200.1/25 (OLDDMZDNS) roufkt04: 10.101.200.1/25 (DMZDNS) This completes the Router 4 legacy infrastructure cleanup initiated on Oct 27, 2025 by kevin.

summary: Email: Syniverse  BGP 172.31.193.126 | ETYX/099974//ZYO is down. | INC000001728630
description: Duplicate ticket to GNOC10123 Syniverse sent an email at 18:27 UTC on regarding BGP 172.31.193.126 | ETYX/099974//ZYO being down. We have referred this to the SME, and they confirmed that the Syniverse router (CPE) is up, as our router has an active BGP session with it. However, since they are not receiving any routes from Syniverse, it appears that the router has lost access to the Syniverse network. Current Status: The SME believes the most likely cause is a fiber vendor issue. We have requested that Syniverse continue its investigation on its end . From: Syniverse Command Center < scc@syniverse.com > Sent: Wednesday, November 5, 2025 1:27 PM To: Syniverse Command Center < scc@syniverse.com >; Global NOC < globalnoc@semtech.com > Cc: CSEmails < CSEmails@syniverse.com > Subject: Re: IP | r004dal01ngn | Numerex | BGP 172.31.193.126 | ETYX/099974//ZYO | INC000001728630 Caution: This email originated outside of Semtech. Hello team, Our vendor indicates that they are currently seeing the circuit up and they are also seeing traffic. However, we are still seeing the circuit down. Could you please validate on your end if you are experiencing any outages that could impact this connection? r004dal01ngn> show bgp summary | match 172.31.193.126 172.31.193.126 22870 0 0 0 58 12:40:01 Active Regards, Alessandro Bermudez Solano NOC Technician T +1 813.233.7870 alessandro.bermudez@syniverse.com syniverse® The world’s most connected company™ Website / X / LinkedIn

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: Email sent to Syniverse From:  Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com > Sent:  Wednesday, November 5, 2025 4:29 PM To:  Syniverse Command Center < scc@syniverse.com >; Global NOC < globalnoc@semtech.com > Cc:  CSEmails < CSEmails@syniverse.com >; Kevin Wohlrabe < kwohlrabe@semtech.com > Subject:  Re: IP | r004-dal01-ngn | Numerex | BGP 172.31.193.126 | ETYX/099974//ZYO | INC000001728630 ||GNOC-10138 Greetings Syniverse,  We have opened ticket  GNOC-10138  for this issue. Our engineers have investigated and confirmed that the Syniverse router (CPE) is up, as our router has an active BGP session with it. However, since we are not receiving any routes from Syniverse, it appears that the router has lost access to the Syniverse network. The engineers believe the most likely cause is a fiber vendor issue. Could you please continue the investigation?

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: Email response from Kevin From:  Kevin Wohlrabe < kwohlrabe@semtech.com > Sent:  Wednesday, November 5, 2025 4:14 PM To:  Ed Guigou < eguigou@semtech.com > Subject:  Re: IP | r004-dal01-ngn | Numerex | BGP 172.31.193.126 | ETYX/099974//ZYO | INC000001728630 yeah we see the syniverse router (cpe) is up because our router has a bgp session up with it. Since we don’t receive any routes from syniverse the router appears to have lost access to the syniverse network. so most probable issue is fiber vendor, but it could be a bad SFP or crossconnect issue at the datacenter but more than likely the fiber vendor is to blame.

summary: Ext: Syniverse-IP | r004-dal01-ngn | Numerex | BGP 172.31.193.126 | ETYX/099974//ZYO | BGP peer down (NSI)
description: On 04 November 2025 at 18:13 UTC , GNOC received an email from Syniverse stating that a BGP peer is currently down since 04 November 2025 at 17:52 GMT . As per the detailed information provided, the following link is affected: IP: 172.31.193.126 Node: r004dal01ngn Customer: Numerex Circuit ID: ETYX/099974//ZYO GNOC has created this JSM ticket to track and monitor the issue. Current Status: The SME believes the most likely cause is a fiber vendor issue. We have requested that Syniverse continue its investigation on its end . Current Status: The BGP link remains unstable (bouncing) as per the latest update from the provider. The provider has informed that they are: Engaging a technician to check the port and fiber at the POP location. Bounced the port as part of initial troubleshooting to restore connectivity. Awaiting confirmation from the customer side to proceed with the onsite technician visit for further checks. Log provided: Logs (GMT): r004dal01ngn> show bgp summary | match 72.31.193.12 172.31.193.126 22870 0 0 0 44 10:30 Active r004dal01ngn> show interfaces descriptions | match 72.31.193.12 ae3.201 up up CLL; 06306; Numerex; Zayo; CID:ETYX/099974//ZYO;IFT516:10; 1gb; s0040505dal01pop xe0/0/25; P2P:numerex1lithiaspringsga:Te0/0/12; 172.31.193.125/30 s0040505dal01pop> show interfaces descriptions | match Numere xe0/0/5 up down CLL; 06306; Numerex; Zayo; CID:ETYX/099974//ZYO; 1gb; P2P:numerex1lithiaspringsga:Te0/0/12; 172.31.193.125/30 xe0/0/5.0 up down CLL; 06306; Numerex; Zayo; CID:ETYX/099974//ZYO; 1gb; P2P:numerex1lithiaspringsga:Te0/0/12; 172.31.193.125/30 Nov 4 17:52:42 r004dal01ngn rpd[4856]: RPDBGPNEIGHBORSTATECHANGED: BGP peer 172.31.193.126 (Internal AS 22870) changed state from Established to Idle (event HoldTime) (instance master) Email

summary: HC : SC Elevated ZENPARK SMS traffic (NSI)
description: On 5th November’2025 , during GNOC’s regular HC, GNOC observed elevated ZENPARK SMS traffic. GNOC investigated the behaviour in OpenSearch & arrived at a Single calling number +447937704086 & 2 top called numbers, +337000020655554, +337000020531476 hitting highest number of transactions. GNOC checked VIAVI & it seems this is a legitimate traffic and with most of the successful traffic. GNOC reached out to SME & leant that this calling number +447937704086 is a disabled smsc account linked to zenpark . GNOC has reached out to SME for more information & advise. NEXT ACTION ITEM: We need to monitor the traffic. If it does not reduce then we need to ask customer via L2 if they are sending legitimate, lot of SMS traffic. (Advised by L3 SME)

NEXT ACTION ITEM: We need to monitor the traffic. If it does not reduce then we need to ask customer via L2 if they are sending legitimate, lot of SMS traffic. (Advised by L3 SME)

summary: Comfone Maintenance Announcement: Lausanne DC SW Upgrade - 20251113_1
description: Dear Customer/Partner, As part of our commitment to maintain a robust and reliable network infrastructure, Comfone is announcing a Maintenance on the core switches in Lausanne Data Center. The maintenance work includes upgrading the network software to the latest vendorrecommended version to ensure optimal performance and stability. Maintenance time start: 13.11.2025, 00:00 (GMT/UTC±00) Maintenance time end: 13.11.2025, 04:00 (GMT/UTC±00) Impact: There will be two brief network interruptions (approximately 1530 seconds each) during the upgrade window. Our switches operate in redundant mode, which means one switch will remain active while its pair is being upgraded, ensuring continuous operation. Expected interruptions: Two brief failover events at Lausanne DC as each switch is upgraded sequentially. STP signaling to Lausanne may fluctuate during switch reloads and failover events The work will minimize service impact as the redundant switch will automatically handle all traffic during each upgrade, and Zurich Data Center infrastructure will remain fully operational and unaffected. Please forward this email to all concerned parties within your organization. If you have any questions and to determine how this work will affect your services, please contact Comfone NOC via the below contact options. Regards, NOC Network Operation Centre

summary: Email: VZW API activation issue with missing Static IP
description: At 19:49 UTC on , Robert copied me on an email from Dave Robins regarding an issue with the Verizon API activation involving missing static IPs. After further investigation, we found that when a SIM is activated in AV for Verizon, a static IP is not displayed in AV, and a manual synchronization must be performed for the static IP to become visible. After working with Andre and Robert, we confirmed that the SIMs are receiving a static IP from Verizon upon activation; however, it is not being displayed in AV. The SIMs are functioning normally and able to communicate without issues. This appears to be a problem with the Verizon API’s communication with AV. According to Jira ticket PLTBUGS11773 , this issue has been ongoing since . Current Status: GNOC has sent an email to Kynief at Verizon requesting an update. Verizon Ticket: INC015671894 From: David Robins < drobins@semtech.com > Sent: Tuesday, November 4, 2025 1:52 PM To: Kynief E Dixon < kynief.dixon@verizonwireless.com >; jordan.harvey@verizonwireless.com Cc: Claudia Johnson VZWVerizon Wireless (NMRX partner) (exNMRX/Uplink) ( Claudia.Johnson@VerizonWireless.com ) < Claudia.Johnson@VerizonWireless.com >; Jeffrey Synnestvedt < jsynnestvedt@semtech.com >; Richard Johnson < rjohnson@semtech.com >; Robert T James < rjames@semtech.com > Subject: Issue: VZW API activation issue with missing Static IP Importance: High Our internal ticket: https://issues.sierrawireless.com/browse/PLTBUGS11773 Verizon Static IP not updating in AV Jeff just updated the ticket... VZW API/Connector Issue with AirVantage: "this is a VZW problem...they are returning 0.0.0.0 Static IP to us after activation is complete. At some point, if you do a synchronize operation in AV, Verizon decides to return the real IP. This is a Verizon problem there's not much we can do about it. I do not know when the IP shows up in the API after the activation but a synchronize operation in AV does then return the proper IP." VZW HighUsage01 Account: 04421254470000

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: I have replied to Dave and Jeff for additional information. From:  Ed Guigou < eguigou@semtech.com > Sent:  Tuesday, November 4, 2025 3:23 PM To:  David Robins < drobins@semtech.com >; Jeffrey Synnestvedt < jsynnestvedt@semtech.com > Cc:  Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com > Subject:  Fw: Issue: VZW API activation issue with missing Static IP ||GNOC-10115 Greetings Dave and Jeff, Robert has asked me to assist with this issue, and we have opened  GNOC-10115 . Dave, have you heard back from Verizon or received a ticket number? Jeff, is this issue customer-impacting? When did the issue start? Just to confirm my understanding — once you activate a SIM in AV for Verizon, they do not return a static IP address. However, if you perform a synchronization operation in AV, Verizon then provides the static IP address. Is that correct? Best Regards,   Global Network Operations Center (GNOC ) SEMTECH

comments.body: I have sent an email to Verizon requesting an update. From:  Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com > Sent:  Wednesday, November 5, 2025 9:06 AM To:  David Robins < drobins@semtech.com >; IOT KyniefDixon < iot.kyniefdixon@verizon.com > Cc:   jordan.harvey@verizonwireless.com  < jordan.harvey@verizonwireless.com >;  Claudia.Johnson@verizonwireless.com  < Claudia.Johnson@verizonwireless.com >; Jeffrey Synnestvedt < jsynnestvedt@semtech.com >; Richard Johnson < rjohnson@semtech.com >; Robert T James < rjames@semtech.com >; Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com > Subject:  Re: [E] Issue: VZW API activation issue with missing Static IP INC015671894 ||GNOC-10115 Greetings Kynief, We are reaching out from the Semtech Global NOC regarding this issue. Could you please provide an update on your ticket  INC015671894 ? Is there any additional information we can provide to assist with troubleshooting this issue?

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: Jeff has sent an email summarizing the issue. From:  Jeffrey Synnestvedt < jsynnestvedt@semtech.com > Sent:  Wednesday, November 5, 2025 9:53 AM To:  Ed Guigou < eguigou@semtech.com >; David Robins < drobins@semtech.com > Cc:  Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com > Subject:  Re: Issue: VZW API activation issue with missing Static IP ||GNOC-10115 The  impact is that airvantage is not displaying the IP of the subscription, it instead displays a dummy value verizon initially gives us: 0.0.0.0  .    The quick summary: after activation when we query verizon's system to get information about the subscription we are given an IP address of 0.0.0.0 at some point verizons system seems to catch up and returns the correct IP - we have no idea how long this takes it seems to vary the display issue in AV can be fixed by forcing another query of verizons system and getting the IP

description: Severe loss in CSREQs for interne.mbqt.net apn via Sparkle on Orange France Postopening on Sparkle on 10/6 Purges from Orange MMEs started coming in instead of Create Session Requests Current Status10/27 Sparkle has confirmed they are resolving DNS queries for this apn of internet.mbqt.net to following PGW ip addresses: 46.23.240.212 , 46.23.240.99 , 46.23.240.100 , 46.23.240.211 with Events logs going towards our side at IP addresses of 46.23.240.99 & , 46.23.240.98 , But, we still are in need of Orange or Sparkle to confirm whether they are seeing Anas’s CSREQs whenever he tests via SIGOS. Kevin Worhlabe has requested most recent IR21 from Sparkle to confirm our routing. Sparkle confirmed they are not seeing the CSREQs come in from Orange France following Anas’s testing on 10/27 at 10:51 UTC However, they are fixated on the device type. comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: I just emailed Sparkle back with the following after looking at Anas’s trace, in which he clearly shows the  internet.mbqt.net  apn getting sent from our hss to the orange france MME Have you gotten Orange France to comment on where they are directing their CSREQs for the  internet.mbqt.net  apn that should be occurring after our successful ULA?  Orange France should be able to trace on this specific ISMI Anas tested with and provide GTPv2 capture for the attempt.  The screenshot below from Anas’s trace on  row 10  clearly shows this apn is getting sent back to the Orange France MME during the LTE registration process ( neui11.mme.epc.mcc208.mnc001.sr.epc.mnc001.mcc222.3gppnetwork.org )

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !!! Update from Sparkle @October 24, 2025 9:06 PM Dear Lewie, I wanted to take a moment to recap the situation and clarify the current status, as reviewing all the emails shows that Valerio and Manuele have already addressed most of your questions. Here’s a summary of the key points:   -You opened a trouble ticket regarding no data sessions for specific IMSIs under Orange France using the VPN " internet.mbqt.net ". -Other IMSIs with different APNs are working fine, so this doesn't seem to be a general issue. -Tests performed with SIGOS failed due to no "Create Session" requests reaching your network. -Other IMSIs using the same APN and within the same network are functioning without issues, so again, this doesn’t appear to be a widespread problem. -Sparkle has confirmed that the issue seems to be with the MNO. However, they are not very cooperative, citing that no major issues have been observed and that this is only a test scenario. -Sparkle has requested that we perform a live session test with you and Orange in order to replicate the scenario. -Sparkle confirmed the APN configurations and hasn't identified any issues on our side showing the DNS resolution. -Sparkle is awaiting scheduling for the live test, which is likely to take place next week. -They’ve made every effort to address the requests, even though this doesn’t seem to be a critical situation.   Given all this, could you clarify what the current issue is? Are we still discussing simulated tool results, or are there actual problems with real subscriber traffic? Do you have any live IMSIs experiencing data traffic issues (i.e., no "Create Session" requests) that we can trace? This would help us push Orange France for further investigation.   From our observations, your subscribers under Orange France seem to be working fine. Below is an example for reference

summary: Alert: SC - COFI-3629 Customer Presence Verte Data session issue following 4G-3G handover Over Orange 901 (CSI)
description: Latest Update: Orange claims to have discovered an issue with MSISDN insertion while modifying the bearer response using GTP. GNOC is getting in touch with L2. Presence Verte: (Since September 25th) Data service degraded when devices fallback from 4G to 3G. This critical issue has been escalated to Orange days ago and is still not fixed. Orange was able to reproduce the issue (on 10/17), however the carrier needs to involve other experts their IP PGW and radio teams. Orange Ticket: S510083629 COFI link : https://issues.sierrawireless.com/projects/COFI/issues/COFI3629?filter=allopenissues 10/28 Conf Call held with Orange901 (Matthiew Labrussias, Sene El Hadj, Krempa Allan & Ameen Ali) & Semtech (EMEA–Sebastien & Ramzi; Semtech Core Support–Minh, Rachid, Anas, Adnen; GNOC–Asim & Lewie Spearman) 1) Semtech’s view of 3GPP specs does not point to the MSIDN includsion within MBResponse as mandatory or influencing the loss of data sessions post CSFB. 2) Semtech is not seeing this issue occur with other R.S. 3) This particular issue flared up after 9/25 4) Ramzi emphasized we were even sending the MSIDN within Modify Bearer response before 9/25, sans issue. 5 ) ORange901 will consult with Mobilium, their R.S. vendor, on seeing if this MSIDN inclusion is at play, although it shouldn’t be. 6) They will research their work order, T509101452, which was performed within their data platform on 9/25, right before Presence Verte started experiencing issues. 10/29 Orange 901 pushed out their followup call slated for 10/29 out to 11/7. 10/29 COFI 3673 Roam Zone completed for getting Presence Verte users through other R.S.s but EMEA is waiting for validation from P.V. before full deployment to production sims. ( https://issues.sierrawireless.com/browse/COFI3673 ) Since issue appears to be within Orange901 connection, hopefully by shifting traffic through alt R.S., we can alleviate symptoms. 10/31 On a conf call with Joelle & Matthieu Labarussias from Orange, along with Seb and Ramzi a

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: Latest News: 4:30 AM UT (as per an email received from Orange) Issue:  Data sessions are interrupted and restart in a loop after CSFB (Circuit Switched Fallback) events, affecting 100% of VIP customers using Orange IMSI 901 in multiple countries (notably Sweden with Telia). Key Details: Problem started September 25, 2025, after Roaming Sponsor platform maintenance Root cause under investigation: "Cancel Location" commands disrupting 3G sessions Tests show issue is specific to Orange IMSI 901 (no problems with other roaming sponsors) Orange technical analysis shows no faults - Diameter and GTP traces appear successful Current Status: Active investigation ongoing with VPLMN (Telenor Sweden) and other stakeholders Main challenges: Limited traces (only 1 IMSI with 3 traces from Sweden), delayed VPLMN responses Orange requests:  More IMSI examples and traces from additional countries (beyond Sweden and France) Next Steps:  Continue trace analysis, await VPLMN feedback, conduct tests with additional VPLMNs, and scheduled meeting to review progress.

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: Latest Updates: No PGW-side action is required for the handover After CSFB, no data traffic is sent to the PGW A new session creation request is received at the PGW without a preceding Delete Session Request Two packets show "destination port unreachable" errors Expected Behavior:  For successful end-to-end CSFB, the PGW session should remain active. If modifications occur, data should be temporarily buffered in the visited network. Outstanding Questions: Why is GTP-U traffic not sent to the PGW during the initial seconds after CSFB? Why was the session recreated instead of maintained? Action Taken:  A ticket has been opened with Affirmed for investigation. Customer Context:  The customer has strict connection quality requirements. Service interruptions under 60 seconds are generally acceptable for most customers, but this case requires investigation to meet their expectations.

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: Ramzi got a reply from Orange(from COFI ticket): After consulting with our EPC_DATA support team, we have noticed that you are inserting the MSISDN field in your responses to GTP Modify Bearer messages.  In the 3GPP TS 29.274 specification, the MSISDN does not appear in the “Modify Bearer Response” message.  We suspect that this is disrupting the operation of our MME when the mobile retrieves the session data. I have shared some MS team notes with Ramzi regarding the next plan of action.

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: In the other hand Ramzi told me that he has informed to customer that the fix is ready on our side. (COFI-3673) He is waiting their feedback to validate so that we can deploy to production sim cards

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: 10/28 – A conf call started up at 8:00 CST/13:00 UTC  with ORange_901, Anas from core support, who can test in SIGOS, and Ramzi and Sebastien from EMEA. Amine from Semtech, and Adnen Abidi from Semtech CORE made the call along with Rachid Benjira. Orange_901 has Labrussias Matthiew, Sene El Hadj, Krempa Allan Ext & Ameen Ali   3GPP specs understanding does not declare the MSIDN included in the modify bearer request is mandatory or would trigger the ongoing session to delete.  We are not seeing this issue occur through other Roaming Sponsors. This peculiar issue started on 9/25. Minh is reaerating that we are not seeing this problem through other Roaming Sponsors.

summary: Migrate av-superset to istio service mesh
description: To add the service into service mesh, it should be deployed with service mesh enabled in helm chart values which adds istioproxy sidecar container to the application Pods and creates VirtualService resource with default configuration. Then, DNS name needs to be migrated from the existing ALB to the new istioingress ALB.
searchable_text: Migrate av-superset to istio service mesh To add the service into service mesh, it should be deployed with service mesh enabled in helm chart values which adds istioproxy sidecar container to the application Pods and creates VirtualService resource with default configuration. Then, DNS name needs to be migrated from the existing ALB to the new istioingress ALB.

summary: Unknown subscriber event not populating for JT in Open Search
description: Hi Faycal, As discussed yesterday, please help resolve the mapping issue for the Unknown subscriber event in OpenSearch. We have validated again and found that in Viavi, the error is visible.
searchable_text: Unknown subscriber event not populating for JT in Open Search Hi Faycal, As discussed yesterday, please help resolve the mapping issue for the Unknown subscriber event in OpenSearch. We have validated again and found that in Viavi, the error is visible.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: @ Wed 10/1/2025 11:19 AM/16:19 UTc Julien advsd into email chain that Amine and Faycal confirmed this type of depiction of traffic is normal behavior within Data Insight. “Indeed all IMSI starting with “23450” which are not found by the HLRHPE  are then processed by the HLRbootstrap, then if it’s not provisioned in the bootstrap it will send a “Unknown sub”. However, there is no event generated by the HLRBoostrap that’s why we can’t see any unknown sub for JT. If I understood correctly, there is no filtering between “SmartSIM” & “R2C” for this range so we can’t see unknown subscriber using DI for JT with the current design.” However, he did inquire to Amine whether he knew how this could be rectified .

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: @ 17:47 UTC, Rachid Benjira did advise that currently HLR bootstrap is “…sending today only UL events (UL, ULR, UL_GPRS). We’ll ask Ouroboros to provide us the feature/mop to send more events to data insight like SAI, AIR.”

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: @ 18:18, Amine offered the following options: We can wait the new probes that should be integrated with kibana to improve the data feed (missing data from HPE-HLR, SMS messages, GTP-C, GTP-U, DNS, Diameter, ...) We can use the data from Viavi directly to check the missing information like unknown-subscriber. (while waiting the new probes) We can ask for a new feature in HLR boostrap to send notification like it’s done in HLR HPE (it will cost us money) Or Study which range of 23450 is not subject to R2C check in boostrap and tune the HLR HPE configuration (it will give a partial view of pure smart-sim unknown subscriber ) to be checked with Fred and Minh

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: @21:32, Minh weighed in, recommending that ultimately Semtech should address the ongoing issue of how lifecycle management for retired/suspended SIMS keeps generating these rashes of unknown subscriber messaging.  on 10/2 at 7:09 UTC, Rachid Benjira concurred.

comments.author: Rachid Benjira
comments.body: We can ask for a new feature in HLR boostrap to send notification like it’s done in HLR HPE   Feature deployed !        [GNOC-9996] HLRBoostrap Upgrade the Site FKT - Jira Service Management

comments.body: Thanks

comments.body: Hi       the changes have been done at HLR bootstrap. Can you do the mapping in the Open search so that we will start getting unknown subscriber events for JT.

comments.body: Hi   , please help on this.

summary: HC: Semtech Monitoring - JT Session Drop | OpenSearch Data Inconsistency post OTA migration (NSI)
description: Greetings ! GNOC Observed a Significant drop in Radius sessions for Customer → Neat electronics in zenoss (Mobile Data Dashboard ) and opensearch (all Signaling Dashboard) radius traffic for Neat since → 25:Oct , 8:16 UTC. There was an Activity > OTA to reduce voice calls with JT IMSI in France and Germany , COFI3493 and COFI3652 , GNOC has validated this using AV and APN sessions on Zenoss Dashboard also N o customer complaints are raised for this drop. > No issues are found on the APN side ( Neat m2m) APN. For AV , The AV shows neat has 117548 active device while open search shows 80942 sims are active in last 24 hrs, concluding the Opensearch has not been ingested the OTA migration details from AV Reference SNIP for the Drop > Sample ICCID →8933240100001409058 not populating traffic after the OTA activity . also after checking AV, for this single Device GNOC is able to check the contionus usage → link Open search Device count > The Active radius Device count for Neat electronics on 22Oct before OTA activity link , is 102415 Last 24 Hrs count for (4 Nov 7:13 UTC to 3rd Nov 7:13 UTC) link Thus GNOC Concluded the 10241580942 = 21473 Devices data is missing on opensearch data Also SME has confirmed Sim cards have been migrated to ESSENTIALS1 and ESSENTIALS2 in the COFI Ticket

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: I had a short discussion with Julien and gather below points:  It was clarified that provisioning changes are not real-time and are pushed periodically. Typically, data is pushed every 2–3 weeks. If there are large changes, a request can be made to Joseph for a new data dump. A future improvement is expected: real-time provisioning ingestion, pending action from Joseph.  I have also asked Julien on  ROAMING PARTNERS - ACTIVE SESSIONS COUNT  in ZenOS for  JT. He is working on this and get back to us.

comments.author: Julien Poisson
comments.body: ROAMING PARTNERS - ACTIVE SESSIONS COUNT  in ZenOss comes from DataInsight Radius DB, so it’s pretty normal to get this behavior, since Data source is from DI. It will be solved when DI enrichment will be corrected.

description: Description Migration of the hosted server AMM99 from 2.17.5 to 2.17.6.1 Impact: The AMM web User Interface will be unavailable during the maintenance window. Gateway operation will be unaffected during this change window. The IP address of this server will change following the procedure. Please confirm with your IT team if your firewall restricts access by IP address. There will be no change to the instance's FQDN. All gateways should be configured to use the server FQDN. In case a gateway running supported firmware and configuration stops checking in after the upgrade, please reboot the gateway and troubleshoot prior to opening a new support case. summary: HC: LTE Authentication Issue with Jersey Telecom (NSI)
description: During a HC, at 16:48 UTC on 29102025, GNOC noticed a increase in ULR and RNA across AT&T, Verizon and Orange across ES3, ADV3 and ADV4 devices mainly for Premier Wireless Solutions. Upon further investigation we noticed a surge in AIR across these VPLMNs. During the investigation we received an email from Velos about them having data connectivity issues with one of their roaming sponsors. GNOC opened a ticket with Velos IOT43685 . Final STATUS : While investigating, GNOC received and email from Velos saying congestion has eased and attachment and authentication signaling services are recovering. We previously created Velos ticket IOT43685. It looks related to the data connectivity issue with their roaming partners they sent emails on. We returned to BAU levels at 20:58 UTC on ULR/AIR and have been stable since. Start Time: 29102025 16:48 UTC End Time: 29102025 20:25 UTC Duration : 3 hours 37 minutes Impacted Customer(s): Premier Wireless Solutions ICCID(s) sampled: 8933240100002805318 Internal IMSI: 234500010032994 MSISDN: 337000020858583 Public IMSI: 234500010032994 Impacted VPLMN: AT&T, Verizon, Orange France Impacted Roaming Partner: Jersey Telecom Country: USA, France Traffic type: Diameter Roaming Partner: Jersey Telecom Traffic Procedure: ULR/AIR Sample ICCID/IMSI: 8933240100002805318/ 234500010032994 Device Behavior: Device Type: ES3, ADV3,ADV4 Start Time: 29102025 16:48 UTC End Time: 29102025 20:25 UTC Duration: 3 hours 37 minutes Affected services: 4G signaling Affected Roaming Partners: JT Affected VPLMN:AT&T, Verizon, Orange France Affected Customers –Premier Wireless Solutions

We returned to BAU levels at 20:58 UTC on ULR/AIR and have been stable since. comments.author: yjadhav
comments.body: Greetings ! Velos provided an updated for the Ticket , The incident occured due to “congestion within one of our carrier partner’s core networks” Hello Global NOC, We have news about your ticket IOT-43685: Dear Semtech, We apologise for the delay in reply to this ticket. The increase in signaling activity across multiple PLMNs was linked to the service incident that reported via email. As mentioned in our previous updates (over emails), the issue was related to congestion within one of our carrier partner’s core networks. Service was fully restored on 29/10/2025 at 20:50 UTC. Please let us know if you require further assistance. Kind regards, Ren Kind Regards, Velos IoT Support Email:  support@velosiot.com Emergency Support Numbers: North America: +1 8336613220 or RoW: +44 20 8068 0990

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: , as per your email from earlier on the Orange ticket  T510229317  , I have requested this same information on the Orange Ticket  T510229324. From:  Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com > Sent:  Monday, November 3, 2025 1:24 PM To:  Mobiquithings NOC [SWI] < mob-noc@sierrawireless.com >;  programmes.travaux@orange.com  < programmes.travaux@orange.com > Cc:  Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com >; Asim Khamari < akhamari@semtech.com > Subject:  Re: OWR;RLCOR4 - CSC OWF LIMOGES;T510229324;MOBIQUITHINGS;Wednesday 19/Nov/2025 at 08:00;Wednesday 03/Dec/2025 at 15:00;;;Without disruption ||GNOC-10094 Dear Orange Team, We acknowledge the notification regarding the upcoming maintenance activity at  Val de Reuil Room 4 , related to the  Add RAM on Data base server + Put ON synch on Roaming Sponsor Room 4 database standby server with new storage disk. However, we would like to request a few clarifications: Could you please elaborate on  the exact scope of the maintenance activity ? What specific operations or configurations are involved in the Add RAM on Data base server + Put ON synch on Roaming Sponsor Room 4 database standby server with new storage disk? The maintenance window is scheduled for  15 days (19Nov – 03 Dec 2025) . Could you clarify  why such an extended duration  is required to complete this activity, especially since it is stated that there will be  no service degradation or interruption ? The notice mentions  “Number of impacted services: 1” . Kindly confirm  which service is impacted  and in what manner, even if there is no expected degradation. Your clarification will help us better assess any potential operational or monitoring considerations on our side.

summary: HC: PCCW- Diameter Traffic Increased(CSI)
description: On 04 November 2025 , during GNOC’s regular Health Check in OpenSearch , GNOC observed an elevation in Diameter traffic for PCCW starting from 03:55 UTC . During the initial investigation, GNOC found that the increase in Diameter traffic was mainly associated with Aperia Technologies , with a minor increase observed for other customers (listed below). Upon investigation with AV , it was identified that there was new activation activity for Aperia Technologies , which could be the reason for the rise in Diameter signaling. Additionally, during detailed analysis, GNOC found that “EPS not provisioned” errors were observed for inventory SIMs of another customer, ARIONEO , around the same time in Australia . This indicates a subscriptionrelated issue for these inventory devices. Current Status: After continuous monitoring for a few hours, GNOC observed that the Diameter traffic returned to normal at 07:00 UTC on 04 November 2025 . GNOC will continue monitoring for a few more hours before completing the ticket. Key Details: Customers: Aperia Technologies, SynoxSGS North America Inc. Traffic Procedure: AIR and ULR Traffic Type: Diameter Roaming Partner: PCCW Device Type: ADVANCED4 VPLMN: AT&T Mobility, Orange France, Rogers Wireless Start Time: 04 November 2025, 03:55 UTC End Time: 04 November 2025, 07:00 UTC Duration: 3 hours 05 minutes Sample ICCID: 8933240100003806081 Reference Snip: OpenSearch Link Synox AV status:

summary: Alert : Increase in SC Bambora Device AB Success traffic (NSI)
description: On 4th November 2025, from 12:07 UTC GNOC received multiple Orange DRA alerts. GNOC investigated the same in OpenSearch & observed there was Increase in SC Bambora Device AB Success traffic without any errors . It seemed some provisioning action was done in AirVantage. But when checked GNOC did not see any provisioning operation. It seems some activity is being done by customer physically . More details as follows. Note: GNOC did not see any errors related to this elevation in traffic. All are success traffic. Hence GNOC believes some activity is being done physically by customer. KPIs are back to normal. GNOC will monitor the KPI for few more minutes before closing the ticket. Keynotes as Follows: Customer : Bambora Device AB Error Type: No errors , only SUCCESS traffic Traffic Procedure: ULR/AIR Traffic type: Diameter Roaming Partner: Orange901 Device Type: Essentials5 VPLMN: Nuuday A/S Country: Denmark Start Time: 11:25 UTC End Time: 12:50 UTC Duration: 1 Hour 25 Minutes Post →

summary: Maingate: Swap end of life router wmg-dna-lahti
description: The aim of change is to swap the end of life and support router(s) used on DNA (All APN / data traffic from DNA) Lahti DC for Maingate wmgdnalahti We will shutdown the old router and replace it by the new one, during the maintenance window the backup router wmgdnaVantaa will handle the traffic. The routers being replaced are for roaming traffic from DNA in maingate, no impact expected as we have already the backup router handling the traffic. NOTE: Even though we don’t expect traffic disruption a maintenance notification is required. Since we need to rely on remote hands at the datacenter we don’t know the exact timeline; this is why the maintenance is longer than normal. We selected multiple customer impact because if we have issues it will cause problems with all DNA APN traffic.

summary: HC:  SC:  WEBB FONTAINE - INCREASE IN LOST SERVICE ON SPACETEL BENIN (CSI)
description: Spotted some irregularities for S6 traffic for Webb Fontaine within past 24 hours during one of the first health checks on 8/21 when confirming data activity for customer, discovered gradual increase for lost service occurrences for this customer in Benin on Spacetel. Am working on a trace and will go from there. Sparkle’s incident ticket is Case CSFA00647022 Spikes in Lost Service are occurring again today, the 22nd, Attached pcaps show the DSREQs come in from Spacetel Benin within a few ms after the GTPv2 session is created and a Radius Start transaction gets sent. After further inspection, the Lost Service occurrences coincide with the rate of User Requests on a daily basis. Also, there are no current or recent SF tickets for this customer. Due to the lack of customer complaints, and also due to the constant rate of output and input octets appearing during the lost service transactions, I am closing this ticket out.

Greetings Sparkle:  We still spot Delete Session Requests coming in within a few seconds after the CSREP is successful for this customer on the following MME belonging to  Spacetel Benin.   mmec14.mmegi8000.mme.epc.mcc616.mnc003.sr.epc.mnc001.mcc222.3gppnetwork.org  In the attached pcap from today for the Sparkle IMSI 222013430575484, the successful GRPv2 traffic is at around row 210m but the Delete Session Request come in at around row 215.  This sudden Delete Request is coinciding with an increase of Lost Service, data-wise, for this particular customer.   The other user, 222013430558236, shows a DSREQ come in within the same second, immediately after the CSREP, again, from the same MME. mmec14.mmegi8000.mme.epc.mcc616.mnc003.sr.epc.mnc001.mcc222.3gppnetwork.org

summary: Alert: SmartSensing seaas-prod - Interrupted Message Flow
description: At 13:34 UTC on Nov 03, 2025, GNOC received a SmartSensing alert with the following message in the Description: Message flow in SmartSensing might be interrupted. More details may be available in the following Slack channel: FINAL Status: Maintenance on Loriot's roaming hub, caused the issue. The expected downtime, as communicated by Loriot, was initially 24 hours. However finally at 15:05 UTC the issue was fixed & alert was cleared. Start Time: 20251103 13:34 UTC End Time: 20251103 15:05 UTC Duration: 1 Hour 31 minutes
searchable_text: Alert: SmartSensing seaas-prod - Interrupted Message Flow At 13:34 UTC on Nov 03, 2025, GNOC received a SmartSensing alert with the following message in the Description: Message flow in SmartSensing might be interrupted. More details may be available in the following Slack channel: FINAL Status: Maintenance on Loriot's roaming hub, caused the issue. The expected downtime, as communicated by Loriot, was initially 24 hours. However finally at 15:05 UTC the issue was fixed & alert was cleared. comments.author: Sema Kimathi
comments.body: , I have assigned this ticket to you and GNOC sent a message to Slack channel  #smartsensing-l3-support  informing of the alert and ticket. Please investigate and let us know if this is customer-impacting.

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: GNOC has sent messages to Chetan and Anton. We have tried to call them but have left messages in teams. I did contact Diego Sandoval and will check with his team on this issue.

comments.author: Anton Beitler
comments.body: Looking at the ‘Last communication’ column in the system table filtered by the Fife Deployment (which uses North LORIOT connectivity), we can confirm, that data flow has ceased Looking at other systems (filtered by the Bield deployment, which uses Semtech-LNS connectivity), we see that Semtech-LNS is not affected: This indicates that the incident is isolated to North Roaming. This is also confirmed by the Canary alert. Background Information: Based on email exchange, we know that LORIOT was planning to perform maintenance on their side to fix a known issue with the roaming hub, see comment in  https://jira.semtech.com/browse/AS-2891 → Hypothesis: This issue may be a symptom of the ongoing maintenance on LORIOT side.

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: According to Diego, A scheduled update is currently taking place on Loriot's roaming hub. The expected downtime, as communicated by Loriot, is 24 hours. They  are clarifying whether there is an additional issue resulting from the update.

comments.author: Anton Beitler
comments.body: Traffic is back Canary also cleared incident:

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: The alert has closed.

comments.author: Anton Beitler
comments.body: At 15:32 CET we notified LORIOT of missing data from their systems.  At 16:04 CET data flow from LORIOT resumed.  No intervention was done on any of Semtech’s systems.

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: We had a P2 alert this morning for Smart Sensing. The issue has been resolved. The L3 team mentioned during the event that the customer might need to be contacted. After speaking with Robert, he requested that Asim review whether a Salesforce ticket warranted communicating this resolved issue to Archangel.

description: Hello, Because we successfully migrated GTP to the new platform, this time we need to decommission the old configuration and clean up the PGW for better readability. Therefore, we decided to decommission the existing configuration with the old vbox. there is no any impact to the traffic.

summary: Migrate av-usage-control to istio service mesh
description: To add the service into service mesh, it should be deployed with service mesh enabled in helm chart values which adds istioproxy sidecar container to the application Pods and creates VirtualService resource with default configuration. Then, DNS name needs to be migrated from the existing ALB to the new istioingress ALB.
searchable_text: Migrate av-usage-control to istio service mesh To add the service into service mesh, it should be deployed with service mesh enabled in helm chart values which adds istioproxy sidecar container to the application Pods and creates VirtualService resource with default configuration. Then, DNS name needs to be migrated from the existing ALB to the new istioingress ALB.

Location Lausanne Maintenance start: 03 rd November 2025, 21:00 UTC+00 Maintenance end: 04 th November 2025, 03:00 UTC+00 Impact: Interruptions of the Signalling links to Comfone Lausanne STP will occur. comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: Statuspage is not required.  Comfone has redundancy on these STP link sets - similar to what was performed in the past.  As mentioned below: Affecting the following:    Interruptions of the Signalling links from/to Comfone Lausanne STP international PC 7-228-0 (16160), international spare PC 0-249-4 (1996) will occur. Not service affecting  as Comfone Lausanne STP international PC 2-061-6 (4590), international spare PC 0-245-1 (1961), Comfone Zurich STP international PC 7-228-1 (16161), international spare PC 0-249-5 (1997), Comfone Zurich  STP international PC 2-061-7 (4591), international spare PC 0-245-2 (1962), will be in service during the maintenance work and process all SS7 signaling traffic. Also reference:

summary: NAM - BICS GRX connection - open traffic
description: CAB is to bring up new BICS GRX connection in NAM. The connection is used to connect the NA PGW (Packet Gateway) to roaming partners and this will provide us additional redundancy from our single vendor Syniverse. No customer impact is expected but in the event of an issue it could impact the NA PGW only (Airadigm Subs, smartconnectivity, etc) . It won’t impact the HLR, SMSC or ECC traffic; It also won’t impact PGW on our Europe PGWs. GNOC / teams should monitor PGW sessions and radius sessions from Data Insights to check for any deviations during the work.

summary: HC:  Semtech - Zenoss Dashboard Traffic Sync issue (NSI)
description: Since 20251027 21:00:00 UTC, during our regular health check, GNOC has noticed that the RNA has increased significantly in the Zenoss Dashboard, along with CSPS location update request drops. The node view looks fine, and GNOC couldn’t find any anomalies or traffic variations in OpenSearch. Reference ticket for the same issue.

summary: HC: Data Insights Monitoring Issues - Missing Parameters  | Data Insights needs Enrichment (NSI)
description: Data Insights Monitoring Issues Missing Parameters | Data Insights needs Enrichment OpenSearch showing small amounts of sporadic System Failures and Cancel Location Request Diameter errors for Sparkle (using Invalid parameter feeds within Data Insights). During the HC, Sparkle is showing System Failures again along with Cancel Location Request. It actually started at 13:10 but it wasn’t much and has been returning to BAU levels but then it peaks again but its been peaking much higher now. We noticed the only country we see is Antartica which Semtech has no subscribers as an example shown below. got on a call with Rashied and Lewie asking about this. Rashied said It is either not provisioned or not yet enriched in Data Insights (OpenSearch/Kibana) so it was referred to the monitoring team Current Status: Data Insights needs enriching. GNOC referred the matter to the Monitoring team. Awaiting a repsonse during normal local business hours. SME reconfirmed: Within Data Insights: “ “ATA” Antartica is the default network when [VPLMNs] arent enriched. So it s a GT or mcc/mnc that is missing. “

summary: HC: Single Customer : Elevation in SMS traffic from customer NCH UK(NSI)
description: Description: On 01 November 2025 , during GNOC’s regular Health Check in OpenSearch , GNOC observed an elevation in SMS traffic for the single customer NCH UK starting from 31st October 2025 at 18:00 UTC . GNOC created this JSM ticket to investigate the sudden increase & coordinated with the L2 SME team to verify if the device is sending legitimate MO SMS or if the spike is potentially caused by spam or abnormal behavior . Key Details: Customer: NCH UK Traffic Procedure: CDRMO Traffic Type: SMS Roaming Partner: Orange901 Device Type: ADVANCED4 VPLMN: TMobile USA, Inc Start Time: 31st October 2025, 19:00 UTC End Time: 31st October 2025, 21:00 UTC Duration: 2 Hours Actions Taken: Observed elevated SMS traffic for NCH UK in OpenSearch. Created this JSM ticket for further investigation. GNOC reached out to L2 SME for deeper analysis. Final Update: L2 SME to review logs and confirm if traffic is legitimate. Initial Investigation and Final Status: GNOC checked the traffic in OpenSearch and observed that the increase is in successful SMS traffic , with no failures detected. and Top callednumber is a single number +337000020550419 . GNOC monitored the traffic trend and observed KPI returend to normal at 21:00 UTC.

summary: Alert: Semtech - PGW High FKT_JT_S8-geo DownLink data transmission (NSI)
description: Since 202050714 23:41 UTC, GNOC received the following alert vipemspgw.mobiquithings.com: The FKTJTS8geo DownLink data transmission rate is unusually high, Current value : 2198 MB/ 5 min GNOC investigated into Zenoss and observed a increase of JTS8GEO Down Link Bytes for FKT. No session drop were observed. SME has been asked to investigate and advise on what caused this increase.
searchable_text: Alert: Semtech - PGW High FKT_JT_S8-geo DownLink data transmission (NSI) Since 202050714 23:41 UTC, GNOC received the following alert vipemspgw.mobiquithings.com: The FKTJTS8geo DownLink data transmission rate is unusually high, Current value : 2198 MB/ 5 min GNOC investigated into Zenoss and observed a increase of JTS8GEO Down Link Bytes for FKT. No session drop were observed. SME has been asked to investigate and advise on what caused this increase.

comments.author: Steven Lee
comments.body: Greetings    Since 20205-07-14 23:41 UTC, GNOC received the following alert vip-ems-pgw.mobiquithings.com: The FKT_JT_S8-geo DownLink data transmission rate is unusually high, Current value : 2198 MB/ 5 min GNOC investigated into Zenoss and observed a increase of JT_S8-GEO Down Link Bytes for FKT. No session drop were observed. Please investigate and advise on what caused this increase.

comments.author: Steven Lee
comments.body: 2025-07-15 01:00 UTC - Anomaly has returned back to normal.

comments.author: Vladimir Florian Bajan
comments.body: Hello, 3GB over 5 min is not that much, a rate of 10MBps. Most probably a customer doing some batch transmission. We could increase the level at which this is triggered.

comments.author: Steven Lee
comments.body: Thanks for this information.  FYI, There was a increase up to 7.5G that lasted for 20 min.  Just would like to clarify some question with you.  1. Down link bytes is the amount of data being downloaded by the customer?? 2. These alerts have been triggering quite often. Lowest I’ve seen is 289 mbps and highest is 8421 mbps.      What new threshold should we set this alert to??  3. What is the breaking point and impact of this down link bytes??

comments.author: Vladimir Florian Bajan
comments.body: Hello, It signalls the amount of traffic on the downlink direction. Also take care on the unit of measure, it is not in mbps but in MB per larger unit of time, in this case 5min. The PGW can handle a lot of traffic and we are putting very little normally. It does have some measure in place for overload control to manage huge spikes. However the values were implemented at the recommandation of the MS, as we do not have a solution capable of stress testing the throughput for the PGW. There are graphs, per each APN, on the EMS, that show the traffic. However we have hundreds of APN, so going through them to identify which customer generated this, manually, is not feasible. Perhaps another automated feature in the monitoring   ? Would this be possible.  I do not know at which limit to put it, perhaps look back 6 months and put it at the 3rd highest level it ever alerted. This is also considering there was never any impact from these traffic spikes so the PGW can hanlde it well.

description: Single Customer Corporate Systems Engineering Known Issue Customer Resumed 5111 Devices | No Service Impact At 20251101 16:35 UTC we noticed a drop in LTE Data Connectivity traffic as single customer, Corporate Systems Engineering (Tony Smith), resumed 5111 devices in Airvantage. This is a known customer issue customer periodically suspends and resumes 5K+ devies annually. When customer Resumes Diameter KPIs go down significantly (for Sparkle/Jersey Telecom) to normal operating levels When customer suspends Diameter KPIs increase significantly (for Sparkle/Jersey Telecom) based upon (suspended) device behavior tramitting ULR/AIR/PUR on the network. Final Update : Corporate Systems Engineering Known Issue Customer Resumed 5111 Devices | No Service Impact Also Reference: Top Customer Details for MVNO Start Time: 20251101 16:35 UTC End Time: N/A; (Until Customer Suspends again August 01, 2026) Duration: N/A; (Until Customer Suspends again August 01, 2026)

summary: HC: (Monitoring) Zenoss STP and SMS Dashboards Missing (NSI)
description: (Monitoring Issue) Zenoss STP and SMS Dashboards Missing | No Service Impact Between Friday, 20251031 and Saturday, 20251101, the STP and SMS Zenoss (Virtana) links were missing within the “MultiGraph Reports” portion of the Zenoss leftwindow pane (tree). GNOC and L3 teams notified the Monitoring team in the #swmonitoringissues slack channel. There was no service impact, however there was a slight loss of visibility into the KPI health of STP and SMS nodes (from a Zenoss perspective). Between 20251101 14:06 17: 25 UTC several Zenoss KPIs stopped reporting (possible VTY Proxy issue), after eing engaged SMEs from Monitoring and Infrastructure teams addressed the issue. Data Insights Unaffected Viavi Unaffected Final Update: STP/SMS Zenoss Dashboards Restored. Zenoss KPI Reporting Restored. Infrastructure team restarted the VTY Proxy fully restoring Zenoss monitoring reporting. comments.body: Greetings  , Since Friday, 2025-10-31, the GNOC and the L3 team noticed that some of the STP and SMS Zenoss (Virtana) links were missing within the “Multi-Graph Reports” portion of the Zenoss left-window pane (tree).  GNOC and L3 teams also mentioned the issue in the #sw-monitoring-issues slack channel.  There is no impact, however there is a slight loss of visibility into the KPI health of STP and SMS nodes (from a Zenoss perspective).  Please investigate to address and resolve.   Awareness:

Unfortunately, today we are having another monitoring issue with KPI not being reported since 2025-11-01 14:06 UTC.     https://swir.zenoss.io/cz0/zport/dmd/reports#reporttree:.zport.dmd.Reports.Multi-Graph%20Reports.R2C.SMSC.AMTP-SMS   Awareness:

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: Greetings  , We are having a Zenoss (Virtana) Monitoring issue - several Zenoss KPIs are not being received.  The Monitoring team reported the folloiwng:    This looks like it is related to Vitry zone. Vitry zenoss collectors are running but relying on the vty proxy (vma-itfrt-05 or 46.23.241.212). This proxy seems to be suffering. Alert on it popped at 01/11/2025 15:42. So it is most likely an Infra issue. Can you take a look?  The Monitoring team is not able to access via SSH or vCenter UI. Also reference slack channel:    Awareness:

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At approx. 2025-11-01 15:30 UTC GNOC left a voicemail for the Infrastructure SME to address issues on   the vty proxy (vma-itfrt-05 or 46.23.241.212).

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2025-11-01 16:39 UTC I spoke with Chris D., he will take look and assess the VTY Proxy.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2025-11-01 17:08 UTC GNOC spoke with Infrastructure Manager, Chris Dingman, he rebooted the VTY Proxy, and by 17:15 UTC the Zenoss was fully restored - confirmed by Montioring & GNOC teams.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: https://issues.sierrawireless.com/browse/COFI-2417             I was just following up on this alert imp request cut back in July of last year.   It appears the trigger for the Alert imp ticket was due to a Birdz ticket in COFI 2417.   Rachid,  is there any plan on the horizon to get SMS failures to trigger GNOC alerts?

comments.author: Adnen Abidi
comments.body: Hello        , based on the ticket analysis the problem was one HA proxy (vma-itfrt-12 vm )  that cause a timeout of requests because the SMSC was unable to reach the vas ; i can't see why you need something related to the SMSC DB at this level , i think we need to check to monitor the HA proxy and not the SMSC or the SMSC DB here to detect this kind of issue unless you want some kind of timeout of sms , and we have already in zenoss a Graph that shows different types of error including Timeout  Graph: SMS Errors - /Server/Linux/Ouroboros/SMSC/SmartSIM/SMSC-ASG - eu-smsca3-asg.aws.mobiquithings.com - smsca3_aws could you specify what you need more ?

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Thanks for explaining.  I did query for any preexisting alerts with  ha proxy  in the message header, and it looks like we are not currently receiving alerts for that network element., nor have we for YTD.   Speaking of that particular SMSC Zenoss feed, could you specify which subfolder would take us there from within this section within Zenoss? The basic intent of the original alert improvement request was to trigger if there is another global SMSC outage.   If it is better to have the trigger from the HA proxy or a trigger for SMS timing out, we are fine with that.  Based on what Rachid said in the email chain, is the HA Proxy still part of the SMSC connectivity flow?   “The Vas and its dependencies are gone. Since last august,  there is a new design where SMSCs are talking to pre-router, router on Av side.”

summary: HC:DRA SPARKLE CT2-SCTP Retransmission Data Chunk Increase
description: Since 20252410 05:10 UTC, GNOC observed an increase of SCTP Retransmission Data Chunks for Sparkle DRA on CT2. We previously had the same issue on CT2 (Sparkle Ticket CSFA00652210) where Sparkle had to reset a link CURRENT STATUS: Sparkle created ticket CSFA00655304 . Sparkle said they don't see an issue but I asked to refer to their previous ticket from 3 weeks ago for the same issue to see if the same fix then, would help now. Waiting on a response. Sparkle asked us to confirm whether the link is stable. Since SCTP Retransmission Data Chunk traffic is now stable, we have replied and also added our new observation in the same email.

GNOC will continue monitoring for any recurrence.

summary: Alert: High Traffic on ELB_HTTP_Code_4XX in Production Environment (NSI)
description: At 12:05 UTC on , GNOC received 2 alerts for High Traffic on ELBHTTPCode4XX in the Production Environment. The alerts indicate a high traffic issue on ELBHTTPCode4XX, with current values exceeding the threshold. Specifically, the values recorded were 12.000000 and 10.000000, indicating a significant number of 4XX errors. The incident involves high traffic, resulting in 4XX errors on specific load balancers in the production environment. Upon reviewing the device URL, no information was available in Zenoss to support troubleshooting. At this time, the issue does not appear to be customerimpacting. GNOC will refer to the SME for investigation.

summary: HC:  Sparkle (double) Fiber Backbone Cut - Roaming Service Degradation (CSI)
description: Sparkle (double) Fiber Backbone Cut Roaming Service Degradation No Customer Reported Tickets Essentials 1 customers may have experienced delays and degradation in SMS, Voice, and Data service establishment. Sparkle is experiencing an ongoing two fiber cut faults on its backbone network. 2 P3 Alerts received 1 alarm lasting 6 minutes, the other alarm cleared within 1 minute Between 20250905 16:34 17:43 UTC Sparkle roaming services were degraded due to a double fiber cut on its national backbone network. Sparkle ticket# CSFA00648950 Final Update: Services Restored. Sparkle confirmed degradation was due to double fiber cut on its national backbone. comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At approximately 2025-09-05 17:10 UTC GNOC began noticing KPI errors increasing.  After investigating GNOC saw that Sparkle was experincing roaming service disturbances across multiple MNOs.  The errors were no composed a large magnitude, the first P3 alerts were triggered at 17:30 UTC and only last a few minutes.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2025-09-05 18:13 UTC GNOC engaged the Sparkle Contact Center sending an email followed by a phone call.  GNOC spoke with Yuga with Sparkle who stated that he received the email and his team would respond.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2025-09-05 18:41 UTC Sparkle responded via email correspondence creating case ticket# CSFA-00648950 for their investigation.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2025-09-05 18:48 UTC the Sparkle NOC Mobile confirmed that, “…the degradation was due to two fiber cut fault ongoing in our national backbone.” “We performed adjustment but it is possible that there is degradation for the next hour until the resolution of the fault.”

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: GNOC began to observe another traffic KPI surge in Sparkle network errors (intermittently) that started at 2025-09-05 20:02 UTC.   At 2025-09-05 20:50 UTC GNOC reached back out to the carrier via email for status update.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2025-09-05 20:52 UTC the Sparkle NOC Mobile team responded with the following: “The Fiber Team has started preparatory works for the cable joint.” “Splicing activity is planned to commence at 20:00 UTC.”

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: 1st Instance Start time:  2025-09-05 16:34 UTC End Time:  2025-09-05 17:43 UTC Duration:  1 hour, 9 minutes   2nd Instance - Intermittent (Degradation) Start Time:  2025-09-05 20:02 UTC End Time:   2025-09-05 20:40 UTC Duration:  Largest traffic degradation spike within 9 minutes

comments.author: syen
comments.body: Traffic appears to have stabilized and returned to normal levels. Communication email has been sent to Sparkle to confirm that the fiber cut issues have been fully repaired and that no further activities are ongoing on the network.

comments.author: syen
comments.body: At 2025-09-05 23:42 UTC, Sparkle Noc Mobile team replied: “The issue should be resolved now. Thank you for your patience.“ GNOC will continue to monitor for any anomalies.

comments.author: syen
comments.body: At 2025-09-05 00:55 UTC, Sparkle Noc Mobile team replied: “Dear Valued Customer,   The team on site resolved the increased attenuation issue after normalizing a portion of bent fiber along motorway and finally link and traffic were UP and stable since 23:19 UTC 05/09/2025  We kindly ask you to check please, yet confirm the TT closure. The TT is set to an automatic closure If no reply is received from your side within the next 06 hours.  Kind Regards, “ Dear Customer, We would like to update you on the Trouble Ticket Number CSFA-00648950 Status: Resolved Service ID: TIS-00000043337 Customer Service ID: Last Update Description: ******************************************** The team on site resolved the increased attenuation issue after normalizing a portion of bent fiber along motorway and finally link and traffic were UP and stable since 23:19 UTC 05/09/2025 ********************************************  Click here  to view and update the case If you don't have access to Customer Community Portal, please, get in touch with your account manager. This is an automatic email, please do not reply. In case of need, please send your messages to  sparklecontactcenter@tisparkle.com   _________________________________________ SPARKLE Sparkle Contact Center Ensuring the best support to our Customers Telecom Italia Sparkle S.p.A. Via di Macchia Palocco, 223 – 00125 ROMA Phone: +39 06 32 1000 Fax: +39 06 5274 4560 Linkedin –  Twitter -  tisparkle.com

comments.author: syen
comments.body: Sparkle NOC Mobile team has confirmed that the issue has been resolved. No further anomalies have been observed, therefore GNOC will proceed to close the ticket.

comments.author: Robert T James
comments.body: Priority = P4.  No service downtime due to network redundancy.

summary: Alert Improvement:  P1 MNO Outage Alerting Implementation [Urgent]
description: Executive Leadership is requesting for the Monitoring team to create alarms that reflect complete MNO outages. “ANY MNO outage should be treated as a P[1] irrespective of the size traffic volume…” Leadership is asking for MNO alerting indicators to detect when “no traffic being sent or received”. Limiting the alert monitoring to MNOs with more than 100 Connections. The Monitoring team has committed to assessing this request. P1 MNO Outage Alerting Implementation ANY MNO outage should be treated as a P1 Limiting the alert monitoring to MNOs with more than 100 Connections. See example of this MNO outage can be found in COFI2352 Orange 901 and VPLMN Orange Guinée (61101)

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: Greetings  , I have selected a soft tentative date of 2024-07-02, nonetheless please review to assess this action item requested and provide an actual Target Due Date for completion.

comments.author: Julien Poisson
comments.body: For the time being we cannot go ahead and implement something, the request is too broad and criteria to define MNO down needs to be discussed, meeting schedule on July 11th.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: The Monitoring team explained that the scope of this project will span several months.  Thus, I moved the target date out  to November 1st.

comments.author: Julien Poisson
comments.body: No budget on DI anymore, so I’m moving the activity to next year.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Julien responded to email inquiry on 1/21 -  pointing out their team will do their best to get done during this quarter we’re in, but it will depend on operational priorities, such as the fresh one that came in lately for LMNO monitoring it may shift a bit.  The work itself could one to two months to finalize, so he is expecting to get started sometime in Feb or March.

comments.author: Julien Poisson
comments.body: As part of the roadmap we’ll perform a 1st iteration to detect country down (several VPLMN down) based on 2 metrics: system failure and Lost service.

comments.author: Julien Poisson
comments.body: 1st phase of MNO done is implemented (not routed to GNOC) around 7 alerts per day. Still required fine tuning

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: I was revisiting an older power point filed I am using for tracking open alert imp requests, and does this recent alert line up with that we discussed on the call on 10/9, as a possible first permutation generated from this ongoing project?  I think the initial description within this ticket was leaning towards setting all VPLMN outages as P1 alerts, so I am not sure whether that is still the working plan or not.   0242ac11-0005-98a0-11f0-9f9bf07b398a kibana: VPLMN DOWN - Telenor Sverige AB [ Diameter/Sigtran - System failure / Unable to comply ] | The number of Failed s I believe this one came in on 10/2. e4fe0b99-28f9-476d-a2d9-9759fa735565-1759415255873

comments.author: Julien Poisson
comments.body: we’ve implemented the VPLMN down alert, considering the effort to define a MNO down, it’s too complex with the current implementation to correlate the alert: coverage, multiple vplmn per country… So we’ll keep as is.

summary: Alert: Increase in System failure via Orange_901 in EU & NAM region (CSI)
description: On 16th July’2025 at 09:29 GNOC received multiple alerts for Orange901 STP link degradation. GNOC checked in OpenSearch & observed that there is increase in System failure via Orange901 in both EU & NAM region. In our Investigation, we found that it was due to " SW’s HLR not getting acknowledgment of the InvInsertSubscriberData message from Orange STP, and after 14 seconds, it has been sending HLR time out with a System failure error . GNOC has created this ticket, released status page, informed L2 SME in SW Production channel. Aside GNOC is engaging Roaming Partner Orange901 for fix & resolution. Orange Ticket ID: S507953208 Orange Roaming Team Ticket: #14653. Notice of Ticket Opening N° 2507H42829 More details as follows, Keynotes as Follows: Customer : Invers GmbH, Networth Telecom, Toyota Material Handling, Thales SIX GTS France – SAPHIR BAR 2023, Solustop, Synox, ETrailer, WEEZEVENT SAS, ZEPLUG, Carrier Fire and Security B.V. etc. Error Type: System Failure Traffic Procedure: UL, ULGPRS Traffic type: SIGTRAN Roaming Partner: Orange901 Device Type: Essentials5 VPLMN: Orange France, Vodafone D2 GmbH, TMobile USA, Inc, Telefónica Germany GmbH & Co. oHG, Vodafone Italia S.p.A, Telia Norge, KPN B.V., Proximus PLC, Telia Sverige AB, Telenor Sverige AB etc Country: France, Germany, USA, Italy, Netherlands etc. Start Time: 09:24 UTC End Time: 12:52 UTC Duration: 3 hours 28 Minutes Sample IMSI/ICCID: 901311532554940 8933240100002543739 Device Behaviour: GNOC took the sample device with IMSI & ICCID 901311532554940 8933240100002543739 & observed that the device is switching MNOs followed by system failure issue. However the session started with a delay. (Snap attached below) Customer Impact: Customer devices are starting sessions with delay. VIAVI Trace: Status Page Timeline: (IN UTC) 09:42 – Initial Status page Sent. 10:01 – Investigation Status page Sent. 12:04 – Next status pae has been sent in investigation Stage. 13:14 – Identified Status Page was shared. 15:11 Moni

comments.body: Greetings !! GNOC has raised the ticket to Orange_901 and provided the sample IMSI.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: GNOC has sent an email Orange Roaming team for resolution .

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Greetings!! We called Orange_901 Contact-  +33 820 89 02 87 , and the representative informed us that they will  transfer the issue to the concerned team/person . We will follow up if no response is received within a reasonable timeframe.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: After a couple of emails and a Call, the Orange Roaming team replied with the ticket number. Hi Dear Colleagues,   Sorry for the delay.   Your request has been raised with the reference  #14653.   We will send you an update asap.   My colleague AMEEN Ali DTOF/DTR  will oversee this ticket.   Hot Line Roaming :  +331 57 36 98 11

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Update from Orange,  Dear Partners,  We observe that there is a issue .  We observe a interruption .  DPC 2-150-4 /OPC 2-202-3  LOGS: 16/07/2025,11:24:58,0236-REPT-LKF: not aligned,**,SLK,3105,A4,xw02s,SLC=00,FECLLI=mobiquit004,CLASS=IPVL 16/07/2025,11:24:58,0318-REPT-LKSTO: link set prohibited,**,LSN,xw02s, 16/07/2025,11:24:59,0316-Route is prohibited,DPC,2-150-4,LSN=xw02s, 16/07/2025,11:24:59,0313-DPC is prohibited,*C,DPC,2-150-4,LSN=xw02s,  Could you please check and update ?  Thanks in advance .  Dylan Teixeira Engineer Customer Service Center – Orange Wholesale International

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Based on the Zenoss charting for today and the previous six days, the session count for orange 901 does not show any extreme deviations from the normal ebb and low on a daily bsis.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: SS7 traffic shows System Failures through Orange 901 remain back to a minuscule level.  Data insights SS7 dashboard for past 24 hrs filtered per System Failures

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !! Some updates from Orange, As per CSCIW, there were some signaling links flapping between our carrier OINIS and your STP. QOS 2G/3G Roaming KPI from Orange

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: With the most recent update:  Following up on Amin's request, Orange added their  @CSCIW VSCO/ICSS  team for further investigation.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings !!! Latest update from Orange . Dear Semtech partner,  Our signaling team are currently checking your request and we will keep you informed within 48h if any update is available. Thank you for your patience.  BR,

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Greetings Team,    Orange Roaming team shares the following observation:  --Email--- Dear Partner,   Our expert has checked and the issue was due to an issue on STP card the 16/05 which lead to a side effect. The affected line was temporarily blocked and then mistakenly unblocked. However, under normal circumstances, this line should have resumed its operation automatically without any manual intervention.   Please apologies for the inconvenience caused   Thank you for your understanding,    They mentioned that the issue on the STP card was on 16/05 (May), but this incident happened in July. I believe they made a typo, and it should not be possible. Pls. suggest.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !!! Later Orange has responded that the issue was on 16/07 not 16/05. It was a typo mistake.  Dear Lewie,  I apologize for the typo in my previous message; I meant to refer to the date 16/07. The impact observed on this card, which is associated with your STPs, is a side effect resulting from another incident that occurred yesterday.  BR,

summary: Alert : Lost Service from Single VPLMN Telekom Deutschland GmbH via JT, Sparkle, MBQT (CSI)
description: On 15th July’2025, since 10:28 UTC, GNOC observed multiple P1, P2, P3 alerts indicating DEGRADED JT Signaling Service. GNOC checked in OpenSeach & concluded the issue was in country Germany for single VPLMN Telekom Deutschland GmbH (TMobile) Via Roaming Partner JT, Sparkle, MBQT for multiple customers like Neat, Invers etc. As JT was highly impacted, GNOC reached out to JT in this regard, requesting an investigation & fix. GNOC also informed the L2 team via SW Production channel & also released the customer notification Status Page. More details as follows, Current Status → KPIs back to normal. No feedbacks received from JT JT/Velos Ticket: IOT40514 CS0121764 Keynotes as Follows: Customer: NEAT Electronics AB, Invers GmbH, CARRIER SCS, RTB GmbH & Co. KG, AssistMe GmbH, SAVVY Telematic Systems AG, AssetLink Global LLC, LugLoc LLC etc Error Type: Lost Service Traffic Procedure: Start/STOP, 2G/3G Auth Traffic type: Radius Roaming Partner: JTMostly, Sparkle, MBQT Very Minor Device Type: Essentials3 & ADVANCED4 VPLMN: Telekom Deutschland GmbH (TMobile) Country: Germany Start Time: 10:25 UTC End Time: 10:57 UTC Duration: 32 Minutes Sample ICCID: 8933240100001244085 Device Behaviour: GNOC took an Essentials3 sample device with ICCID 8933240100001244085, checked in OpenSearch & observed that the device lost services at 10:38 UTC & kept retrying the same VPLMN until it could start a session at 11:08 UTC. They are still investigating the issue, We will inform you as soon as we receive any information from them. Kind regards, Vladislav Kind Regards,

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !! Update from JT & Response from GNOC. Hello Support, Greetings !!   The issue has been resolved. Yes, the issue was reported as shown in the  timeframe & the SIMs were located in Germany. Hello GNOC, We have news about your ticket IOT-40514: Hello, We have received feedback from the networking team. There are no issues observed in the monitoring for T-Mobile Germany. Please advise, is the issue still ongoing or if it only occurred during the time observed in the screenshot and if the SIMs were located in the same location? Kind regards, Vladislav Kind Regards, Velos IoT Support

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra

We are responding them that, “ Customer equipments are  spread across Germany & the issue was encountered by our multiple customers. “     FYIP. Hello, Thank you for the update. However, please clarify, if the SIMs were located in roughly the same are in Germany. Kind regards, Vladislav Kind Regards

JT informed that they have acknowledged our responce and they are working on it:   Hello GNOC, We have news about your ticket IOT-40514: Hello, Thank you for the additional details. We have informed the networking team and will update you as soon as we receive any feedback from them. Kind regards, Vladislav

Unfortunately, as the issue is no longer present, we will be unable to investigate further.  If issues re-occur, please raise a new case with examples for us to investigate further. Kind regards, Vladislav Kind Regards, Velos IoT Support

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: GNOC has responded to JT’s email requesting ticket closure , Hello Team, Greetings !!!  On 7/15 we had supplied sample IMSI & still waiting for a feedback from you. (It was communicated that the network team was looking at the issue.)  However on 07/17 it was communicated from your end, the issue has been resolved & you can’t provide any more update We are eager to know the, cause of  the issue.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Update from JT,     FYIP Hello GNOC, We have news about your ticket IOT-40514: Dear Arabinda, The issue was being investigated by the networking team at the time. However, while the investigation was ongoing, the issue seems to have already been resolved. As was mentioned previously, the networking team did not observe any issues for T-Mobile Germany during their investigation. Unfortunately, in this case, it is not possible to investigate this issue further. Kind regards, Vladislav Kind Regards, Velos IoT Support

summary: Alert:  NA AirVantage - ALMS Platform Disruption (CSI)
description: NA AirVantage / ALMS Platform Service Disruption (CSI) Semtech experienced a brief (13minute) disruption in NA AirVantage / ALMS services, the platforms were unresponsive. This disruption affected UI, API, Device front end, and data push services for NA AirVantage. “'New' operations couldn't be created nor from the UI/API during the blackout since the services were not answering but ongoing operations would continue their processes even if the services was unavailable to the 'outside world'“ Current Status: Services Restored. AirVantage Cloud team will continue to working on identifying the cause and resolution; Investigation is onoging. Cause: Due to a connection spike, avrouting reached its connection limit. We had frequent an m3da connection spike, it is possible that this was a fleet of unregistered devices regularly attempting to push data; Investigation ongoing… Resolution: N/A; Under Investigation… At 20250710 13:10 UTC muliple North American (NA) AirVantage (AV) alarms were received and at 13:12 UTC the AV Cloud team reported issues with the NA AV services, indicating different apps were not responding. SME stated that the web apps were not responding, the NA AV platform web page was unresponsive. This disruption affected UI, API, Device front end, and data push services for NA AirVantage. Start Time: 20250710 13:10 UTC End Time: 20250710 13:23 UTC Duration: 13 minutes

summary: ALERT: Sparkle Network Connectivity Incident (CSI)
description: GNOC RCA: GNOC8689: 07022025 Sparkle Network Connectivity Incident Final RCA Note: Case downgraded from P2 to P3 determined by postincident assessment. No Customer complaints Minimal Impact 1.5% Data session loss Sparkle Network Connectivity Incident Fiber Cut | No Customer Tickets Reported Final Update: Services Restored. RCA received Major (backbone transport) Fault, Fiber Cut; Sparkle's thirdparty provider restored services by repairing the Fiber circuit link between the cities of Roma and Bologna. Incident was elevated to a Level 2 carrier escalation. GNOC8689: 07022025 Sparkle Network Connectivity Incident Final RCA Sparkle ticket# CSFA00640559 Semtech Data Service Timeline ( See Chargepoint Data Sessions sample ): Start Time: 20250702 22:21 UTC End Time: 20250702 23:06 UTC Duration: 45 minutes Sparkle Incident Timline Start Time: 20250702 22:07 UTC End Time: 20250702 23:43 UTC Duration: 1 hour, 36 minutes Additional Details: Between 20250702 22:07 23:43 UTC, Essentials 1 subscription offers in the EU and NAM markets may have experienced delays and failures in SMS, USSD, Voice, and Data service establishment, as well as network connectivity. Although Sparkle’s incident lasted 1 hour, 36 minutes, the first 45 minutes of existing Data Sessions were impacted until 23:07 UTC. At 23:07 UTC, Data Service recovered; Existing Data sessions were unaffected, however, there may have been delays establishing new sessions. For the entirety of the incident, Essentials 1 customers may have experienced delays in SMS, USSD, Voice, Data service establishment, and network connectivity. After 23:06 UTC, existing Data sessions were unaffected; however, there may be delays establishing new sessions. Note (from PostIncident Assessment): In accordance with the Incident Management Process, GNOC management has reclassified the incident (lowered severity) from a Priority 2 (P2) to a Priority 3 (P3). For context, ChargePoint—the largest customer utilizing Essentials 1 on the Sparkle netwo
