comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings !!! GNOC has  sent out resolved status page notification at 03:48 UTC .

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings ALL !!! GNOC has received RFO from Sparkle  The issue was due to a Major Fault - Linkdown on Italian Backbone (INC000000421831).   A fiber cut was present between Roma and Bologna since 21:13 UTC+2 02/07/2025 Our provider has been involved in futher investigation the issue.  A part of the circuit was involved in a Fiber Cut, that has been resolved after the intervention of the fiber team of the provider around 23:50 UTC.  Another part of this circuit has been involved in another fault that has resolved around 23:30 UTC.  Link has been kept under monitoring successfully. No more outage is expected.

summary: Alert:  VTY PGW nonresponsive Data/SMS/Voice Services in Multiple Regions (CSI)
description: GNOC RCA: GNOC8669: 06302025 VTY PGW Impairment Final RCA Executive Summary: The Semtech network experienced a signaling storm over all 4 major Roaming Partners. Essentials 1/2/3/5 and Advanced subscription users experienced failures and delays in establishing 2G/3G/LTE service for SMS/Voice/USSD and Data sessions. The SMEs were engaged, and they determined that sessions were unable to establish any data connections from the PGW in VTY. The SMEs decided to move traffic to the FKT PGW. The SMEs believe that the congestion on the PGW in VTY caused the network to crash. At 21:21 UTC, GNOC received an influx of P1, P2, and P3 signaling degradation alerts from JT/BICS/Orange/Sparkle alerts. After reviewing the KPIs, we are seeing a major increase in signaling traffic from all RPs. GNOC opened a bridge, and SMEs joined. They observed the HLR rejecting calls. GNOC received an email from BICS stating that they had detected a GTPv1/GTPv2 traffic failure towards Semtech. Traffic fails without any answer from your side. GNOC received a call from JT, and they were seeing an enormous failed traffic from Semtech. JT joined the bridge. Current Status: The SMEs are working with JT to block EU number ranges to restore traffic. Range has been reopened and system are back online COFI Ticket → COFI3363 Duration: 3 hours 27 minutes Start Time: 20250630 21:21 UTC End Time: 20250701 00:48 UTC Impacted Customer (Salesforces Ticket) Invers 01871623 Carrier Fire and Security B.V. 01871628 Legrand AB 01871637 THALES SIX GTS France SAS 01871647 AMS Systemer 01871675 StatusPage Initial StatusPage (Investigation) 20250630 21:50 UTC Second StatusPage (Investigation) 20250630 22:53 UTC Third StatusPage (Identified) 20250630 23:26 UTC Fourth StatusPage (Identified) 20250701 00:26 UTC Fifth StatusPage (Identified) 20250701 00:56 UTC Sixth StatusPage (Monitor) 20250701 01:22 UTC Seventh StatusPage (Monitor) 20250701 02:22 UTC Eighth Status Page (Resolved) 20250701 07:29 UTC Ticket Number BICS Ticket:

comments.author: Steven Lee
comments.body: At 2025-06-30 21:21 UTC, GNOC received multiple P1, P2 alerts. Upon investigation, GNOC observed a major Signaling increase for all roaming partner (JT, Orange, BICS, and Sparkle). GNOC engaged SME and opened a bridge. Based on initial investigation and alerts received, this only impacted Signaling. However, this impact was bigger than expected. LTE and Sigtran (PS) was also impacted. JT reached out to us because they are seeing large amount of failure from us. They also said that this originated from our side. Richard joined that soon after and asked GNOC to invite JT on to the bridge as well. After JT got on and performed their assessment, JT saw that we are maxing out our TPS (400) on our STP linksets. After discussing on the bridge, JT blocked all ranged to clear the network. Shortly after, improvements were being observed and JT started to slowly release the range. While doing so, HLR restart was performed and also some additional action were performed on the PGW. At 2025-07-01 00:48 UTC, session improvement were observed in Zenoss. SME and Richard said that they believe that there was a congestion on the PGW that caused the network to crash. They also mentioned that this may also be caused by a customer having issue on their network, which eventually caused this signaling storm. SME said that they will provide the root cause tomorrow during their business hours. ------Duration: 3 hours 27 minutes------ Start Time:  2025-06-30 21:21 UTC End Time:  2025-07-01 00:48 UTC ------Impacted Customer (Salesforces Ticket)------ Invers  01871623 Carrier Fire and Security B.V.  01871628 Legrand AB  01871637 THALES SIX GTS France SAS  01871647 AMS Systemer  01871675 ------Ticket Number------ BICS Ticket Number:  SC4546503 JT Ticket Number:  IOT-40148 ------StatusPage------ Initial StatusPage (Investigation) -  2025-06-30 21:50 UTC Second StatusPage (Investigation) -  2025-06-30 22:53 UTC Third StatusPage (Identified) -  2025-06-30 23:26 UTC Fourth StatusPage (Identified) -  2025-07-01 00:26 UTC Fifth StatusPage (Identified) -  2025-07-01 00:56 UTC Sixth StatusPage (Monitor) -  2025-07-01 01:22 UTC Seventh StatusPage (Monitor) -  2025-07-01 02:22 UTC Steven Lee Steven Lee    06:29 At 2025-06-30 21:21 UTC, GNOC received multiple P1, P2 alerts. JT reached out to us because they are seeing large amount of failure from us. They also said that this originated from our side. Richard joined that soon after and asked GNOC to invite JT on to the bridge as well. After JT got on and performed their assessment, JT saw that we are maxing out our TPS (400) on our STP linksets. After discussing on the bridge, JT blocked all ranged to clear the network. Shortly after, improvements were being observed and JT started to slowly release the range. While doing so, HLR restart was performed and also some additional action were performed on the PGW. At 2025-07-01 00:48 UTC, session improvement were observed in Zenoss. SME and Richard said that they believe that there was a congestion on the PGW that caused the network to crash. They also mentioned that this may also be caused by a customer having issue on their network, which eventually caused this signaling storm. SME said that they will provide the root cause tomorrow during their business hours. ------Duration: 3 hours 27 minutes------ Start Time:  2025-06-30 21:21 UTC End Time:  2025-07-01 00:48 UTC ------Impacted Customer (Salesforces Ticket)------ Invers  01871623 Carrier Fire and Security B.V.  01871628 Legrand AB  01871637 THALES SIX GTS France SAS  01871647 AMS Systemer  01871675 ------Ticket Number------ BICS Ticket Number:  SC4546503 JT Ticket Number:  IOT-40148 ------StatusPage------ Initial StatusPage (Investigation) -  2025-06-30 21:50 UTC Second StatusPage (Investigation) -  2025-06-30 22:53 UTC Third StatusPage (Identified) -  2025-06-30 23:26 UTC Fourth StatusPage (Identified) -  2025-07-01 00:26 UTC Fifth StatusPage (Identified) -  2025-07-01 00:56 UTC Sixth StatusPage (Monitor) -  2025-07-01 01:22 UTC Seventh StatusPage (Monitor) -  2025-07-01 02:22 UTC

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !! Summary of incident. Executive Summary: Potentially an issue was detected with the Semtech PGW (VTY PGW) in the core network, where data session requests from devices were being rejected. While device registrations (attach procedures) were successful, the PGW failed to establish data sessions, causing devices to repeatedly re-register. This repetitive signaling behavior led to congestion on the SS7/SIGTRAN links, resulting in increased system failures and delays/drops in SMS and voice services across the network. As a consequence, some devices temporarily switched from 4G to 2G/3G to maintain service continuity. The situation has stabilized, with affected devices gradually returning to 4G as signaling loads normalize. A detailed root cause analysis is pending from the SMEs. Interim mitigation and monitoring are in place to ensure continued network recovery and prevent recurrence. Incident Summary : On 30 th  June’2025 at 21:21 UTC, GNOC received an influx of P1, P2, and P3 signaling degradation alerts for multiple Roaming Partners like JT/BICS/Orange/Sparkle alerts. After reviewing the KPIs, in OpenSearch GNOC observed a major increase in signaling traffic from all Roaming Partners. GNOC informed SMEs on SW Production channel at 22:00 UTC. Then GNOC opened a Teams bridge. Roaming Partner JT & Semtech SMEs joined.  Initial investigation done by Semtech SMEs pointed issue to Semtech HLR rejecting calls. However detailed analysis & root -cause pending from SME.   Service Impact: Data Services were impacted for new/existing sessions. However there was delay in SMS & Voice Services.   Progress During Investigation & Restoration of Services:   At 22:14 UTC BICS sent Semtech an email highlighting issue with our PGW. At 22:50 UTC JT/Velos sent Semtech an email highlighting issue with Semtech’s HLR.  Eventually at 11:12 UTC, JT informed Semtech that current TPS was at 14400, if it would have hit 15000, JT would have been forced to bring down Semtech  IMSI ranges.  After discussing on the Teams bridge, JT blocked all ranges om IMSI to clear their network. Shortly after, improvements were being observed and JT started to slowly release the range. While doing so, Semtech HLR restart was performed and also some additional corrective actions were performed on the PGW.  At 2025-07-01 00:48 UTC, session improvement were observed in Zenoss. Semtech SME and SLT believe it to be a  congestion on the PGW that caused the network to crash. It is also believe that a Semtech customer having issue on their network, which eventually caused this signaling storm.   Technical Investigation by GNOC:  GNOC took a sample IMSI 208243091292771 for Invers GmbH & checked in VIAVI. It was observed that there  was issue with GTPv1 with error No Resource Available .  (Generic temporary error condition indicating that some resource is used up at the moment (excluding the conditions all dynamic PDP addresses are occupied and no memory is available).   GNOC checked traces in Packet Core, also system failure was observed.  (Logs Attached Below)  It Seems there was an issue with Semtech/s PGW, (VTY PGW), affecting 4G Data Sessions. Device registration was successful, but was unable to start a session, as there was rejection from the PGW when device requested to start a session. Devices kept re-registering repeatedly which eventually congested SS7/SIGTRAN link resulting increase in System Failure which resulted in delay in SMS & Voice Services.  So, eventually some devices switched RAT from 4G to 2G/3G. (These devices are slowly re-registering & changing RAT back to 4G)    Update from BICS:  Investigation from BIC end was pointing towards issues with Semtech’s PGW BICS SGSN (213.144.191.x) sends Create PDP Context Request →  Semtech GGSN (46.23.240.99/100)  completely silent  →  SGSN times out waiting for response  → No data session established  To add upto the above statement, GNOC also observed that there was traffic shift from VTY PGW to FKT PGW at around 00:45 UTC (1 st  July)    Logs: (Semtech) Packet Core cause distribution for above IMSI. Bold letter indicates number of errors received between 21:20 and 00:00.  GTPv1 System failure   123   1            0.40327868852459015 GTPv1 No resource available   88   1  0.28852459016393445 GTPv1 Request accepted   63   1        0.20655737704918034 GTPv2 Request accepted   21   1        0.06885245901639345 GTPv2 Context Not Found   4   1        0.013114754098360656 GTPv1 No Cause   2   1                        0.006557377049180328 GTPv2 Request rejected reason not specified  2  1  0.006557377049180328 GTPv1 Non-existent   1   1                   0.003278688524590164 GTPv2 System failure   1   1                      0.003278688524590164 Approximate Impacted Device Counts: (For Lost Service) GNOC checked in OpenSearch & observed that there are approximate 75k devices had lost service.   Time Frame:   June30, 21:21 UTC to July1 12:48 UTC → Device Count for Lost Service = 94,264  (OpenSearch_link) Time Frame:   June29, 21:21 UTC to June30 12:48 UTC  → Device Count for Lost Service =  19,545  (OpenSearch_link)

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Update from Orange --> Hello Last night, we were confronted with disruptions on the Roaming Sponsor 901 solution. Your services were impacted from 11:30 p.m. to 4:15 a.m. We have encountered a spike in signalling from a third-party operator that we have regulated. Since 4:15 a.m., the service has been at nominal level. Our expert teams remain mobilized and the situation is being monitored. Kind regards Centre Support Client Orange Wholesale

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: We have investigated the issue using Viavi and found the following: Upon analyzing multiple scenarios in packet core traces, we observed that both  GTPv1  and  GTPv2  sessions are being consistently rejected by our  PGW/GGSN  with cause values: “No resources available . ” “System failure” In parallel, we reviewed the  SS7 signaling traces  and observed that the majority of failures are associated with  I-HSS-FE1-VTY  and  I-HSS-FE2-VTY  nodes.

comments.author: Mohamed Amine Elbahi
comments.body: Imsi range are defined in confluence page  https://confluence.sierrawireless.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=70660164 To have a estimation of traffic per prefix (Example JT): use superset: SELECT SUBSTR(imsi, 1, 9) AS imsi_prefix,        COUNT(*) AS count FROM hlr_hpe_notif_sigtran WHERE year = '2025'   AND month = '06'   AND day = '30'   AND roaming_partner = 'JT' GROUP BY SUBSTR(imsi, 1, 9) ORDER BY COUNT DESC

Kind regards, Customer Care Tel. +32 2 547 51 51 BICS SA/NV www.bics.com

summary: ALERT:  Orange Network Congestion Incident - Affecting Essentials 5 Connectivity (CSI)
description: GNOC RCA: GNOC8633: 06262025 Orange Network Congestion Incident Final RCA Orange Network Congestion Incident Affecting Essentials 5 Connectivity (CSI) | Partner Escalation 1st Instance: Between 20250626 12:04 – 13:21 UTC Orange experienced a network disruption affecting Semtech’s Essentials 5 customer Data services and connectivity. The Orange network service disruption caused intermittent connectivity affecting several customers across multiple MNOs. 2nd Instance: Between 20250626 15:37 20250626 22:00 UTC Another issue or recurrence of the issue began causing Essentials 5 customers Delays and Degradation in USSD, SMS, Voice, and Data service establishment. LTE and LPWA Data services and network connectivity were minimally affected. Existing Data Services are unaffected. GNOC initiated a Level 3 carrier escalation to drive partner resolution. Final Update: Services Restored. Orange encountered Signaling Storms (Roaming Sponsor 208 situation) that they mitigated traffic filtering to gradually restore services. Orange restored its Roaming Services to BAU carrier levels by 20250627 15:00 UTC. Semtech Customer Services were fully restored by 20250626 22:00 UTC. 2nd Instance: Degradation and Delays in USSD, SMS, Voice, and Data service establishment. Existing Data Services are unaffected. GNOC initiated a Level 3 carrier escalation. 1st Instance: Essentials 5 customers may have experienced delays and failures in establishing services for Data and network connectivity. comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2025-06-25 17:51 UTC, I called into Orange and spoke with Ahmed, engineer with the Orange Customer Services Centre, to escalate the incident.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: We started up a bridge at around 2:45 pm/CST19:45 UTC, or shorlty thereafter.. we got jean pierre and SEabsiten from EMEA support. and Amine joined the call . After I called to Matthieu once and requested orange participation no the bridge and then getting his voicemail. on the 2nd attemp. Rashied called to Joelle at Orange 901.  Matthieu from Orange  finally responded to GNOC inbox with the following at 20:34 UTC   Hello, We understand your situation, but all our experts are involved in the current incident. It is not possible for us to dedicate a person to your bridge. All our customers are affected. We will get back to you as soon as we have any progress to report. Regards,

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: starting shortly after 20:00 we noticed a precipitous drop in SIGTRAN traffic from Orange… and this was pointed out to Orange via email and over the phone earlier.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: (Partial) Discussion items mentioned on technical bridge: Thales request to move RZT - Cancelled At 2025-06-25 21:04 UTC  the proposal to move Thales MSISDNs to the newly roaming zone enabling Bouygues with BICS imsi  was cancelled. Reference:   COFI-3353   01871006: RS03 alert increase Orange ticket#  S506921009  raised to a Level 3 Escalation At 2025-06-25 20:29 UTC  GNOC called and spoke to the Orange Director of Client Services, Joelle Guimet, raising the incident to a Level 3 escalation.  Joelle acknowledged that the carrier had a big problem on the Orange network, and stated her teams would not be able to join a joint-technical Teams bridge.  She mentioned that their internal resources were already engaged on multiple bridges working towards resolution.  She committed to sending ojt periodic update via email and within the Orange portal.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: we just received following from Orange 901 inside their online portal system at 17:03 CST/22:03 UTC Hello, Our Roaming Sponsor solution is experiencing a significant disruption. We are facing a signalings storm, requiring careful monitoring and traffic filtering to gradually restore the service. Our teams and the top management remain fully committed to reestablishing operations. We sincerely apologize for any inconvenience caused. Best Regards, Matthieu Status Page is not ready to change to Monitoring status as of 22:07 UTC… kibana still hasn’t recuperated completely…

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2025-06-25 22:02 UTC the Orange Wholesale Customer Care Team sent another status update: We are still experiencing a signaling storm on our mobile network. Our teams are mobilized and security operations will continue throughout the night. We will keep you informed of any developments in the situation. We sincerely apologize for any inconvenience caused.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Greetings,  A few  P2 and P3 Orange linkset alerts  were triggered, indicating a possible disturbance in the Orange linkset. Upon checking in  Zenoss , it appears that  STP links  pstpi_m1_fkt_tk1  and  pstpi_m1_fkt_tk2  have stopped receiving traffic. The good thing is that the  traffic has successfully shifted to the VTY linkset ( VTY_tk1  and  VTY_tk2 ) , which suggests  no major impact on signaling traffic from Orange  at this point. We have informed SME to review the situation and if any corrective action is required from our side (e.g., restarting the FKT STP links). We are reaching out to Orange regarding this matter. Thanks !!

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Greetings !! As Orange is still observing some issues, we are keeping the Status page in Monitoring state and have sent the updated monitoring status page at 05:09 UTC on 27th June.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: As per the latest update from Orange, they are still observing some issues in Signaling. They are working on it.   Hello, Following our teams' mobilization, the initial incident affecting Orange France's core network has been resolved. However, we are experiencing ongoing impact on our Roaming Sponsor services, which are facing signaling peaks, preventing service from returning to normal operations. Our technical and management teams are fully engaged in finding a solution to restore traffic as quickly as possible. Orange sincerely apologizes for any inconvenience caused.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: The latest update from Orange: Hello, Technical operations are currently in progress to gradually restore Roaming Sponsor 208. Our teams and senior management are fully mobilized, and we will keep you informed of the operation's progress. We kindly request that you accept our sincere apologies for any inconvenience caused. Best Regards, Orange Wholesale Customer Care Team

comments.body: Resolved Status page sent.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: On  26 June at 12:04 UTC , Semtech's Essentials 5 and ADVANCED4,  ADVANCED3 customers experienced connectivity failures across the Orange roaming network. Traffic analysis showed  USSD, SMS, Voice, and Data connection requests  were being rejected along with huge  signaling storm occurrences  and  timeout responses , indicating Orange's roaming sponsor systems were  rejecting the device registration and possibly interruption in data session uses. The incident occurred in  two phases : initial  77-minute disruption (12:04-13:21 UTC)  reported as resolved, followed by  extended outage starting 15:37 UTC  continuing beyond the reported  22:00 UTC resolution time . 1st Instance:  Essentials 5 customers may have lost the Session and experienced delays and failures in establishing services for Data and network connectivity. Start Time:  2025-06-26 12:04 UTC End Time:  2025-06-26 13:21 UTC Duration:  1 hour, 17 minutes   2nd Instance: Essentials 5 subscribers may have delays in USSD, SMS, Voice, and Data service establishment. Start Time:  2025-06-26 15:37 UTC End Time:  2025-06-26 22:00 UTC Duration:  6 hours, 23 minutes ORANGE STATUS FOR THE INCIDENT - Orange has acknowledged a  major network incident  affecting their mobile infrastructure that began on June 26, 2025. They confirmed they are experiencing a "signaling storm" that has caused significant disruption to their mobile network and Roaming Sponsor solution, requiring careful traffic filtering and monitoring to gradually restore services. Orange has escalated the incident to the highest levels, with top management and expert teams fully mobilized for around-the-clock operations. Orange  has deployed multiple technical bridges in parallel to identify the root cause while implementing cautious traffic management to prevent further network degradation. Orange has issued multiple customer communications acknowledging the severity of the situation and apologizing for the inconvenience, though they have not provided specific details about estimated restoration time, or the underlying cause of the signaling storm that triggered this widespread service disruption. The  root   cause  remains  under active investigation  by Orange engineering teams, with contributing factors including  signaling capacity overload ,  roaming sponsor routing failures , and  STP infrastructure instability . GNOC maintains  Level 3 carrier escalation  with Orange committed to providing  ETA for STP link restoration  and  continued signaling storm mitigation .   KEY NOTES – Impacted Customer(s):   Invers GmbH, Carrier Fire and Security B.V., Toyota Material Handling, ZEPLUG, Thales SIX GTS France – SAPHIR BAR 2023, Wilson Electronics, Solustop, Bambora Device AB, Synox, Networth Telecom Impacted Roaming Partner:  Orange Impacted VPLMN:           Orange France, Vodafone D2 GmbH, KPN B.V., TelefÃ³nica Germany GmbH & Co. oHG, Telia Sverige AB, Telia Nattjanster Norden AB, Telecom Italia SpA, Nuuday A/S Country / Region:  EU, NAM Traffic type:  2G/3G and LTE Data services and network connectivity Device Type:  ESSENTIALS5, Advanced4

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: I called to Joelle earlier in the day at 9:09 & 10:34 CST/14:09 & 15:34 UTC, and left a vm message, explaining that we needed them to fully confirm the extent of the outage, whether it was isolated to France (Orange France 208-01), and if not, to how many other VPLMNs and what types of protocols were affected. She called me back later on 16:47 UTC and she committed to getting us a better description of their assessment of the issue sometime today. I explained that we want their definition of the magnitude issue while conceding that an RCA may take another day or so.  As of 19:56 UTC, their ticket only states the following at 14:51 UTC  Hello, Following our last communication, we propose that you close the ticket. An RCA will be sent to you by our customer service managers. If you do not return within 24 hours, this incident will be automatically closed. Best regards, Matthieu Zenoss does show a surge in signaling during the incident.  And, the following screenshots indicate a severe droppage in key customer data sessions: Droppage in Active Session Count traversing Orange 901 on 6/26 Top 3 Customer Average Session Count shows Invers counts dropped by half:

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !! Update from Orange . Hello,   Please find a detailed incident report, about June 26th Roaming Sponsor 20801 incident, while we wait to send you an RCA.   Outage Period:   ·        Roaming sponsor (20801) start of the impact 26 June 2025, 7:15 PM (UTC+2)  ·        Recovery of the service : 27 June 2025, 5:00 PM (UTC+2)  Incident Description   ·        Incident began with a sharp rise in CPU load on the national mobile DNS (June 26, 5:10 PM UTC+2), degrading 4G/5G domestic voice/data services and roaming ·        This caused an over-incident on roaming sponsor service by triggering a signaling storm on the roaming sponsor services Key Actions Taken   ·        Top management was activated (26 June, 6:00 PM UTC+2)  ·        After expert analysis, several operations were carried out, such as overload protection settings on DNS and a gradual decrease of traffic flow to secure the Roaming Sponsor service ·        Thanks to that, DNS was restored on 27 June, 2:00 AM (UTC+2) and the roaming sponsor service were gradually reopened between 2:30 and 2:50 AM (UTC+2)   ·        Management of traffic on roaming sponsor service enabled to restore all services gradually. The service was fully normalized on 27 June, 5:00 PM (UTC+2)   Root Cause (Preliminary)   ·         Primary Root Cause: Still under investigation as of 02 July 2025    Medium terms Action plan :   ·       Close monitoring of service and hardware stability during the whole week ·       Looking for primary root cause  ·       Avoid any DNS overload reoccurence - The solution will be deployed within 2 weeks ·       Improve RRS resiliency design - The solution will be deployed within 2 weeks ·       Improve resiliency recovery procedures in case of signaling storm - The solution will be deployed within 3 weeks ·       Full root cause analysis (RCA) to follow up and plan.    Best regards,

summary: HC: SC (Presence Verte Affected) - Voice calls failure on June 22 (CSI)
description: Voice calls failure on June 22 COFI 3343 Incident Timeline Date & Time (CET) Event Description 19 June CAB GNOC8556 executed: MSS (AWS) upgraded Jun 19, 2025, 7:00 AM TO 10:00 AM UTC 22 June, Customer Presence Verte experienced multiple SIP call rejections in Paris region around 10:00 UTC , Zenoss KPI were Degraded between – 8:25 to 12:35 UTC 23 June CAB GNOC8592 executed: Rollback of MSS upgrade due to customer impact Jun 23, 2025, 10:30 AM TO 12:00 PM UTC INCIDENT SUMMARY – On 22 June around 10:00 UTC , the customer Presence Verte experienced multiple voice call failures. SIP trace analysis showed that SIP INVITE requests were being rejected with responses such as "Unknown error" and "SIP 486 Busy Here," suggesting that the MSS was unable to route certain calls correctly. Additionally, a significant increase in SIP 6XX response codes was observed during this period, indicating that the system was globally rejecting many call attempts . The issue was isolated to the MSS instance located in the Paris data center , while the MSS in the Vitry data center continued to function normally. Although an MSS upgrade was carried out on 19 June under CAB GNOC8556, it has not been confirmed that this change directly caused the incident. To mitigate the customer impact, a rollback of the upgrade was performed on 23 June via CAB GNOC8592. The root cause of the call failures remains under investigation, with possible contributing factors including delayed activation of certain MSS components, customerspecific traffic behavior, or internal MSS routing anomalies. Further analysis is ongoing to determine the exact trigger and ensure longterm service stability. KEYNOTES – Customer : Presence Verte Location Of Impacted MSS : Paris Region only (Vitry unaffected) Symptoms : Multiple voice call failures SIP traces show INVITE messages rejected due to: Unknown Error SIP Busy Impact : Partial voice service disruption for customer Presence Verte in Paris MSS on 22 June Background CAB GNOC855

summary: Alert: vip-rtms-r2c.mobiquithings.com: CS System Failure : 1069.00 errors/5min
description: At 19:40 UTC on , GNOC received a CS System Failure alert. After further investigation, we determined that the System Failures are being generated over Sparkle. The alert cleared at 20:05 UTC but again returned at 20:20 UTC. After reviewing the PCAP, we are seeing that the cause of this anomaly was that we did not receive an ISDACK response from Sparkle, and the HLR timed out. We have referred this issue to Sparkle for investigation. comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: System Failure Towards Sparkle (GNOC-10029)   Summary: On October 29, 2025, at 19:40 UTC, GNOC received multiple P3 alerts indicating CS System Failure errors. Initial analysis revealed a spike in Sigtran and Diameter signaling starting from 17:15 UTC. The root cause was traced to the absence of ISD_ACK messages from Sparkle’s STP, resulting in timeout failures during subscriber data insertion procedures. This impacted SMS, Voice, and 2G/3G data registration for customers using SW Essential 1 (Sparkle) and Advanced devices. Despite this, overall active sessions remained stable. Sparkle later confirmed SS7 signaling congestion caused by circuit overload and provider capacity limits on their side. Timeline: Start Time: 2025-10-29 17:15 UTC End Time: 2025-10-29 21:55 UTC Duration: 4 hours 40 minutes Impacted Entities: Customers: Webb Fontaine Group FZ LLC, Nextivity, TECHNO BAM S.A.S., John F Hunt Power, SAVVY Telematic Systems AG (Diameter), Synox, Matooma, BAMS DIGITAL SAS, Networth Telecom, Ingenico France, AddSecure Group Services AB, Solustop, SAVVY Telematic Systems AG, Loomis Digital Solutions VPLMNs: Tele2 Sverige AB, Société Réunionnaise du Rad, Etisalat Benin SA (Diameter), Bouygues Telecom, Orange France, Orange La Réunion, Orange España S.A., Everything Everywhere Limited, Orange Polska S.A., Orange Caraïbe Mobiles Roaming Partner: Sparkle Country: France Traffic Types: Sigtran and Diameter Procedures Affected: CS Location Update, GPRS Location Update, S6a Cancel Location Device Types: Essential 1 and Advanced 🔍 Investigation and Findings (Detailed Analysis) Root Cause Summary: The issue originated from Sparkle’s network, where HLR did not receive acknowledgment (ISD_ACK) for the Inv_InsertSubscriberData messages sent through Sparkle STP, resulting in timeout errors and system failures during subscriber data updates. This primarily impacted signaling flows on both Sigtran and Diameter channels.     Sample-Based Technical Investigation 1️⃣  Sigtran Traffic Analysis – (Matooma IMSI: 208243093933187) Device attempted multiple Send Authentication Info (SAI) procedures simultaneously. It switched MNOs after receiving a Cancel Location (CL) from the previous network. Due to network congestion, the Update Location (UL) failed, resulting in repeated “System Failure” messages. This retry cycle led to increased signaling load, worsening congestion on the Sparkle STP. Finding: Continuous re-registration attempts under congestion created a feedback loop of signaling spikes, aggravating the overload condition.   2️⃣ Diameter Traffic Analysis – (Synox IMSI: 208243090755348) Device received a CLR, attempted to reattach to 2G/3G, but encountered a System Failure. It reverted to 4G, successfully performing AIR (Authentication Information Request) and ULR (Update Location Request). However, the session failed to complete and was stopped due to “User Request”. This pattern repeated, increasing Diameter signaling load and delaying data session establishment. Finding: Devices switching between RATs (2G/3G/4G) under signaling congestion triggered incomplete session setups and multiple AIR/ULR retries, increasing Diameter load and delaying service initiation.     3️⃣ Data Session Validation – (ChargePoint IMSI: 208243094654351) GNOC analyzed a non-impacted customer (ChargePoint) to verify the issue scope. At 17:59 UTC, the session was stopped due to “User Request” and immediately restarted successfully. No delay or service degradation observed — confirming data-plane traffic remained stable. ⚠️ Note: The “User Request” disconnections appear to be device-induced; hence, they cannot be classified as network errors. Finding: Data sessions were unaffected; failures were confined to control-plane signaling. “User Request” session terminations were likely device-triggered events.           SIM Count & Traffic Overview: Category SIM Count Major Impacted Customers Diameter 1,549 Webb Fontaine Group FZ LLC Sigtran 6,760 Matooma, Synox, Networth Telecom Sessions stopped due to “User Request”: Pre: 37,463 Post: 42,391 Increase: ~5,000 (mostly from ChargePoint) ⚠️ Note: These session stops were likely device-induced events, not service-impacting failures.   Impact Summary: System Failure volume: < 1000 occurrences — overall impact moderate and contained. High signaling congestion led to timeouts and “System Failure” responses. Essential 1 and Advanced devices  faced slightly longer reattachment delays. Affected services: Voice, SMS, Data, and USSD (2G/3G/LTE). Conclusion: The incident was caused by Sparkle’s SS7 circuit overload and capacity congestion, leading to missing ISD_ACK messages and control-plane signaling failures. While less than 1000 System Failures were recorded, the resulting signaling congestion led to minor delays in session registration. “User Request” session terminations were device-induced and not network faults. GNOC maintained active coordination with Sparkle, monitoring until full service restoration at 21:55 UTC.

summary: EXT:  Jersey Telecom (Velos) - Email  Requesting Semtech to Address High Volume traffic Authentication requests with Unknown Subscribers errors (NSI)
description: Jersey Telecom (Velos) Email Requesting Semtech to Address High Volume traffic Authentication requests with Unknown Subscribers errors No Customer Impact On 24th September at 11:13 UTC, GNOC received an email from JT/Velos mentioning they have observed 4530 Semtech IMSIs generating highvolume of authentication traffic with Unknown Subscriber errors. (As per JT they did send us an email on 17th September) . GNOC reached out to SME to address. Current Status: JT (Velos IoT Support) reported 4530 (Semtech) IMSIs generating high volume of authentication requests and receiving Unknown Subscriber Errors . Much of these errors are coming from Deactivated IMSIs. Awaiting feedback from the L3 NEO team… JT/Velos Ticket → IOT42186 Core Support has not supplied an ultimate fix to prevent these IMSIs from generating SAI traffic through JT. Consulted with Asim over the phone, today, 10/7 circa 14:30 to 15:10 and we viewed he timeline for Unknown Sub responses from our HLRs back for 23450 SAIs. Pre9/17 (time of the Frankfurt shutdown during upgrade of hardware racks– 8190 9331 on 9/17…9331 was for the actual shutdown of Telco nodes) displays our HLR regularly sending back Unknown Sub in response to SAIs coming in from JT. Screenshot of the feed based on the Transaction and Cause code are provided below these bullets. Planned start Sep 17, 2025, 1:00 AM Planned end Sep 17, 2025, 1:00 PM Thus, what caught JT’s attention was the resurge of Unknown Subscriber to pre9/17 levels, which averaged around 20k at any given hour during the day. Therefore , JT/Velos IoT needs to provide a timeline before the Frankfurt temporary shutdown on 9/17; based on the PA run again today in Viavi, the droppage in Unknown Sub in response to SAIs didn’t catch their attention, but when traffic returned to normal, what JT interpreted as elevated was actually the levels returning to what they were before 9/17 I just emailed JT/Velos IoT back, and cc’ed Mona, requesting they revisit their captures, but this ti

(As per JT they did send us an email on 17th September) . GNOC reached out to SME to address. Current Status: JT (Velos IoT Support) reported 4530 (Semtech) IMSIs generating high volume of authentication requests and receiving Unknown Subscriber Errors . Much of these errors are coming from Deactivated IMSIs. Awaiting feedback from the L3 NEO team… JT/Velos Ticket → IOT42186 Core Support has not supplied an ultimate fix to prevent these IMSIs from generating SAI traffic through JT. Consulted with Asim over the phone, today, 10/7 circa 14:30 to 15:10 and we viewed he timeline for Unknown Sub responses from our HLRs back for 23450 SAIs. Pre9/17 (time of the Frankfurt shutdown during upgrade of hardware racks– 8190 9331 on 9/17…9331 was for the actual shutdown of Telco nodes) displays our HLR regularly sending back Unknown Sub in response to SAIs coming in from JT. Screenshot of the feed based on the Transaction and Cause code are provided below these bullets. Planned start Sep 17, 2025, 1:00 AM Planned end Sep 17, 2025, 1:00 PM Thus, what caught JT’s attention was the resurge of Unknown Subscriber to pre9/17 levels, which averaged around 20k at any given hour during the day. Therefore , JT/Velos IoT needs to provide a timeline before the Frankfurt temporary shutdown on 9/17; based on the PA run again today in Viavi, the droppage in Unknown Sub in response to SAIs didn’t catch their attention, but when traffic returned to normal, what JT interpreted as elevated was actually the levels returning to what they were before 9/17 I just emailed JT/Velos IoT back, and cc’ed Mona, requesting they revisit their captures, but this ti

comments.author: Anas Tablieh
comments.body: Hello SIM with status “Deactivated“ in AV Backoffice and I don’t see the SIM exist at all on our HLR. so this traffic for terminated SIM Regards, Anas

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: Hi   , thanks for your reply. What will be the solution for this. We need to reply to JT as they are facing signaling storm issue.

comments.body: Greetings

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: There is an ongoing discussion for the topic via email. If it goes for long we need to put the ticket into problem.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Greetings !! We have informed JT about the ongoing investigation.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: I just emailed JT/Velos IoT back with the following: We just got an IT ticket created to get the email issue addressed. As far as these ICCIDs that are generating signaling excesses, can you confirm whether this is still occurring today, the 30 th ? Last week, I tried some SS7 tracing on the top user reported in the original spreadsheet, ( IMSI:   234507089768208 ;  ICCID :  89332401000008463677 ;  MSISDN :  337000064092993), but I couldn’t capture any messaging at the time.  Also, if you can provide a list, would you also happen to have a pcap of a user, (234-50), sending over an SAI through your network towards us?     Charles Austin < Charles.Austin@velosiot.com >; Support < support@velosiot.com > Cc:  Viktor Jukka < Viktor.Jukka@velosiot.com >;

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: Hi Lewie, I took the first examples and found 70% error related to unknown subscriber.  Also if you look at the history this issue has started long back however on 17th Sept error has decreased for some time 7 AM 17th Sept to 9 AM 18th Sept. It started again . And look at the per day count of the error which is clearly matching with JT reported.  From the pcap trace it is clear that our HLR is sending a unknown subscriber error and as device is repeatedly sending SAI the error count is increasing. Attaching the pcap file.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: I updated the Velos IoT ticket 42186 with the following just now. Greetings, Viktor & Charlie,  I went inside Viavi and did run a PA for SAIs transactions combined with the cause code of Unknown Subscriber, based on your linksets, for both North and West.  Going back for the past 30 days, the counts seem to routinely approach 500k.  I am not sure if this is a deficiency in how our Viavi is capturing the data for SS7 traffic.  Could you provide a screenshot of the feed of when this volume of 4500 users spiked up?  We would like something that points to the start date of this rise in Unknown Subscribers coming back from us towards your side for these 4500 users.  Also, can the individual trace be sent in a pcap format?

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: called Velos IoT ( 1 (833) 661-3220 ) and advsd them that we would like some capture of how levels of Unknown Subscriber were before 9/17 so we can get a sense of what was normal from before, and if they could send us a trace in pcap format.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings !! Update from JT,  Hello.  We are awaiting a response from the partners on this matter.  Viktor Jukka| Assurance Engineer

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Recent IT  blocking of emails from external sources had kept hidden JT-Velos IoT’s response to me for their ticket 42186 I had to go into a tool called MimeCast, which was provided to me in a departmental call today, for which before I was completely oblivious to I received this update once I had released it from the tool and permitted this sender going foward.  “Hello  lspearman@semtech.com , We have news about your ticket IOT-42186: Hello. Our partners ask why you consider any amount of SAI 'unknown subscriber' failures acceptable. They insist that if a SIM card is not in use, it should either be physically removed from the device or explicitly disabled. Could you please clarify what is behind this? Do you manage the customer service this way, for example, in case of non-payment? Partners want this behavior observed in their signalling to stop. If the situation is not resolved, they may decide to permanently deactivate the affected IMSIs via OTA. Viktor.”

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings      !!! We received below email from Velo/JT. Kindly review & suggest a way forward to respond to them.  Hello.  Our partners ask why you consider any amount of SAI 'unknown subscriber' failures acceptable.  They insist that if a SIM card is not in use, it should either be physically removed from the device or explicitly disabled. Could you please clarify what is behind this? Do you manage the customer service this way, for example, in case of non-payment?  Partners want this behavior observed in signalling to stop.   Viktor Jukka| Assurance Engineer viktor.jukka@velosiot.com Väike-Paala 1, Tallinn, Estonia

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings      !!! JT is following up with response to our last email, that we are checking .. Please help checking this & help replying them accordingly.    GNOC has not responded to them yet.

comments.author: Steven Lee
comments.body: 2025-10-21 10:41 UTC - Velos/JT is following up asking why we would consider any amount of SAI “unknown subscriber” failures acceptable.         Please help with how GNOC should respond to this question.

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: We are discussing with Minh and Fred on what should be the reply from us.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !! GNOC has sent response to JT . Hello Team, Greetings !!!  Thank you for your patience.   After detailed & thorough internal discussion with our engineering team & management we are working on a plan to terminate the sims permanently.  We will provide more updates on the execution in as soon as possible. In the meanwhile we request to kindly close the ticket.    Thank You,

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings !! With GNOC’s request, JT/Velos has closed their ticket.

summary: SIM v4.4.1 Behaviour Issue During Telia Sweden Outage
description: ssue Summary: During the Telia Sweden network outage on September 22, 2025 , devices using SIM v4.4.1 and connected via Sparkle failed to switch IMSI profiles or MVNOs. Approximately 2,400 Sparkleconnected devices were affected and remained without service until Telia was restored. In contrast, BICSconnected devices with the same SIM version successfully switched to alternative networks. Devices with SIM v5.x handled the situation correctly, switching IMSI and MVNO as needed. Customer Impact: The most affected customer was Eskilstuna Strängnäs Energi & Miljö (ESEM) . Out of 9,366 Advanced2 SIM v4.4.1 devices , around 25% experienced prolonged outages . Technical Investigation: ICCID 8933240100001435914 was identified as ESSENTIALS1 , despite AV showing it as ADVANCED2 . The label “SmartSIM to Easy 210101” suggests it was updated via OTA . ESSENTIALS1 uses Sparkle IMSI only , which may explain the lack of IMSI switching during the outage. Followup: Asim provided additional ICCIDs for verification: 8933240100001890344 8933240100001435159 8933240100001820472 Fred confirmed that all are ESSENTIALS1 , likely migrated from ADVANCED2 via OTA.

summary: INCIDENT: Orange Sigtran Traffic Block Incident (CSI)
description: GNOC RCA: GNOC8513 : 06132025 High Signaling Congestion Towards Orange Executive Summary: On June 13, 2025, Semtech identified significant congestion on Orange STP links, resulting in increased system failures towards Orange and disruption in device connectivity. This impacted customers using Essential5 SIMs, who may have experienced delays, degraded service quality, or complete failures in establishing Data, SMS, USSD, and Voice services. While services have since been restored, the root cause analysis from Orange is still pending. Summary: Since June 13, 2025, at 09:05 UTC, GNOC began receiving multiple P2 and P3 alerts indicating PS/CS system failure errors. Upon investigation in OpenSearch, it was observed that there was a significant increase in system failures towards Orange. A deeper analysis using Zenoss revealed that there was no outbound STP traffic being sent towards Orange. In response, GNOC issued an initial status update and escalated the issue to the Subject Matter Expert (SME) via the SW channel, while also engaging with the roaming partner, Orange (901), to seek clarification and resolution. During a parallel analysis with VIP customer Invers, GNOC observed RNA on the Diameter traffic around the same time, which has since recovered. According to the SME, Orange was sending SCCPMGprohibited traffic, which caused a blockage of outbound traffic towards their network. This blockage led to congestion on the STP links and a rise in system failures. To mitigate the issue, the SME performed a restart of both Orange STP links. Current Status: Services were fully restored by 10:40 UTC on the same day, June 13, 2025. Root Cause & resolution Shared by Orange: The root cause of the incident has been identified: a VPLMN transmitted an "SCCPMG" message via the Roaming Sponsor offer to the HPLMN (Sierra). The Sierra network processed the message by blocking signaling from the point code of the last equipment, namely Orange's STP. This resulted in blocking all SS7 s

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Greeting, We are still waiting for the feedback from Orange:

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2025-06-13 14:35 UTC, I called into Orange CSC (Customer Service Centre) at the 011 33 8 20 89 02 87 and I spoke with Florentin.  I requested case ticket# S506896133 to be raised to a P1.  Florentin was imformed that the carrier response is not reflective of the true case severity level.  The following was requested from the CSC:  Raised Escalation request Root Cause Resolution / Corrective Action An update Escalation List w/ contact numbers  (since Bernard Chaulet is retiring today, and he is being replaced with Nicholas Martin). I explained to him that Semtech is in a vulnerable state, and that we already had to 2 instances of SCCPMG restricted messages that caused a total outage in connectivity services for Semtech customers.  I let him know that we need to ensure that we do not have a recurrence (outage). GNOC will continue to press Orange to ensure carrier network stability and an official RFO report.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: I called +33820890287,    And spoke to Pascal at their frontline support; he did understand that we required an RCA but he wasn't able to provide answers, and he claimed he was working remotely and could not reach anyone internally when he put me on hold to do so.     I got off the line with him and called   +33 6 80 20 15 41 for Olivier Souzy   I left a vm with the ticket number, description of the issue and my direct line in MS Teams. I am trying the number now for  L3 Escalation - Joelle Guimet Director Service Client +33 6 08 15 39 94

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: I reached Joelle on the phone, and she requested I send her an email with the ticket number.  I explained that this is critical issue for which we need an RCA before we enter into the weekend sans explanation.  I will be emailing her forthwith.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: just sent the following to  joelle.guiment@orange.com Joelle,     Thanks for answering my call earlier.  As I stated over the phone, we had a severe outage of signaling connection with your linksets today; these were at 9:05 to 10:05 UTC and between 10:20 to 10:40 UTC, roughly.  You have created ticket number  S506896133.     During this time, we were receiving a specific and unusual reject code (SCCPMG) from your network, (point codes 5715 & 5716), stating Subsystem Prohibited, however, the SSN is value 6, which should be normal, since it is indicative of messaging coming from our HLR.     We urgently need an explanation as to why your network was sending us this reject earlier today, resulting in signaling loss with multiple VPLMNs.  Could you state whether this reject code was unique in your messaging back to us or whether you were sending this to other MVNOs?        Thanks for providing a detailed RCA before we enter into the weekend and for treating this with the utmost urgency.  I am including a trace that shows the reject, and a screenshot from the trace is included below.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: I emailed the following to joelle just now @  joelle.guimet@orange.com “Joelle,  I did get a call back from Matthieu earlier about this ticket. I explained to him over the phone that this was not isolated to one specific VPLMN or one particular country; in fact, the initial SCTP rejects led to  a complete shutdown of our connection  with your network earlier today.  As you can see in the two gaps in the screenshot below.  The first one started at around 9:00 UTC and then flared up again at around 10:00 UTC for a shorter period.  Could you inform us of what your engineers are looking into as far as explaining this sudden reject of the Subsystem Number value of 6?   We want to make sure the ticket shows there that the support team fully understands this outage was not for just a few customers on one particular local carrier in just one country.   Earlier in the ticket, there appears to be some confusion as to the magnitude of the problem.”

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: The Orange ticket was just updated from their side from Matthieu in response to what I had reiterated in the ticket -  France is 7 hours head of CST. INFO        Contact client entrant        06/13/2025 17:51        --        202506131551=Matthieu, as we discussed over the phone. This outage that occurred today was not only happening on users located in Germany or the U.S. but was impacting our customers on multiple VPLMNs in various countries. That trace from earlier was just one example, but the reject was occurring for all our signaling connectivity with your network during the following timeframes. 9:05 to 10:05 UTC and 10:20 to 10:40 UTC, roughly. We need a detailed RCA as to what caused this reject before we enter into the weekend.   INFO        Commentaires        06/13/2025 18:26        --        Hello, We're still working on it. An internal escalation has just been carried out. I don't have any information to give you at the moment, as the root cause has not yet been defined. I need to get back to the international teams to find out what happened. Regards, Matthieu

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: I just received the following at Fri 6/13/2025 11:53 AM/16:53 UTC from Matthieu Labarussias at Orange “Hello Lewie,  To reassure you, the problem is well understood on our side, and we will do everything we can to ensure that the commitments you have made to our customers are kept.  The incident is in the hands of our ROAMING and Carrier teams, who have enough information to investigate and give us a rootcause as soon as possible.  However, it will be difficult to provide you with an RFO before or during the weekend.  The interruption of service is no longer an issue, so we can at least be reassured in that respect.  In my experience, we'll be able to give you concrete information from Monday onwards.  I remain at your disposal, should you have any further information to share with us.” matthieu.labarussias@orange.com  Fixe :  +33 1 55 89 84 56  Mobile :  +33 6 89 02 55 94

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: at Fri 6/13/2025 1:47 PM/18:47 UTC, Matthieu responded with the following:  Our teams have just investigated the trace you sent us by e-mail (probably from your user in Germany) The error comes from the VPLMN network.   The error sccp.handling == 0x08 in Wireshark corresponds to the Subsystem Prohibited (SSP) indication in the Signaling Connection Control Part (SCCP) protocol. This means that the specified subsystem (SSN) is prohibited or inactive on the destination signaling point Our teams can't investigate the problem any further. I think it would be interesting if you could provide us with a list of the countries affected by the error, as well as a pcap/horodate trace.   In any case, investigations can resume on Monday, and we'll be able to call on the VPLMN for further information.   Our Roaming and Carrier teams have no knowledge of any incident impacting several VPLMNs to date;   We look forward to hearing from you.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: a meeting invite has been sent out to Orange, including Joelle, our escalation contact, and their engineering team, including Amine from our side to discuss why there were sending over the particular reject on friday morning.  start time of inter-company call is slated for 9 am CST/14:00 UTC.   From Friday afternoon until now, Orange is fixated on the one IMSI located in Germany; however, the issue was a much larger magnitude than just one vplmn and one country.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: SOUZY Olivier OW/OWF/DSO & LODOLA Benoit OW/OWF/DSO (Unverified) was invited to the meeting.   from Orange joined the call, and RAchid, Adnen and Amine from Semtech were on, along with Robert James; they did see the trace provided from Amine back on Friday, showing the reject from Orange in response to subsystem number 6 (hlr) ; Rachid pointed out this is very rare and has hardly come across this before from Roaming Sponsors.  Souzy Olivier did not have the teams on the call that could comment and explain as to why they were sending this over.  The trace we had shown them on Friday was uploaded to the group chat; we reiterated that once we started receiving that reject on Friday, we ceased communicating back through our STP links with them; they did comprehend the need for an accurate RCA, since this resulted in a complete loss of signaling for our customers using them as the roaming sponsor during the previously noted timeframes:      1st instance -    9:05 UTC and  second instance  - 10:05 UTC 2nd instance  - 10:20 UTC and  second instance  - 10:40 UTC We scheduled a follow up call tomorrow to see what their teams will be able to capture retroactively for the incident on 6/13.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: GNOC has left a note on portal to provide RCA/RFO for the issue.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Update from Orange . “Hello, We haven't heard back from the root cause yet. Our teams have escalated to their expert for feedback. We're trying to get in touch with one of his experts at our 4pm meeting (Paris time). Sincerely, Benoît,”

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: WE joined the 9:30 am CST call today, with Robert James, Amine, Adnen, Rachid, but no one from Orange appeared …a minute after the start time of the call, we received an email from olivier souzy, stating the following.  “Unfortunately, we don't have any feedback from our Signaling experts at the moment. I'll let you know as soon as we hear back from them.” I updated the Orange ticket  S506896133  with the following just now.  Benoit, we received an email, advising us you didn't have any new insights or findings from your internal teams, a minute after the inter-company call had started up today at 14:30 UTC.  We were under the impression from the 16th, heading into the follow up call today, the 17th, that either you would have an RCA for the Friday 13th issue, or you would at least have your engineers involved in signaling on the call with our HLR/STP SMEs.  We require some type of evidence you are working to provide us a sound and logical root cause explanation to what triggered the SCCPMG messages having issues with SSN value 6.  Shortly after it was obvious that no one from Orange would be joining the call, Robert James send the following back to Orange and cc’ed Joelle Guimet This is very disappointing.  We were clear on the call yesterday that we need answers from the responsible experts.  Although the incident is not ongoing, due its impact we need to get an understanding of what actually caused the problem…  What is the ETR for your experts analysis?

comments.author: yjadhav
comments.body: Greetings! Update from Orange , Their team is working and investigating , they assured for presence of an expert for the next point

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Update from Orange --> Hello Robert James,  Our teams took a trace but there is no message history in our probes, nor in our equipment. We have several hundred customers on our STPs and we have not observed any incident on our TK2 node. The trace you have attached shows that SSN=6 corresponds to an HLR and cannot be sent by an STP . Regards, Matthieu      FYIP.  Could you please suggest a way forward.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Update from Orange, Hello,  We are following up on your inquiry regarding the SS7 signaling incident that occurred on June 13th.  Upon analysis, we confirm there was no interruption on the Roaming Sponsor platform, no issues with the STP router of our TK2 node, which serves several hundred partners, nor any technical operations on that date.  The SS7 signaling was correctly transmitted through the Orange network (identical at input and output), consequently, no alarming occurred.  The root cause of the incident has been identified: a VPLMN transmitted an "SCCPMG" message via the Roaming Sponsor offer to the HPLMN (Sierra). The Sierra network processed the message by blocking signaling from the point code of the last equipment, namely Orange's STP. This resulted in blocking all SS7 signaling. Unfortunately, the available traces do not allow us to identify the VPLMN that originated this message (our probe are unable to capture data at the lower protocol layer).  To prevent any recurrence of this issue, our teams have implemented a script that can be manually activated to unblock SS7 signaling if necessary.  We confirm that it is not possible for our teams to modify the "STP management" parameters.  Could you please provide us with your STP reference ?  Please do not hesitate to contact us if you have any further questions or need additional clarification.  Best regards,  Sébastien Brunie  Pilote de Services Mobile Automatisation  Orange/WHOLESALE/OWF/DSO/DPO MPI

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !! Orange has closed the ticket.

comments.author: Rachid Benjira
comments.body: An improvement has been deployed on the STP    FYI,

summary: INCIDENT: Sparkle ORS Network Congestion Incident (CSI)
description: GNOC RCA: GNOC8478 : 06112025 Sparkle ORS Network Congestion Incident HighLevel Summary: Sparkle experienced network congestion in their ORS platform. Some Essentials1 subscribers experienced service establishment delays and/or data connectivity failure. Some Advanced subscribers switched from Sparkle to alternative roaming sponsors. Duration was 4 hours and 59 minutes. Summary: On 11th June 2025 from 06:57 UTC, GNOC received multiple P2,P3 alerts for PS/CS system failure . GNOC did investigate in OpenSearch & observed that there is increase in System Failures. GNOC reached out to Sparkle for the root cause and learnt that it is because of a maintenance of one of its ORS customer Roberta. The maintenance of Roberta (Customer of Sparkle) did not go as planned & congested the ORS platform of Sparkle, which in turn impacted/degraded Semtech Essentials1 services with Session time out error & system failure. Investigation Summary: Our HLR is not getting acknowledgment of the InvInsertSubscriberData message from Sparkle and after 14 seconds it has been sending HLR time out with a System failure error . However GNOC has received a mail from sparkle regarding they are also experiencing idle Time out issue with SS7 & Diameter traffic. Roberta, one of the Sparkle’s customer had a planned maintenance, which did not go as planned. In turn it congested Sparkle’s ORS platform impacting Semtech’s services because of congestion. Current Status → System failure KPI is back to normal. GNOC continues to monitor. Ticket details : INC000000414544 | TIS25SF0021 (Sparke Created) Ticket Number: CSFA00637577 (Sparke Created on GNOC’s request) SF ticket → 01868188 (BAMS Digital) 01868247 (ChargePoint) CSFA00637681 → Sparkle ticket for RCA Keynotes as Follows: Customer : Chargepoint, Nextivity, AddSecure Group Services AB, Inpro/Seal Company, Ingenico France, Wilson Electronics, Synox, Allcom Telecom Brazil Reseller, Eskilstuna Strängnäs Energi & Miljö (ESEM), NX BUDDY, Toyota Material Handli

summary: Verizon APN num.vzwentp VPN Migration
description: Moving the Verizon connections over to new routers / new VPN tunnels to prep for the core router upgrades starting later in November. The current connection is on end of life hardware and is required to move to new device. No impact is expected; In the event of problems this would cause issues for all customers using the Verizon ECC num.vzwentp APN only. Other private custom Verizon APNs would be unaffected. GNOC / Teams should monitor data insights Verizon for that specific APN to look for any traffic deviations during the maintenance. We have worked with one of the large customers (Toyota) using this APN and this is the date they would like the migration to happen; if any issues arise with their devices we will move traffic back to old tunnels.

summary: EXT:  Verizon Data Service and Connectivity Outage - Broadband Access Customers (CSI)
description: EXT: Verizon Data Service and Connectivity Outage Broadband Access Customers (CSI) We are investigating reports received by customers of service data degradation on the Verizon network. We are in the process of engaging the partner to determine the scope and cause of the issue. Impact: Some Broadband Access and ECC customers may have experienced failures or degradation in network connectivity, Data services, iMaestro platform access. Smart Connectivity services were unaffected. (edited) Broadband Access devices impacted: 22% Carrier+/ECC devices impacted: 2% RCA Received on 06/25/2025: Final Update: Verizon Services Restored. GNOC and L2 Technical Support Leadership teams worked with Verizon Technical Support management to expedite case investigation# WB250530952476 (WFT1459163). The official RFO report was received on 20250625 20:05 UTC. During Incident VZ Business department was closed, making a technical resource unavailable. comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2025-05-30 00:20 UTC I was on a call with Verizon, which was fruitless.  The Verizon Business office was closed with no on-call engineering resource options.  Joshua and I were conferenced with a man named Diego from the Verizon NOC, and he was unable to assist due to limit access.  Diego made several attempts to find a technical resource from the Verizon Business department.  Thus, the L2 Technical Support team will follow up with VZ in the morning during local business hours.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: Incident summary for the ( GNOC-8322) 05292025 Verizon Data Connectivity Service Outage. Status: Restored No Alerts   Customers reported Verizon service failures in Data and network connectivity.  The Verizon outage lasted 47 minutes.  L2 Technical Support and GNOC teams conferenced in Verizon Support and were informed that the VZ Business department was closed, making a technical resource unavailable.  The L2 Technical Support will follow up with the partner first thing in the morning for an RFO report during normal local business hours.   Impact:  Some Carrier+/ECC and Broadband Access customers may have experienced failures or degradation in iMaestro platform access, Data services and network connectivity. There were at least 110+ Broadband Access circuits (devices) affected during the peak of the incident. Smart Connectivity services were unaffected.   Start Time:           2025-05-29 22:26 UTC End Time:            2025-05-29 23:14 UTC Duration:             47 minutes Detected:  2025-05-29 23:07 UTC – (L1 team notified GNOC with customer report) Initial Statuspage sent at 23:47 UTC   Customers Affected: ECC -  Sentinel Broadband Access  - Concentra, Vonage Business

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: I spoke with Isis at Verizon Technical Support creating ticket # WB250530952476 (WFT1459163) to start the carrier investigation.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: As an escalation, Leadership from GNOC and L2 Technical Support teams are working with Verizon Technical Support management to expedite case investigation# WB250530952476 (WFT1459163).  A preliminary incident report with root cause and resolution, and ultimately an official RFO report is being requested.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2025-06-25 20:05 UTC Verizon’s Distinguished Solutions Architect, Jordan Harvey, delivered the official Verizon RCA NRB000011848574 report to Semtech Leadership via email.  See below.

summary: HC: VPLMN Proximus -Data Service lost for Multiple customers (CSI)
description: Starting from 18th May, 08:50 hrs UTC, GNOC is observingan increase in error Lost Service and Radius traffic for multiple customers Solustop and NEAT Electronics (mostly) via VPLMN Proximus Belgium through Roaming partners JT and Orange901 , more information as follows. No customer tickets have been received. Current Status: GNOC is reaching out to Roaming partners JT and Orange901 for the cause and fix, however, the KPIs have returned to normal levels. GNOC completed the ticket. Service impact for Essential Devices old session drop due to lost services for VPLMN (Proximus) then devices switched VPLMN and registered with new VPLMN (Orange Belgium and Telenet group ) and started the session , affected Essential devices were Essential3 devices belong to Customer Neat Electronics using MVNO Jersey and Essential5 devices of Invers through MVNOOrange901 Approx affected device count for Essential devices 2300 Essential3 and 130 for Essential5 Essential Device Behavior ICCID8933240100001203788 showed devices switched from Proximus to Orange Belgium due to lost service. For Advanced devices , The Advanced devices after Dropping the Session from VPLMN Proximus due to lost service trying to reregister to the same VPLMN by changing the RAT and the devices got RNA , the sample iccid 8933240100002833761 showing the same behavior, existing session was dropped and new session faced delay in registration and starting the data session. Approx device count for Advanced devices 350 Velos IoT ticket : IOT38848 Orange Ticket: S505855632 Keynotes as Follows: Customer : NEAT Electronics, SoluStop , Invers GmbH Traffic Procedure: START/STOP Traffic type: RADIUS, VSSF, SIGTRAN Roaming Partner: JERSEY and Orange901 Device Type: ESSENTIALS3, ADVANCED4, ADVANCED2 VPLMN: Proximus PLC (MCC, MNC 206 01) Country: Belgium Start Time: 18th May 2025 08:50 UTC End Time: 18th May 2025 14:40 UTC Duration: 5 Hours, 50 Minutes Sample ICCID: 8933240100002833761 , 8933240100001203788 OpensearchLink

summary: HC: Sparkle -MAP Traffic | TIS-25-PW0069 | System Failure during Maintenance (CSI)
description: GNOC RCA: GNOC8058: 05082025 EXT : Sparkle MAP Traffic | TIS25PW0069 | Services disruption Greetings!! GNOC is opening this ticket to have an analysis and findings from the ongoing SPARKLE’s planned Maintenance ( GNOC7887) on its ORS GEOREDUNDANT PLATFORM DUAL IMSI SERVICE located in MILAN and CATANIA, which affects Diameter, Global Signaling only degradation, and all GTP sessions will be dropped during the server restart. Sparkle has announced that the maintenance activity has been extended till 6:00 hrs UTC due to an unexpected instability problem for MAP traffic. Since Sparkle experienced stability issues during maintenance, they rolled back the configuration changes at 08:56 hrs UTC to avoid more service disruptions. Final Status: At 07:50 hrs UTC, all services found stabilized and returned to normal levels after rollback of configuration changes at SPARKLE end. Sparkle Maintenance Ticket Number: INC000000399009 | TIS25PW0069 RCA received from Sparkle: Type of Fault: Degradation Fault Localization: Tis Network/Ors Platform/Configuration (Catania) Fault root cause: The scheduled activity TIS25PW0069 (Service Configuration DB switch and service restart) caused an unexpected instability problem for MAP traffic. The activity failed due to unexpected traffic congestion generated by a SS7 timeout at the end of the switch procedure planned. Remedy action: Roll back procedure on Milan DB as active due to traffic handling problem Background of the Incident: Sparkle scheduled a maintenance on their ORS GEOREDUNDANT PLATFORM DUAL IMSI SERVICE in location MILAN AND CATANIA to be done on 6th May 01:00 local time to 06:00 local time. Because of Fiber cut Fault on Italy National Backbone (between Milan and Catania) the planned maintenance was rescheduled twice. First the activity was rescheduled to 7th May 01:00 local time – 06:00 local time. Finally, after 2nd reschedule the activity started on 8th May 01:00 local time . However, maintenance end time could not be completed as

summary: HC: Salt Mobile SA MNO Incident affecting Smart Connectivity in Switzerland (CSI)
description: While performing HC, GNOC observed an increase in UL AvG Errors for Invers. After reviewing the KPIs, we see devices in Switzerland receiving RNA errors from Sunrise Communications over Orange901. We reviewed one IMSI, and starting at 1430 UTC, the traffic switched from Salt Mobile to Sunrise Communications and received RNA. After further investigation of Salt Mobile, the graphs shows disruption of GTP traffic on SALT Mobile. Devices attempted to register on to Sunrise Communications, however Invers is blocked from registering on Sunrise Switzerland. GNOC has reached out to Orange and requested that they refer this issue to Salt Mobile SA. Current Status: GNOC has referred the issue to Orange901 to contact Salt Mobile to resolve the issue. K ey Notes: Start Time: 29/04/2025 14:30 UTC End Time: 29/04/2025 15:30 UTC Duration: 60 minutes Customer: Invers, Weezevent, exc… Traffic type: GTP Roaming Partner: Orange901 VPLMN: Salt Mobile SA Country: Switzerland Device Type: Essentials 5 ICCID 8933240100002253394 Orange Ticket: S504825789

summary: Airlink Page Not Accessible for Customers
description: GNOC received P1 alert at 15:14 UTC for (P1) AMM Site amm89; but other customer sites soon followed over the course of next hour. Airlink Mobility Domain wasn't accessible externally. Registration Renewal for the domain was not routing properly as of this morning, shortly before 10:15 am CST/15:15 UTC. An erroneous DNS server change for the airlink.com domain name had been processed this morning to forward airlink.com to https://www.sierrawireless.com/routesolutions/airlink The change was reversed at around 17:30 UTC. Shortly after 17:30 UTC, internal engineers could start accessing the domain without problems. the AMM internal checkpoint sites were alarming at different times. Customers were experiencing 404 page not found.

Shortly after 17:30 UTC, internal engineers could start accessing the domain without problems. the AMM internal checkpoint sites were alarming at different times. Customers were experiencing 404 page not found.

comments.body: since P1s are still coming in , I am calling out to Suyash via MS teams, ; I have just pinged him and VArdi via slack channel for production issues. left a vm on suyash’s vm box, describing the alerts and the ticket number, and left my ms team phone number.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Called Vardi and he advised he is looking into it now.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Did speak with Vardi again on conference call; we are encountering DNS resolution issues across various AMM sites.. wil be sending out a status page shortly.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Status Page 1st one was sent out at  16:08 UTC; Vardi advsied me & Robert over the phone that although he was not the assigned on-call he was logging into the appropriate programs and reaching out to the necessary individuals and groups to get dns resolution issue resolved.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: There is an ongoing slack channel entitled     It was discovered there was a lack of proper renewal for the airlink.com domain today, causing external queries to fail at 11:14 CST16:14 Ageu Santiago had cut helpdesk ticket helpdesk ticket #283071 Changes are getting rolled back so that external queries will succeed for this domain.  At 12:26, Ageu estimated it will take around an hour to fully propagate at NS domain records to query the previous address.  with current queries (during the slack channel conversation) resolving to  godaddy dot  com , this points to gap of registration renewal.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: at 12:31 CST/17:31 UTC, Chris Rose advised that hosted amm servers are able to get resolution again when he queries.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Status page was updated at 17:58, changed to monitoring status.  the IT engineers did reverse the DNS change request from earlier this morning.  full updates across all DNS queries may take an addiitional 30 minutes or so for all originating query locations.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: and in uptrends, the amm sites are no longer alarming and no longer show DNS lookup errors

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Website is accessible now.  Final status page was sent out shortly after 19:00 UTC.

summary: Telia Sweden Data Outage
description: GNOC RCA: GNOC7683: 03242025 Telia Sweden Data Outage Final RCA Executive Summary Version 2.0 Data Outage occurred on Telia Sweden on 3/24/2025, causing Essentials 1 customers to seek services on other Swedish carriers, such as Tele2; unfortunately, Tele2 is not allowed for customers such as Add Secure Group. Sparkle pursued VPLMN, Telia overnight from 3/24 into 3/25. Sparkle delivered RCA on May 12th 2025, without much details from local carrier (Telia Sweden). Version 1.0 Sparkle RP experienced default bearing not being established after our PGW receiving GTP. Customer may not be able to establish new data session. Existing data session should not be impacted. Total Outage Time 11 hours 31 minutes Start Time 20250324 19:49 UTC End Time 20250325 07:20 UTC Sparkle Ticket: CSFA00628612 Customer Complaints Zesec of Sweden AB Salesforce → 01855109 COFI ticket – COFI3089 First StatusPage Initial StatusPage (Investigating) 20250324 20:25 UTC Second StatusPage (Monitoring) 20250324 21:33 UTC Third StatusPage (Resolved) 20250324 22:48 UTC Second StatusPage Initial StatusPage (Investigating) 20250325 00:46 UTC Second StatusPage (Investigating) 20250325 02:47 UTC Third StatusPage (Investigating) 20250325 05:04 UTC Fourth StatusPage (Investigating) 20250325 07:10 UTC Fifth StatusPage (Monitoring) 20250325 08:34 UTC Sixth StatusPage (Resolved) 20250325 10:42 UTC Detailed Summary At 19:50 UTC, GNOC received several P3 alerts for Sparkle Link sets speed to high. After investigating the KPIs, we see customer AddSecure Group Services receiving RNA for users attempting to register on Spring Mobile Sweden or Tele2 Sverige Sweden. Calls are completed over Tele Sverige. L3 investigated this issue and they looked on the GTP side they saw a lot of sessions restarted around the time of the spike, and our PGWs accepted them all. SME also informed that the GTP are being received on the PGW side. However, there is no default bearer being established. Hence, no sessions were being created. S

comments.author: Steven Lee
comments.body: Since 2025-03-24 19:50 UTC, GNOC received alert. Upon initial investigation, GNOC saw a RNA increase and contacted Sparkle for investigation. Sparkle informed that RNA is coming from our side, hence, GNOC contacted on-call SME to have them investigate the matter. SME investigated and informed that the GTP are being received on the PGW side. However, there is no default bearer being established. Hence, no sessions were being created. GNOC passed this information over to Sparkle to have them investigate. After constantly following up with Sparkle, they contacted their RP to resolve the matter. At 2025-03-25 07:20 UTC, GNOC started to observed a significant improvement.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: I just sent the following to Sparkle from GNOC inbox…Marco did state that so far Telia has not been forthcoming with an RCA for the issue yesterday into the overnight hours from 3/24 into 3/25. “Vincenzo,  Thanks for the confirmation of the overnight data outage occurring on Telia Sweden and for pushing them towards resolving the issue.  In an effort to achieve full closure of the data outage, have they possibly volunteered an underlying explanation as to what caused the lack of successful GTP traffic?  Marco did state earlier today on our conference call that so far, they have gone into a more explicit explanation of what went awry.  Thanks for sharing any possible details they might divulged.”
