comments.body: Time Line (UTC)   2025-03-24  19:50: GNOC received several P3 alerts concerning Sparkle Link sets speed to high. 20:06: GNOC started the investigation 20:25: GNOC sent Status Page 20:33: GNOC opened ticket with Sparkle 20:43: Sparkle responded that we observed a spike in Diameter and SIGTRAN Traffic from Semtech. 20:51: GNOC started to reinvestigate the issue on the Semtech side. 21:26: GNOC observed this could be an RNA issue 23:02: SME started to investigate 23:34: SME determined a data problem for customers in Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and Finland. 23:34: SME They observed were unable to set up a default bearer the attach is rejected and it will continue to try to attach.   2025-03-25 00:30: GNOC referred the issue back to Sparkle to investigate. 01:11: Sparkle stated they had referred the issue to their RP. 03:00: GNOC escalated issue with Sparkle 03:15: Sparkle escalated issue with RP 06:31: Sparkle determined the issue to be with Telia Norway and Telia Sweden 07:20: GNOC was seeing some improvement 09:20: Levels were back to normal

summary: NAM LTE Data Connectivity Incident
description: LTE Data Connectivity Disturbance in North America No Customer Reported Tickets Our monitoring tools showed network disturbances in North America that affected Semtech Smart Connectivity LTE Data services and network connectivity. The partner was engaged to nvestigate to determine cause and resolution of the issue. Impacts: For brief periods, subscribed customers may have experienced delays and failures in LTE Data services and network connectivity. 6K LTE NAM Data Sessions dropped at 16:52 UTC and recovered at 16:58 UTC (4 min. recovery) 6K LTE NAM Data Sessions dropped at 20:09 UTC and recovered at 20:11 UTC (2 min. recovery) At 20250320 16:53 UTC GNOC observed drops in NAM Geo LTE Data sessions. At the time Zenoss had an ongoing Zenoss maintenance, thus KPIs required validation. GNOC is reaching out to the PGW SMEs to validate and engaged the Sparkle Contact Center. At the time there was an ongoing Zenoss maintenance. GNOC has also been inundated with unrelated PGW alerts and there were a few PGW maintenance activities that occurred earlier in the day. Current Status: LTE NAM Data Services Restored. Awaiting Partner Carrier Feedback. comments.body: At 2025-03-22 19:51 UTC, following an email sent to the provider, GNOC called and spoke with Jeremy at the Syniverse Command Center to inquiry about any potential LTE network connectivity disturbances on their North American network during the Thursday. 03/20/2025, incident timeframe.  Jeremy stated that he will forward the inquiry request to the Connect Team to review.  Since the issue is no longer ongoing, Syniverse may not respond until next week during normal business hours. See email correspondence below. +globalscc-engage@syniverse.com   From:  Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com >  Sent:  Saturday, March 22, 2025 5:51 PM To:  Syniverse Command Center < scc@syniverse.com > Cc:  Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com > Subject:  Syniverse | Semtech Inquiry into 03/20/2025 LTE Network Connectivity Disturbances | GNOC-7654   Greetings Syniverse team,   This is a post-incident inquiry.  On Thursday, 03/20/2025, we observed several KPI anomalies for multiple MNOs in North America.  There were at least 3 separate network spikes in LTE traffic.  Were there any nationwide disturbances in the US or Canadian regions during the times specified below?  If so, were there any notifications sent? 1st network event:  2025-03-20 16:52 - 17:07 UTC 2nd network event : 2025-03-20 17:13 - 17:26 UTC 3rd network event:  2025-03-20 20:08 - 20:29 UTC Total Duration:  50 minutes   MNOs included: AT&T, T-Mobile USA, Verizon, and Bell Mobility   Our GNOC is referencing internal ticket# GNOC-7654 for tracking.  Thank you for your assistance.     Best Regards,   Global Network Operations Center (GNOC) Email:   GlobalNOC@semtech.com SEMTECH / SIERRA WIRELESS Toll Free  +1 (844) 367 7939  ::  13811 Wireless Way, Richmond, BC, Canada V6V 3A4

comments.body: REached out to Kevin and Vladimir via Slack channel for production issues: nLewie Spearman   1:38 PM Looking back through the Kibana charts, we there seemed to be some major spikes for traffic LTE issues occurring last Thursday (JSM ticket 7654) at three intervals via Orange and Sparkle for traffic on Lithia Springs cluster 192.119.177.163; the spikes were mostly concentrated, radius wise, on carriers such as T-Mobile, AT&T mobility and Rogers Wireless, while the traffic would stabilize within 8 to 15 minute timeframes on 3/20.  i did look through an older ticket from last September (6207), but the trigger for that issue was a known fiber cut with Syniverse.  could either of you comment on any major reshuffling of traffic between LS and Madison last Thursday?   Radius spikes on 3 20 for 192.119.177.163   The interval from the screenshots below are for the 1st one shortly after16:40 UTC on 3/20

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !!!  Update from Sparkle . Dear Team, The logs we have shared in our previous e-mail are dating back to March 20th, to the day and timestamps you have complained about. As we have already reported, no issues have been found in our platform and we haven’t received other complaints. What we could observe is a list of “DIAMETER ERROR - Unknown EPS Subscription” coming from your nodes; as we haven’t received any feedback regarding this aspect, we are closing this ticket. Remember that for any other problem, you can open another ticket. Thank you for your cooperation.

summary: AV Database Issue Impacting AV Components for AV NA and ALMS
description: GNOC RCA: GNOC7642: 03192025 NA AirVantage/ALMS Platform Incident Final RCA Summary: AirVantage SME reported there was an issue with the AirVantage NA platform. SMEs investigated a possible database query that locked up a table and blocked communication with the database. Customers could not manage their devices in the AV, ALMS, and iMaestro Platforms. There was no impact on actual devices on the network. The SMEs restarted avcore (avbackend) and eventually fixed the issue but triggered a backup in device messages. Once the backlog was drained the devices started to report in real time. A Second event took place. A restart of the avcore (avbackend) was performed and additional capacity was added. After the backlog queue cleared, the devices reported in realtime. Current Status : As per SME Jacques AirVantage platform remains stable since ~22:30PM UTC 19th March. GNOC has sent out resolved status page . At 13:47 UTC, the SMEs notified GNOC in Slack that there were communication issues with AV NA and ALMS. No alerts were received. GNOC then sent a status page. GNOC opened a conference bridge, and the Airvange SME stated that the issue was possibly a database query that locked up a table and blocked communication with the database. Customers could not manage their devices in the AV, ALMS, and IMaestro Platforms. There was no impact on actual devices on the network. The SMEs restarted avcore (avbackend) and eventually fixed the issue but caused a backup in device messages. Once the backlog was drained the devices started to report in real time. AV team to open a Confluence Post Mortem ticket. At 18:53 UTC, GNOC received another Slack message that again there were issues with the ALMS gateways in NA. GNOC reconvened the conference bridge. The AV SMEs observed the same process action that caused the 1st event. Again the avcore (avbackend) to restore service. The SMEs have determined that customer Duke Energy is sending an operation that is causing the AV table to lock up

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: Times are in UTC 13:47:  GNOC received a Slack message from the ALMS team that gateways for NA were not working 13:50:  GNOC opened ticket GNOC-7642 13:47:  GNOC received a Slack message from SME stating there was a communication issue with AV Platform in NA. 14:06:  Initial Status Page sent 14:36:   GNOC established Conference bridge. 14:45:  SME reported Customers could not manage their devices in the AV, ALMS, and IMaestro Platforms, but there was no impact on actual devices on the network. 14:57:  SME stated the cause may have been a possible database query that locked up a table and blocked communication with the database. 15:00:  The AV SME restarted the av-core (av-backend) server, which triggered a backup in device messages 15:10:  SMEs began to see delayed device messages starting to report. 16:24:  All devices were reporting in real-time.

summary: Orange Data Connectivity (15-min.) Incident - Involving Multiple MNOs / Customers
description: Since 20250310 22:01 UTC, GNOC observed a increase of traffic over all network (Radius, Vssf, Sigtran, Diameter). Upon investigation, GNOC saw that there was a increase of System Failure for multiple customer and over multiple Roaming Partner. However, Roaming Partner Orange was showing the most impact. Hence, externally, Orange was engaged, GNOC believes that Orange had some issue on their network that impacted other VPLMNs and Roaming Partners. Internally, GNOC also engaged the L3 team to investigate this matter from a Semtech network perspective, i.e., PGW. Current Status: Service Restored. Awaiting Orange CSC feedback. GNOC requested for the carrier to check in with their IPX/GRX provider for the 15minute incident occurring between the 20250310 22:00 – 22:15 UTC timeframe. Orange Ticket Number S503732893 Customer Complaint Invers GmbH ( SaleForce Ticket 01852942 ) Start time: 20250310 22:00 UTC End time: 20250310 22:15 UTC Duration: 15 minutes Impacted customers: Invers GmbH, Bambora Device AB, ZEPLUG, Carrier Fire and Security B.V., Thales SIX GTS France – SAPHIR BAR 2023, Networth Telecom, Solustop, Synox Impacted VPLMNs: Orange France, Vodafone D2 GmbH, TelefÃ³nica Germany GmbH & Co. oHG, Vodafone Libertel B.V. Ziggo, KPN B.V., Vodafone Italia S.p.A, Telia Sverige AB, Telenor Sverige AB, Nuuday A/S, Vodafone EspaÃ±a Affected services: Sigtran, Diameter, Vssf, Radius Affected Roaming Partner: Orange Device Type: ESSENTIALS5 Sample ICCID: 8933240100001641588 Orange System failures on Sigtran and Diameter traffic: Service Impact: GNOC took an example of one sample ICCID 8933240100001641050 & observed that the existing sessions dropped with system failure & with multiple tries for Update Location finally the device established a session. So, all ongoing data sessions were dropped. And there was delay in registration & starting of new data session.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: GNOC This ticket is getting more visibility.  Orange needs to explain why multiple MNOs were impacted.   Thus, I reached back to the Orange Service Centre within the portal for the following inquiry which needs answers.  See a portion of my follow request(s) to the carrier after they ignored the inquiry request for the past few days.  What was taking place on your IPX/GRX peer provider from 2025-03-10 22:00 UTC to 2025-03-10 22:15 UTC?   Please investigate and examine as we see multiple MNOs (VPLMNs) disturbed during that time frame.  This investigation can be expedited by approaching the Orange IPX/GRX provider.  Please investigate why many of Orange’s roaming sponsors or MNOs were impacted at the same time.    Here is a list of Orange visited (partner) networks also impacted:     Orange France, T-Mobile USA, Inc, Hutchison 3G UK Limited, Vodafone D2 GmbH, KPN B.V., Telia Sverige AB, AT&T Mobility, Telia Nattjanster Norden AB, Telenor Sverige AB, Hutchison Drei Austria, KPN B.V., Telenor Norge AS, Orange Espana, S.A., sociedad unipersonal, Proximus PLC, Telecom Italia SpA Why did all these Orange roaming partners get impacted?   Who is the Orange IPX/GRX peer provider?   Do you already have an existing ticket opened with the IPX/GRX provider for this incident?    Greetings    , Please have your team(s) also analyze this issue to rule out any underlying issues that may be lurking within Semtech’s network and/or maybe your investigate can help us assist Orange in their investigation maybe to prove that they may have had a potential IPX/GRX Peer provider issue. Awareness:

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: Greetings     ,   Were you or your packet core team given a chance to investigate this 15-minute 03102025 Orange Incident?  Did anything happen to our PGW similar to 12182024 Data Service (GTP-U) incident?  Based upon what L2 Technical relayed, Invers claimed that all 40+K units on Essentials5 “did not send / receive data for ~ 15 min.” Timeline Start time: 2025-03-10 22:00 UTC End time: 2025-03-10 22:15 UTC Duration: 15 minutes Could this be related to the VTY PGW bug issue that stopped forwarding user-plane traffic on 12182024?  If so, that would result in impacted devices being unable to successfully send and/or receive data, correct? Can you provide an Invers assessment from the PGW perspective between 2025-03-10 22:00 – 22:15 UTC? Right now, GNOC has the carrier checking in with their IPX/GRX provider.  Awareness:

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: GNOC will continue to pursue the carrier investigation for their IPX/GRX provider feedback. Per my conversation with Minh, he looked at the case and reported that there were no issues on the PGW platform.  The Affirmed (PGW) bug that attributed to the 12182024 Data Service (GTP-U) Incident was patched up about 3 weeks ago.  All PGWs have been updated to version 14.  Thus, the GTP-U issue was not present in this case and there were no PGW (GTP-U) alarms triggered.     Awareness:

Their team have resolved the issue Telia ticket: op000007195310 Status Page: 22:43 UTC: Initial Status Page sent. 23:59 UTC: 2nd Status Page sent .Maingate Cacti Dashboard as of 22:19 UTC 3/10 Since Marcel has helped explain how we can obtain customer impact from Cacti graphs and the issue was rectified by the morning of 3/11, I am resolving out this particular ticket.

summary: EU AirVantage Degradation in Alerting based on Monthly or Daily Data
description: EU AirVantage Degradation in Alerting on aggregated Usage Records and aggregated DataPoints The Cloud Engineering team reported: Semtech has an issue on AirVantage that relates to data aggregation. This started on the 25th at around 04:00 UTC. The impact is on alerts based on aggregated datapoints or aggregated connectivity usage records and API access to recent aggregated datapoints; access to aggregated datapoints computed before the 25th is ok. The root cause is the database in charge of serving these APIs became stuck during an AWS maintenance process, preventing the database to be updated with newer data. We are working on restoring those features. Final Update: ( Resolved ) The AV Cloud team confirmed that the data aggregation processing has caught up At 20250228 01:17 UTC. This issue is RESOLVED; GNOC will monitor for 2 hours before closing the ticket. The Cloud team initiated a postmortem investigation via INCIDENT1960 . Resolution: AWS Support unblocked a locked table. The aggregation process began resuming. The AV Cloud Engineering team monitored and waited for aggregated data to catch up and come uptodate. As of now, the process has caught up to today, February 28th, being realtime.. No Customer reported tickets No Alerts were received. Start Time: 20250225 04:00 UTC End Time: 20250228 01:17 UTC Duration: 2 days, 21 hours, 17 minutes

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: Status: On-going; Recovering – (Significant Improvements / Progress Monitored) Currently, Aggregated Data is behind 3+hours No Customer Reported tickets.   Next Steps:   The AV team is closely monitoring progress, and GNOC is working with SMEs awaiting confirmation that the Monthly and Daily Datapoints and connectivity usage records are up to date.  Currently, data aggregation has caught up to today, February 27th 19:00 UTC, making the platform over 3 hours behind being real-time. ETRs have proven unreliable as SMEs mentioned that there are large batches (of data) to process  Summary: Since 2025-02-25 04:00 UTC the EU AirVantage (AV) platform encountered a data aggregation issue affecting Alerting based on Monthly or Daily Data.  This incident was triggered by a database in charge of serving API access to recent aggregated datapoints became stuck.  An AWS maintenance process prevented the database from being able to update with newer data.  Our AV Cloud Engineering team was actively working with the AWS Support team on a locked table that the AWS team was able to unblock.    Following the corrective actions AV Cloud Engineers worked to resume the data aggregation process.  Right now data aggregation has caught up to today, February 27 th  19:00 UTC, thus the platform is over 3 hours behind being real-time.     Impact: Subscribed customers may have experienced delays in data push for connectivity usage, alerting based on aggregated datapoints, or connectivity usage records after February 24th.  These services also fall into the UI, API, and Data Push AV categories. Affected:  There was Degradation in Alerting based on Monthly or Daily Data.  Subscribed customers may have experienced delays in data push for connectivity usage, alerting based on aggregated datapoints, or connectivity usage records after February 24th.  AirVantage UI (EU), API (EU), Data Push (EU) Unaffected:  Provisioning operations, traffic, and device data management remain fully operational.  All access to aggregated datapoints ( Alerting based on Monthly or Daily Data ) computed before February 25th are fine. Start Time: 2025-02-25 04:00 UTC End Time:    N/A Duration:     N/A   Detected: 2025-02-27 15:22 UTC Initial Statuspage sent at 15:59 UTC   Pascal and Rashied worked on the incident with AV SMEs from Cloud Engineering engaged with AWS.   There were No Alerts There were No KPIs to health check.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2025-02-28 01:17 UTC The AV Cloud team confirmed that the data aggregation processing has caught up.  This issue is RESOLVED, GNOC will monitor for 2 hours prior to closing ticket. Awareness:

summary: Software bug on Carrier Partner Sparkle's ORS Platform
description: GNOC RCA: GNOC7091: 01242025 Service Disruption due to bug in Sparkle platform Final RCA Executive Summary: Sparkle encountered a roaming service outage in North America across multiple MNOs, mostly AT&T and TMobile. Upon investigation with Sparkle, it was concluded that the issue was because of activation of a feature on their ORS platform which was enabled to help them with health check. Sparkle had to roll back the changes & restarted the service to fix the issue. This incident primarily affected Chargepoint customer devices with failures and delays in Essentials 1 service establishment and network connectivity. Current Status: Sparkle has provided RFO. 1st Instance: 20250124 15:40 – 18:10 UTC 2nd Instance: 20250125 14:54 – 17:50 UTC On January 24, between 15:43 UTC and 18:00 UTC, GNOC observed an increase in RNA and system failures for ChargePoint and some other customers, over RP Sparkle and VPLMN Verizon. In response, GNOC referred the issue to the SME and Sparkle for investigation. Sparkle confirmed they had already reported KPI degradation, particularly for the afternoon of January 24 (Rome Timezone). They engaged Syniverse Carrier to identify the root cause, focusing on significant impacts for US destination traffic. Currently, GNOC is still waiting for feedback from Sparkle regarding the investigation. Sales Force Ticket → 01846329 (ChargePoint) Sparkle Ticket: CSFA00622629 Preliminary RCA provided by Sparkle . Final RCA to be received on 29th Jan’2025. Dear Team, Regardng the last episodes, after indepth research, we found out that the root cause of the recent Diameter traffic spikes is to be found in an incorrect functioning of the recently implemented Health Check feature on ORS platform. To resolve the problem, it was necessary to deactivate the feature and restart it to complete the change. The issue was definitely resolved at approximately 13:00 CET, 27/01/2025. As agreed during the Teams call, Sparkle will provide a summary detail and all informatio

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: Per Minh’s investigation, I have been checking. It looks like there was possibly an issue at&t for Sparkle. This triggered the devices to try other networks which is Verizon and that is the cause of the RNA   but you can tell by looking at the sequence. Most of these devices were working on AT&T, the something occurred for a session-timeout of the radius record. Then the device would try to register onto AT&T again. But was not working so it got PUR. the sim then tried to use another network which was verizon

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: called contact center for sparkle at +39 06 321000, and they advised they did have a ticket opened up with syniverse but could not divulge the ticket number from syniverse side.  they could not guarantee the estimated timeframe of updates from syniverse but advised we should be receiving some new information via email and we can call back.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: called to the support line for sparkle contact center again @ +39 06 321000 and they advsd they didn’t have any udpates from synvierse as of 22:00 UTC, but they had escalated to level 1 support.  i did admit services do look to be restored back on non-verizon vplmns, but that we still needed to find out what happened with syniverse.  we should be looking for updates in the gnoc inbox and if not getting anything within next hour or so, probably give them a call back.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Sparkle has asked for the latest KPI trend and fresh PCAP, and GNOC has replied.  FYI…traffic is back to normal and we will continue to push Sparkle for the root cause and will share the fresh logs.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Greetings!!   Below are the Investigations we found with the help of Sample IMSI and Viavi: We captured the fresh PCAP and based on our analysis we find that below are two major issues: 1: RNA : IMSI: 222013430025404 After receiving UL requests from unknown sources(MCC-311, MNC-480 Verizon Wireless) , They are not allowed to use Semtech Network hence Our network is sending an RNA. 2: Delete Session Request: IMSI: 222013167665353 There was something happened at T Mobile during that time and Semtech received a Delete Session request from Sparkle. All the message was a success. We have sent the same sample to Sparkle and asked for the root cause ASAP.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Latest feedback from Sparkle: As already reported we observed some KPI degradation especially for yesterday afternoon (Rome Timezone), we engaged Syniverse Carrier in order to find some specific root cause reason because you reported US Country destination major impacted. We are still waiting their consistent feedback.   Currently the KPI are back to normal from our check performed.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: Greetings     ,  Why did we see Data (retransmission) messages or errors between our Semtech DEA and HSS?  Between Internet Protocol Version 4, Src: 46.23.241.243, Dst: 10.60.40.62 See  IMSI   208243090864799 captured packet trace: http://10.90.40.14/ens/calltrace/rlataillade/f480aad0-db7e-11ef-8e97-ed777f7e3cce?loading=true

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: Although we have observed significant traffic improvements, we are still seeing elevated levels of Diameter traffic.  Sparkle was requested to continue to work with Syniverse to gather answers for the last two days of their provider network issues in North America for the following time frames:. 1st Instance:   2025-01-24 15:40 – 18:10 UTC 2nd Instance:  2025-01-25 14:54 – 17:50 UTC 01/25/2025 (All Times 24Hr. UTC) 18:21:   I called into the Sparkle Contact Center and spoke with Andre, requesting a Syniverse update from their escalation.  Andre stated that his team was awaiting feedback from GNOC regarding improvements or any ongoing issues to report. 19:00:   As the carrier requested, I sent an email to Sparkle with current KPIs:  Diameter traffic remained high and Sigtran traffic returned to normal.  I continued to   request a Syniverse update from their provider escalation. 19:31:   I called Sparkle NOC Mobile manager, Marco Marciante, he was upset about receiving unknown EPS subscription (5420) and RNA (5004) detail information from the GNOC.  He mentioned that Manuele Paoletti, NOC Mobile Team Leader, would be sending out an email with detailed feedback explaining that Sparkle does not control those errors on the Semtech network. 19:45:   NOC Mobile Team Leader,   Manuele Paoletti,   sent out an response to me (GNOC) stating the following:   “Yes, as we have mentioned, the incident was escalated with Syniverse.” “According to their analysis (and as our analyses confirm), the problem is not in the UL, but in the fact that you are replying with an error ‘Diameter error unknown EPS subscription(Exp 5420)’….” “Please find pcap trace attached.”   From:  Paoletti Manuele (Guest) < manuele.paoletti@guest.telecomitalia.it >  Sent:  Saturday, January 25, 2025 2:45 PM To:  Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com > Cc:  Corsaletti Valerio (Guest) < valerio.corsaletti@guest.telecomitalia.it >; Yash Vijay Jadhav < yjadhav@semtech.com >; Noc Mobile < noc_mobile@tisparkle.com >; Marciante Marco < marco.marciante@tisparkle.com >; Forgione Vincenzo < vincenzo.forgione@tisparkle.com >; Sparkle Contact Center < SparkleContactCenter@tisparkle.com >; Paoletti Manuele (Guest) < manuele.paoletti@guest.telecomitalia.it >; Robert T James < rjames@semtech.com >; Rashied Lataillade < rlataillade@semtech.com > Subject:  R: [EXT] Re: Case CSFA-00622629 Your Ref: GNOC-7091    Hi Rashied,   Yes, as we have mentioned, the incident was escalated with Syniverse. According to their analysis (and as our analyses confirm), the problem is not in the UL, but in the fact that you are replying with an error “Diameter error unknown EPS subscription(Exp 5420)”. Please find pcap trace attached.   According to Syniverse:   SVR it is sending traffic as its supposed to and Telecom Italia Mobile it is responding with a 5420. No SVR issue found in packet capture example attached.   So, your customer is not allowing Verizon in this case, correct?   Basically, Syniverse confirms no problem on their side; if you have any clarifications about this issue (for example, what do you mean by "unknown sources") you can share them, as we remain available for further investigations. With that said, let me confirm once again that no issue has been observed within TIS network and no other customer has complained of the same problems in North America or, more generally, with diameter traffic. TIS has no ongoing Major Outage with any Roaming Partner and there are  NO PROBLEMS ON SPARKLE NETWORK WITH USA .   In addition to this, let us show you our graphs related to Diameter traffic. This is the situation for our ORS platform:   And this is the situation for SEMTECH:   These graphs seem to point to the fact that SEMTECH is generating network spike, while no abnormality is observed on TIS side. We suggest to check this on your side.   Best Regards, Manuele Paoletti _________________________________________ Sparkle   NOC Mobile   Team Leader Network Operations Service Platform Telecom Italia Sparkle S.p.A. Via di Macchia Palocco, 223 – 00125 ROMA +39 06 52744821 Linkedin  –  Twitter  -  tisparkle.com 20:33   I responded to the Sparkle team via email stating that we will re-analyzed this case with the GNOC team internally. 21:12:   I called into the Syniverse Control Center and spoke with Diego, an engineer with the provider.  I asked if they had “any issues with EU roaming customers attempting to roam on AT&T, T-Mobile, and other operators in North America?”  “If so, has an incident notification been communicated?”   Diego stated that there was an incident earlier approximately between 2025-01-25 14:00 – 16:10 UTC.  However, if I wanted the formal incident notification that I would need to send an email with the details of the issue.  I did not email the provider since formally all of Semtech’s information was directly connected to Sparkle for the span of the incident. 01/26/2025 (All Times 24Hr. UTC) 00:28:   I am responded to the Sparkle NOC Mobile and its leadership via email with the following: Greetings Manuele / Marco / Sparkle colleagues,   That is correct; that customer is not allowed to utilize Verizon.  Pardon the errant information that was sent earlier.  Although we have observed significant traffic improvements, we are still seeing elevated levels of Diameter traffic.  Please continue to work with Syniverse to gather answers into the last two days of their provider network issues in North America.   1st Instance:   2025-01-24 15:40 – 18:10 UTC 2nd Instance:  2025-01-25 comments.body: 2025-01-26 08:40 UTC - Sparkle informed that they restarted their GTProxy module as a preventative action. As a result, a spike of traffic for Diameter and Sigtran was observed through Kibana. After Sparkle performing their restart, ChargePoint and Nextivity increase has both dropped back down to normal pattern.  From:  Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com >  Sent:  Sunday, January 26, 2025 08:53 To:  Forgione Vincenzo < vincenzo.forgione@tisparkle.com >; Rashied Lataillade < rlataillade@semtech.com >; Paoletti Manuele (Guest) < manuele.paoletti@guest.telecomitalia.it >; Marciante Marco < marco.marciante@tisparkle.com >; Noc Mobile < noc_mobile@tisparkle.com >; Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com > Cc:  Corsaletti Valerio (Guest) < valerio.corsaletti@guest.telecomitalia.it >; Yash Vijay Jadhav < yjadhav@semtech.com >; Sparkle Contact Center < SparkleContactCenter@tisparkle.com >; Robert T James < rjames@semtech.com > Subject:  RE: [EXT] Re: Case CSFA-00622629 Your Ref: GNOC-7091   Greetings  @Forgione Vincenzo , Thanks for the update. We have been keeping an eye on this matter, and we did observe an increase of Diameter traffic that lasted for 24 minutes. We will continue to monitor this matter and inform you when we are certain that everything is back to normal.  Please keep this ticket open until further notice. Sincerely,   Global Network Operations Center (GNOC)    SEMTECH | SIERRA WIRELESS Toll Free  +1 (844) 367 7939   Suite AB & E, 7/F, No. 1&3, Yuangdong Rd., Banqiao Dist., New Taipei City 22063, Taiwan  From:  Forgione Vincenzo < vincenzo.forgione@tisparkle.com >  Sent:  Sunday, January 26, 2025 08:40 To:  Rashied Lataillade < rlataillade@semtech.com >; Paoletti Manuele (Guest) < manuele.paoletti@guest.telecomitalia.it >; Marciante Marco < marco.marciante@tisparkle.com >; Noc Mobile < noc_mobile@tisparkle.com >; Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com > Cc:  Corsaletti Valerio (Guest) < valerio.corsaletti@guest.telecomitalia.it >; Yash Vijay Jadhav < yjadhav@semtech.com >; Sparkle Contact Center < SparkleContactCenter@tisparkle.com >; Robert T James < rjames@semtech.com >; Forgione Vincenzo < vincenzo.forgione@tisparkle.com > Subject:  R: [EXT] Re: Case CSFA-00622629 Your Ref: GNOC-7091    HI Rashed and all Semtech team,   Just to update and inform we restarted our GTProxy module as preventive action some minute ago in order to see if improvement observed for you problem. The degradation you seen after is only a consequence but now should be ok.   Please continue to monitor on your end and inform us if now kpi are back to normal.   Thanks for cooperation   Best Regards, Vincenzo Forgione __________________   Duty Manager GNOC Chief Tecnology Office -  Operations Service Platforms & Multicloud Infrastructure   Telecom Italia Sparkle S.p.A.  Via di Macchia Palocco, 223 – 00125 ROMA  Linkedin  –  Twitter  -  tisparkle.com

comments.author: Mohamed Amine Elbahi
comments.body: ‘Why did we see Data (retransmission) messages or errors between our Semtech DEA and HSS?” ==> deduplication mechanism in viavi doesn’t work if the packet is seen on different probe-id. which is the case for the call flow sent.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Sparkle has updated that they will provide an official RCA/RFO by 29th .

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings !!! Preliminary RCA provided by Sparkle . Final RCA to be received on 29th Jan’2025. Dear Team, Regardng the last episodes, after in-depth research, we found out that the root cause of the recent Diameter traffic spikes is to be found in an incorrect functioning of the recently implemented Health Check feature on ORS platform.  To resolve the problem, it was necessary to deactivate the feature and restart it to complete the change. The issue was definitely resolved at approximately 13:00 CET, 27/01/2025. As agreed during the Teams call, Sparkle will provide a summary detail and all informations available during the Monthly meeting scheduled for Wednesday 29th. Best Regards

description: Planned Maintenance : CN:1092001 Customer Impact Syniverse has scheduled planned maintenance on Electronic Data Transport Hosts which will require a reboot of the following servers on the scheduled dates. During this reboot activity, customers may experience brief connectivity issues while sending files to Syniverse for approximately 30 minutes. summary: EU AirVantage Usage Display and Alerting Degradation
description: Executive Summary Database memory has over exceeded limited due to a huge ingesting of usages from Verizon. Throttling adjustment for ingestion were made for resolution. Customers experienced delays in realtime usages publishing and alerting degradation. Total Outage Time 2 hours 33 minutes (153 minutes) Start Time 20250114 08:08 UTC End Time 20250114 10:41 UTC Duration 2 hours 33 minutes Customer Complaint None Initial StatusPage (Investigating) 20250114 09:42 UTC (Degraded Performance) Second StatusPage (Monitoring) 20250114 10:44 UTC Third StatusPage (Resolved) 20250114 12:44 UTC Detailed Summary Since 20250114 08:08 UTC, AV was showing a delay to publish realtime usages. Upon further notification, AV SME informed that customer were impacted. Hence, GNOC initiated incident management process in which StatusPage and dedicated channel were setup for engagements. SME informed that this is due to a huge ingesting of usages from Verizon, hence, using too much memory in database. SME adjust the throttling of ingestion and waited for all yak events to catch up before declaring issue resolved.

summary: Alert: Lost Service for Single VPLMN Everything Everywhere Limited (CSI)
description: GNOC Received a P4 Kibana alert for kibana: VPLMN DOWN Everything Everywhere Limited [MVNOEU LostService] Radius Terminated sessions increase | GNOC Checked the KPI and observed a 28mins , 7:55 to 8:23 UTC Lost service . The levels are back now and GNOC Dont observe any anomaly further GNOC initiated an investigation and observed a temporary increase in Lost Service for the single VPLMN Everything Everywhere Limited in UK . The service degradation began at 20251029 07:55 UTC and was associated with ORANGE901 and SPARKLE roaming partners. Multiple customers were impacted, including Nextivity and Invers GmbH , and primarily 4G authentication services were affected. After detailed analysis, GNOC confirmed that the error count Decreased , and no customer complaints were reported during the event. There was a Drop in Diameter traffic and a SIGTRAN ( SAI ) traffic was increased , The Device might Switched The RAT to 2G/3G From 4G . Key Details Parameter Details Customer(s) Nextivity Yara International ASA John F Hunt Power Invers GmbH Tedd Engineering BEA S.A. Prod Error Type LOST Service Traffic Procedure STOP, 2G/3G Auth Roaming Partner(s) ORANGE901, SPARKLE Device Type(s) ADVANCED4 ADVANCED2 ESSENTIALS1 ESSENTIALS5 ADVANCED3 VPLMN Everything Everywhere Limited Country UK Start Time 20251029 07:55 UTC End Time 20251029 08:23 UTC Duration 28 min Current Status & Next Action Lost Service levels are normal . Continuous monitoring is ongoing via OpenSearch dashboards. No customer complaints or escalations received so far. Customer Impact : Devices likely experienced momentary network drops, followed by delayed session reconnection. Reference Snip: OpenSearch Link

summary: Alert : Lost service Spikes in NAM (CSI)
description: On 14th October 2025, from 7:45 UTC, GNOC observed 3 intermittent spikes for Lost service 7:45 to 7:54, 7:58 to 8:13, and 8:26 to 8:34 in NAM via multiple VPLMN (TMobile, At&t mainly). Through Multiple Roaming Sponsor, GNOC observed that data sessions were shifted from 4G to 2G/3G during these spikes GNOC investigated this issue in OpenSearch & observed that the increase in Lost Service started at 07:45UTC & returned to normal level at 08:34 UTC. More details as follows. Current Status → Lost Service via has returned to normal level. Sparkle Ticket → CSFA00654097 Update From Sparkle → Dear NOC Team, We received below update from the Roaming Partner; We do not see any impacts in Diameter S6a ULReq's on 14 October. However, there was a dip during last night's maintenance window but that has normalized after the maintenance window closed. See attached trending graph. From Syniverse the first maintenance notification was via CN:1088322 , which had updated as “ The Syniverse NW Infrastructure Team will be performing a reload on the r002wnv NGN router in Wentzville, MO due to memory corruption caused by excessive uptime. This activity may briefly impact Wentzville STP SS7/Sigtran signaling connections. Redundancy is in place to carry any related traffic via Syniverse Dallas STPs to avoid interruptions. However it seems the failover to Syniverse Dallas STPs did not work as expected. Hence Syniverse sent us an new notification via CN:109005 which mentioned – IPX, IPX peering, DSS, SS7 and MPCC customers connect to dallas might detect BGP flapping during the FPC restarted. Hence Syniverse engaged vendor to investigate, vendor provided option to perform a hard reboot, Syniverse followed instruction and all service back to normal at 10:08 EST / 14:08 UTC, device has been stable for 14hrs since the reboot. So it seems the Lost Service we encountered in NAM via Premium carrier TMobile & ATT was because of this Syniverse issue. Another reason is also, during this incident we did

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: GNOC levels have remained normal. Will continue monitoring. Zenoss

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: I just responded to Sparkle with the following: During these episodes of Lost Service for data our customers experienced this mostly on the T-Mobile USA network.   These disruptions to their ongoing radius activity spiked at the aforementioned timeframes  1 st  Instance - 7:45 to 7:54 UTC 2 nd  instance - 7:58 to 8:13 UTC 3 rd  instance - 8:13 to 8:26 UTC 4 th  instance - 9:42 to 9:53 UTC  However, the main commonalities were between the SGW IP addresses as listed below:  Roaming Sponsor IP addresses 213.144.191.13 213.144.191.69 213.144.191.68 213.144.191.12  And our NAS IP address of  192.119.185.115  I am including a pcap for one of our customers during these spikes of data disruption.    Please confirm whether there was an issue with the local VPLMN or the GRX provider at the time and engage either party as needed.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !! With the information provided from Syniverse, I  have tried to correlate our Lost Service issue with Syniverse Updates & arrived at below conclusion.   As the issue has been resolved & it seems the issue was from Syniverse end,    &    I am complething this ticket now. From Syniverse the first maintenance notification was via CN:108832-2 , which had updated as “  The Syniverse NW Infrastructure Team will be performing a reload on the r002-wnv NGN router in Wentzville, MO due to memory corruption caused by excessive uptime. This activity may briefly impact Wentzville STP SS7/Sigtran signaling connections. Redundancy is in place to carry any related traffic via  Syniverse Dallas STPs  to avoid interruptions.   However it seems  the  failover to Syniverse Dallas STPs   did not work as expected.    Hence Syniverse sent us an new notification via CN:109005 which mentioned – IPX, IPX peering, DSS, SS7 and MPCC customers connect to dallas might detect  BGP flapping during the FPC restarted.   Hence Syniverse engaged vendor to investigate, vendor provided option to perform a hard reboot, Syniverse followed instruction and all service back to normal at 10:08 EST / 14:08 UTC, device has been stable for 14hrs since the reboot.      So it seems the Lost Service we encountered in NAM via Premium carrier T-Mobile & ATT was because of this Syniverse issue. Another reason is also, during this incident we did not encounter any issue with ECC offering in NAM.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Update from Sparkle. Dear NOC Team, We received  below update  from the  Roaming Partner;  We do not see any impacts in Diameter S6a ULReq's on 14 October. However, there  was a dip during last night's maintenance window  but that has normalized after the maintenance window closed. See attached trending graph.

summary: Comfone Maintenance Announcement: Diameter Signaling links in Amsterdam AM7 - 20251028_01
description: Dear Customer, As part of our commitment to continually improve the level of service we provide, Comfone is announcing a Maintenance on its backbone infrastructure in Equinix Amsterdam AM7. The maintenance work includes upgrading device firmware which will impact your diameter Signalling links in Amsterdam AM7 as per below. Maintenance time start: 28.10.2025, 22:00 (GMT/UTC±00) Maintenance time end: 29.10.2025, 04:00 (GMT/UTC±00) Impact: There will be interruptions on Diameter Signalling links to dranl02.comfone.com . The work will not be service affecting as Comfone Amsterdam AMS9, Ashburn DC2, Singapore SG1, Miami MI1 and Hong Kong Mega I Advantage DRAs will remain in service and be unaffected. Please forward this email to all concerned parties within your organization. If you have any questions and to determine how this work will affect your services, please contact Comfone NOC via the below contact options. Regards, NOC Network Operation Centre Comfone AG Mingerstrasse 12 3014 Bern Switzerland Phone: +41 31 341 11 00 Email: noc@comfone.com Web: www.comfone.

Update may cause temporary glitches to web interfaces and small break to Main numbers and queues. Logged in user may need to relogin to Web interface. This is an automatic email from https://efecteitsm.dna.fi

summary: Data Service (GTP-U) Incident | Affecting Orange Smart Connectivity - Advanced/Essentials5 devices
description: GNOC RCA: GNOC6780: 12182024 Semtech Data Service (GTPU) Incident Affecting Orange Smart Connectivity Final RCA Executive Summary : Semtech experienced a network disruption in Smart Connectivity Data services and connectivity. The incident affected customers using the Orange global network's Advanced / Essentials 5 subscription offers. For most premier customers this service outage lasted 30 minutes without intervention. However, some devices for a subset of customers are not enabled with a quick session recovery setup, which prolonged their outage experience. Thus, Semtech SMEs manually cleared those devices from the network to reestablish service. The root cause is under investigation; Semtech engineers engaged the partner carrier, Orange, and its PGW vendor, Affirmed, to assess and further investigate the cause. (Major Outage) All Customers Start Time: 20241218 09:04 UTC End Time: 20241218 09:34 UTC Duration: 30 minutes (Extender) A subset of customers Some customer devices are not enabled with a quick session recovery setup. Those devices required manual clearing from the network to reestablish service. Start Time: 20241218 08:50 UTC End Time: 20241218 16:37 UTC Duration: 7 hours, 47 minutes Details Summary On December 18th, at 09:04 UTC, a signaling storm with roaming sponsor Orange901 caused a disruption lasting approximately 15 minutes. This issue impacted some customers' data services, resulting in reports of service loss. Our technical team (SME & L2) is working promptly to restore services, focusing on customers whose devices failed to automatically reset after the disruption. Issue : The P1 and P3 alerts received by the GNOC (Global Network Operations Center) indicate that the BICS/Orange/JT Signaling Service is DEGRADED . The related alerts are automatically closed after 2030 minutes. Impact : Four customers have reported outages affecting their private APN, and the L2 Team has confirmed abnormal data traffic via Slack for some customers. Note: The issue

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: Update from Xiaosong from Slack channel. Just for an update: take a look at the global session numbers. I noticed a significant increase in the transition from idle to active states — the number of active sessions is three times higher than before. I also verified that there are no major events or packets causing session deletions and recreations that could explain this transition. I doubt the accuracy of the session state being displayed as 'active'. anyway just keep you update for any progress and news:

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2025-01-07 16:04 UTC after leaving voice messages with Orange’s L2 and L3 escalation contacts, I spoke with Emil from the Orange Customer Service Center.  Emil mentioned that there was a default on the Swedish operator network according to the GTP-U trace that I provided.  On the phone/portal - I requested for Emil/CSC team to provide feedback to inquiry below:  What is the status update? Which Operator? Can we get access to stats / traces from the [Orange – VPLMN (operator) side] to see what dialog occurred with devices at the signaling and GTP levels (in particular, where the delete originates, their count vs active sessions)?

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Greetings Team, Orange has shared the conclusions of their Investigation on the Portal: Hello Semtech team, Please find below the results and conclusions of our analyzes and investigations following the incident of 12/18/2024.  Following an initial analysis, our expert teams  observed the same issue as you mentioned in the ticket : a spike in all types of signaling (MAP DIAMATER, ETC, etc.) but only on Semtech IMSIs at the given time stamp. Further investigation revealed no traces of MAP, DIAMETER or GTPC on the given time slot on the Imsi example you provided. Our platform provider (Mobileum) also confirmed that nothing had been found concerning GTPU during the same time slot, and recommended contacting the swedish VPLMN(s) as the traffic spike was originating from their networks. In the days that followed, our roaming experts contacted the 2 Swedish VPLMNs (Telenor & Telia), but they did not confirm Mobileum's claims, as neither of them had observed any anomaly. We have therefore exhausted all avenues of investigation and remain to this day without any explanation as to what happened during the given range. Sincerely Benoit    Please suggest here, What we can reply to Orange. Thanks !!

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !!! Orange has closed the ticket.

comments.author: Robert T James
comments.body: Incident priority = P2

summary: Alert:  Lost Service for Single VPLMN Orange Espana, S.A., sociedad unipersonal (CSI)
description: On October 19, 2025, at 06:24 UTC , GNOC received a P4 Kibana alert indicating that VPLMN Orange España, S.A., Sociedad Unipersonal was reported DOWN . GNOC initiated an investigation and observed a temporary increase in Lost Service for the single VPLMN Orange España, S.A., Sociedad Unipersonal in Spain . The service degradation began at 20251019 06:00 UTC and was associated with ORANGE901 and SPARKLE roaming partners. Multiple customers were impacted, including Invers GmbH , and primarily 2G/3G authentication services were affected. After detailed analysis, GNOC confirmed that the error count remained moderate , and no customer complaints were reported during the event. A JSM ticket was raised for tracking purposes, and during the investigation, we observed a steady recovery toward normal levels . Key Details Parameter Details Customer(s) Invers GmbH, WEEZEVENT SAS, CARRIER SCS, Loomis Digital Solutions, PV6430: PV SUDOUEST Error Type LOST Service Traffic Procedure STOP, 2G/3G Auth Roaming Partner(s) ORANGE901, SPARKLE Device Type(s) ESSENTIALS5, ADVANCED4 VPLMN Orange España, S.A., Sociedad Unipersonal Country Spain Start Time 20251019 06:00 UTC End Time 20251019 08:40 UTC Duration 2 hour 40 min Current Status & Next Action Lost Service levels are gradually reducing and trending toward normal . Continuous monitoring is ongoing via OpenSearch dashboards. No customer complaints or escalations received so far. GNOC will continue monitoring until the service is fully normalized , and updates will be shared in the JSM ticket accordingly. Customer Impact : Devices likely experienced momentary network drops, followed by delayed session reconnection. Reference Snip: OpenSearch Link

summary: Alert: Lost Services Increasing for Sparkle (CSI)
description: Since October 19, 2025, 08:15 UTC , GNOC started receiving multiple P3 alerts indicating high DRA Sparkle signaling messages on the AT1 and AT2 links . Following a detailed investigation using OpenSearch and Zenoss , GNOC observed that between 08:07 UTC and 08:22 UTC , a 15minute Lost Service issue occurred through the Roaming Partner Sparkle , impacting multiple VPLMNs . This incident primarily affected several customers in the NAM region , including ChargePoint . During the impact window, ESSENTIALS1 , Advanced2 , and Advanced3 devices may have experienced session drops and minor delays during reregistration. Although the issue lasted approximately 14 minutes , GNOC has informed the NAM L2 team via Slack and engaged Sparkle for further investigation to identify the root cause . Sparkle ticket number CSFA00654647 Keynotes as Follows: Customer: ChargePoint Inc, AddSecure Group Services, Nextivity, Eskilstuna Strängnäs Energi, Inpro/Seal Company, Mostly Error Type: LOST Service Traffic Procedure: STOP Traffic type: Radius Roaming Partner: Sparkle Device Type: Essentials1, Advanced2 and ADVANCED3 VPLMN: Telia Sverige AB, AT&T Mobility , TMobile USA, Inc , Orange France , Telenor Norge AS ,Telus Communications Inc., Rogers Wireless , Hutchison 3G UK Limited , Bell Mobility Inc. Region: USA and NAM Start Time: 20251019 08:07 UTC End Time: 20251019 08:22 UTC Duration: 15min Sample ICCID: 8933240100002390734 Customer Impact: Customer devices must have dropped off from the network momentarily & would have started the session with a minor delay. Reference Snip:

summary: Cluster of Infrastructure Alerts coming in throughout 2025
description: Recent alerting trends showing up more so since Week 35 into Week 43 have indicated a cluster of prominent servers/nodes alerting without the need of the GNOC’s involvement, by and large. The majority of these have been alerting as P3s, however, none of them have been indicating customer service impact. Since the GNOC hasn’t seen the majority of these point to a service impacting issue or network incident, we are curious as to what duration of the alert’s lifespan would indicate a potential problem to customer’s services? Could it be feasible for these particular alerts to have their priority adjusted to P4 status but they could change to a P3 once they hit a certain timeframe threshold? The list below is for a cluster of 5 server/nodes we have seen trending. Thanks for your input. lspiftpl02: Free space on volume: lspiftpl02/disk1 – 120 YTD lspiftpl01: High CPU Usage – 81 lsppsqlwxpr11: Free space on volume – 290 lsppboswas – 44 lsppftpwxpr01– 141 lspiftpl02: Free space on volume: lspiftpl02/disk1 – 120 YTD – Shows steady influx of one alert per day definitely throughout all of October. lspiftpl01: High CPU Usage – 81 – have picked up in earnest in October, with about one every other day during this current month. lsppsqlwxpr11: Free space on volume – 290 – these come in regularly at intervals of every 7 days (between 6 to 8 alerts) lsppboswas – 44 – these have sporadically come in since 2/13/2025 for servers ranging from lsppbos was23 : High CPU Usage to lsppbos was32 : High CPU Usage lsppftpwxpr01– 141 – have come in varying frequency since for High CPU Usage or Node Down since 1/5/2025

The list below is for a cluster of 5 server/nodes we have seen trending. Thanks for your input. lspiftpl02: Free space on volume: lspiftpl02/disk1 – 120 YTD lspiftpl01: High CPU Usage – 81 lsppsqlwxpr11: Free space on volume – 290 lsppboswas – 44 lsppftpwxpr01– 141 lspiftpl02: Free space on volume: lspiftpl02/disk1 – 120 YTD – Shows steady influx of one alert per day definitely throughout all of October. lspiftpl01: High CPU Usage – 81 – have picked up in earnest in October, with about one every other day during this current month. lsppsqlwxpr11: Free space on volume – 290 – these come in regularly at intervals of every 7 days (between 6 to 8 alerts) lsppboswas – 44 – these have sporadically come in since 2/13/2025 for servers ranging from lsppbos was23 : High CPU Usage to lsppbos was32 : High CPU Usage lsppftpwxpr01– 141 – have come in varying frequency since for High CPU Usage or Node Down since 1/5/2025

summary: Alert: High Traffic on ELB_HTTP_Code_4XX in Production Environment (NSI)
description: At 12:05 UTC on , GNOC received 2 alerts for High Traffic on ELBHTTPCode4XX in the Production Environment. The alerts indicate a high traffic issue on ELBHTTPCode4XX, with current values exceeding the threshold. Specifically, the values recorded were 12.000000 and 10.000000, indicating a significant number of 4XX errors. The incident involves high traffic, resulting in 4XX errors on specific load balancers in the production environment. Upon reviewing the device URL, no information was available in Zenoss to support troubleshooting. At this time, the issue does not appear to be customerimpacting. GNOC will refer to the SME for investigation. Current Status: After further investigation, GNOC found ticket GNOC9962 . According to the notes, GNOC stated that the L3 SME confirmed: “This alert should be deactivated; the SMPP bind for this carrier was just taken down by the network group.” However, we continue to receive alerts. GNOC has opened a task ticket, GNOC9963 , to address this issue. We need to follow up with the monitoring team.

summary: AMM01 Migration from 2.17.6.1 to 2.18
description: Migration of hosted server AMM01 from 2.17.6.1 to 2.18 Impact: The AMM web User Interface will be unavailable during the maintenance window. Gateway operation will be unaffected during this change window. The IP address of this server will change following the procedure. Please confirm with your IT team if your firewall restricts access by IP address. There will be no change to the instance's FQDN. All gateways should be configured to use the server FQDN. In case a gateway running supported firmware and configuration stops checking in after the upgrade, please reboot the gateway and troubleshoot before opening a new support case. AMM 2.18 There are many new features and updates in this release. For more info, please review the release notes at

summary: HC: SC Bambora Devices Diameter RNA (NSI)
description: During HC at 20:30 UTC 10/26/2025, GNOC found an increase in RNA for Bambora. After further checking ticket , this is a known issue. Per Ahmad from L2, Bambora ESS5 SIM can attach only to SONERA MOBILE/Finland and if SONERA has any radio/coverage problem, Bambora Devices will try to attach to DNA and will get RNAs. GNOC to continue to monitor. Final Status → RNA issue is a known customer issue, hence GNOC is complething this ticket. Customer has not opened any tickets in Salesforce Zenoss Roaming Partners View Data Insights HLR Diameter Messages

summary: Syniverse Congestion Issue causing system failure towards multiple roaming partners (JT, Orange & MBQT)
description: Incident Summary: On December 27, 2024, the GNOC received multiple P2 and P3 alerts indicating a system failure. An investigation using OpenSearch revealed the issue affected Jersey, Orange901, and MBQT across multiple VPLMNs for several customers. GNOC has been actively monitoring the situation and conducting further investigations. After extensive communication with our roaming partners, JT worked with their MNO to implement changes, leading to improvements in the KPIs. The system failure issue was fully resolved by December 28, 2024, at 03:20 UTC. Incident Date : December 27, 2024 Incident Start Time : 16:09 UTC Incident End Time : December 28, 2024, 03:20 UTC Duration : 11 hours 11 minutes Tickets : JT/Velos Ticket : IOT34917 Orange Ticket : S412583796 Sparkle Ticket : CSFA00620203 Syniverse Ticket: CN:1014419 Investigation Findings Root Cause : Semtech’s HLR did not receive acknowledgment for the InvInsertSubscriberData message from the roaming partner, resulting in a timeout error after 14 seconds. Current Status : JT reported that their SCCP provider, Syniverse, is experiencing congestion issues causing timeouts and network failures. The final root cause is still pending confirmation. Impacted Areas Customers Affected : Nearly all, including ChargePointnc, Networth Telecom, Franke & Werner GmbH, Nextivity, Solustop, WeezeVent SAS, etc. VPLMNs Affected : Primarily TMobile, Bell Mobility, and Telus. Services Affected : 2G/3G signaling, voice services. Roaming Partners Affected : Jersey, Orange901, MBQT. Device Types Affected : ESSENTIALS3, ESSENTIALS5, and advanced devices. Regions Affected : Mostly North America (NAM). Customer Impact No issues with existing connected devices, but delays were noted in new device registrations. We may Observed degradation in data, SMS, voice, and USSD services. Incident Timeline December 27, 2024 15:50 UTC : Initial status page sent, marking the start of the investigation. 22:27 UTC : Followup status page indicating ongoing inv

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: just emailed sparkle at  sparklecontactcenter@tisparkle.com  with a pcap for 208243090743313 and sp;readsheet listing other affectd VPLMNs..

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: followed up at JT’s portal for  IOT-34917 /CS0105991 asking for more recent findings and if he could confirm if this was an issue with syniverse or some other sccp.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: most recent update from velos IoT - Fri 12/27/2024 6:09 PM ….this is not a good response from the RS… i am on the phone with them now.  Hello GNOC, We have news about your ticket IOT-34917: Just had a call with the engineers and what they see now is and UL is sent from you site toward JT, that promtly pass it on to carrier. Then emediately the session is closed down from you side - so it seems like there is a timeout set to low on you site. Is it possible for you to look into the timeout on you site ? Kind Regards, Velos IoT Support Email:  support@velosiot.com Emergency Support Numbers: North America: +1 8336613220 or RoW: +44 20 8068 0990

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: got their frontline support on the phone and sent over most recent pcap and included screenshot of the timeout, and explained over the phone the timeout is expected if we don’t get a continue or return result last from the far end carrier..   i informed with him that when spotchecking over sytsem failures within same PA sesion, one on at&t and the other on bell canada, they are all hitting the 14th second, thus generating the map timeout.  i told him if they needed to escalate with the connecting sccp, we plead with them to do so.  he xplained that so far, tmobile hasnt been responsive and are claiming we sending back system failures immediately.  i advsd that if is the case, then they should send over to us traces indicating this straightaway.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: spoke with their frontline support twice within past 20 minutes, urging them to set up a call with their engineers.. epxlained we don’t want this issue getting dusty going into the weekend. they just repsonded back to gnoc inbox with following @ 01:41 12/28 UTC  Hello GNOC, We have news about your ticket IOT-34917: waiting for callback from the network engineers will gett a feedback to you asap

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: The latest Update from JT/Velos: JT has confirmed that they have raised cases with Telus, T-Mobile, Bell Canada, and AT&T. Telus has responded with the following message: Their SCCP provider, Syniverse, is currently experiencing congestion issues that are causing timeouts and network failures. They are actively working to restore service as soon as possible, and there have been indications of improvement over the past hour.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Greetings Team, We pushed JT/Velos for this incident, and per the latest feedback received:  “The issue has been escalated to our Network Partner. They handle the contact regarding the SCCP provided through roaming partner agreements. Both Velos IoT and our Network Partner are aware of the urgency and are pushing for a resolution.” We have checked the System Failure error and found that The Failure is decreasing, we are keeping an eye on it and will reach out to the Roaming Partner for the root cause of the issue.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Latest Update from Orange: Orange informed us that They have investigated the issue but did not find any defects on the Orange network. As the issue is already resolved, We have asked to monitor the situation for a few more hours before closing the ticket.  Thanks !!

comments.author: Ajeet Kumar
comments.body: Hi Team, The restoration of services has been confirmed by JT Velos. Official RCA has been requested by GNOC.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: earlier today, we did receive an update from Syniverse in the GNOC Inbox, although we didn’t reach out to them directly; circumstances seem too align for this to be a simple coincidence. email came in between 14:50 to 14:56 UTC today.  Service Degradation : CN:101441-2 Notification This morning, Syniverse noticed an increase in failures, with one customer involved in the service degradation of  December 27th 2024  (not November, disregard date on last notification). We reached out, and they acknowledged that they are having an issue and are working with their vendor. We have blocked the customer and are discarding their traffic to protect the environment while they work to resolve this with their vendor. We will provide additional updates as needed. Again, we value your partnership and appreciate your patience as we've worked to restore normal operating levels.  Impacted Products  SS7 - LINKS AND PORTS,   Notification ID  CN:101441-2 Degradation Source  Syniverse Timestamp  Start Date/Time (GMT):  2024-12-28 12:00

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !! Update from JT  Hello GNOC, We have news about your ticket IOT-34917: We recived this from our Network vendor:  SCCP Carrier Syniverse observed high utilisation and congestion issues with one of their customers which resulted in increased traffic on egress connections.  That partner was initially blocked but has now been re-enable following a fix on their end. In short this was a signaling storm in the US area, unfortunately that is all the information we can expect to receive. Kind Regards, Velos IoT Support

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !! Update from Syniverse . We are providing an additional service update.  Continued rebalancing measures across the global network have resulted in further stability, as monitored, and reported throughout the day. The Syniverse team will move forward with our industry partners to resolve remaining third-party issues. We will share significant updates, should and when they arise.

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: Response from Syniverse Syniverse wants to provide another update to our ongoing progress to improve the stability of the global SS7 network and customer experience of the global roaming community.?  Given the congestion experienced on December 27 and 28, as well as third-party disruption this morning ET, Syniverse has taken additional necessary steps to harden the network in preparation for spikes in traffic anticipated in connection with the New Year?s holiday or any further unanticipated third-party issues.?  We will be making these changes this afternoon (December 31 ET). This will propagate throughout the network on a rolling basis through midday January 1 ET. We will actively monitor throughout the window. We do not anticipate any material impact to end users.?  We appreciate your partnership and will continue to provide updates if any significant changes arise. Impacted Products   SS7 - LINKS AND PORTS,   Notification ID   CN:101441-10 Degradation Source   Syniverse Timestamp   Start Date/Time (GMT):  2024-12-28 12:00   

comments.author: Ajeet Kumar
comments.body: Hello Team, There was some activity on Syniverse on December 31, 2024. There are no anomalies that GNOC has noticed.  Official RFO is still pending.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Update from Syniverse . Happy New Year from all of us at Syniverse! We appreciate your continued trust in us. The scheduled technology maintenance window that we informed you of yesterday has been successfully completed.  Stemming from the congestion issues on 27 and 28 of December, further steps were taken to harden the network in preparation for traffic spikes and any unanticipated third-party issues. We appreciate your patience and understanding during this process as we continue to enhance the reliability and performance of our services. We value your partnership.  And again, offer our best wishes to you in the year ahead. We will continue to keep you aware of any significant changes/updates.

summary: Alert: Sparkle-System Failure Issue(CSI)
description: Summary: Since July 22, 2025, at 08:05 UTC, GNOC began receiving multiple P2 and P3 alerts indicating PS/CS system failure errors. Upon investigating Viavi and Zenoss, it was observed that there was a significant increase in system failures related to Sparkle. A deeper analysis using Viavi trace revealed that there was no acknowledgment of the InvInsertSubscriberData message from Sparkle. After 14 seconds, it was sending HLR time out with a System failure error. GNOC issued an initial status update and reported the issue to the SME via the SW channel, while also engaging with the roaming partner Sparkle to find the root cause and fix it. Current Status: Services were fully restored by 08:57 UTC on the same day, July 22, 2025. We have sent the resolved Status page. Root Cause & resolution from Sparkle: RCA received from Sparkle, Note: Open Search is experiencing a delay issue in the counter, so we are unable to provide the additional details. Sparkle Ticket: CSFA00643086 Sparkle ticket for RCA: CSFA00643400 Keynotes as Follows: Keynotes as Follows: Customers: Pending due to OpenSearch issue Traffic Procedure: Error: System Failure Roaming Partner: Sparkle Device Type: Essentials5/ Advanced VPLMN: Pending due to OpenSearch issue Country: Pending due to OpenSearch issue Start Time: 20250722 08:05 UTC End Time: 20250722 08:57 UTC Sample IMSI: 208243090767141 Customer Impact : Customers using Essentials1 & advanced devices faced registration issues with 2G, 3 G, and LTE services, resulting in service impairment for Voice, data, SMS & USSD. comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: RCA received from Sparkle . Type of Fault:   Degradation Fault Localization:   Tis Network/Ors Platform/Sw Fault root cause:   The issue was due to spoofing GT generated at signaling layer, wrongly routed towords the platform increasing the amount of timeouts. Remedy action:   Sparkle identified the wrong routing inside the switching network and definitely solved the issue.

summary: Alert : Orange 901 , Sparkle  System Failure issue (CSI)
description: Summary: Since July 25, 2025, at 10:18 UTC, GNOC began receiving multiple P2 and P3 alerts indicating PS/CS system failure errors. Upon investigating, it was observed that there was a significant increase in system failures related to Orange 901 and Sparkle. A deeper analysis using Viavi trace revealed that there was no acknowledgment of the InvInsertSubscriberData message from Sparkle. After 14 seconds, it was sending HLR time out with a System failure error. GNOC reported the issue to the SME via the SW channel. SME confirmed no issue on our end, while also engaging with the roaming partners to find the root cause. Final Update: Services Restored. GNOC is pursuing RFO reports from the roaming partners. Sparkle Confirmed, they were aware of the problem and checking with their vendor. Per Sparkle (Starting 12:15 Rome time, we are observing signaling traffic increase and possible degradation ongoing. We are troubleshooting with the Vendor and Mobile Specialist team.) The issue was resolved since 12:43 UTC. Feedback from Orange → The issue was from Dutch Carrier iBasis. The root cause is that the signalling was congested at the Dutch carrier IBASIS. Orange ticket S507969661 Sparkle ticket CSFA00643575 Keynotes as Follows: Keynotes as Follows: Customers: Invers GmbH , Chargepoint , Matooma , Networth Telecom, Franke & Werner GmbH , Nextivity , Synox etc. Traffic Procedure: Sigtran Error: System Failure Roaming Partner: Sparkle, Orange 901 Device Type: ADVANCED4 , ESSENTIALS1 , ADVANCED , ADVANCED3 , ADVANCED2 , ESSENTIALS5 VPLMN: KPN B.V ., TMobile USA, Inc., Bouygues Telecom, Orange France etc Country: France, USA, Germany, Netherlands. etc Start Time: 20250725 10:18 UTC End Time: 20250725 12:43 UTC Duration: Sample IMSIs: 901311532684542, 901311532666946, 901311532451960 Customer Impact : Customers using Essentials1, Essentials5 & advanced devices faced registration issues with 2G, 3G, and LTE services, resulting in service impairment for Voice, data, SMS & USSD.

summary: System failure increased through Sparkle
description: On December 12, 2024, at 10:24 UTC , GNOC received P2 & P3 alerts indicating a system failure issue towards Sparkle. Initial investigations, using OpenSearch and Viavi tools, identified elevated spikes in system failure affecting multiple VPLMNs for SIGTRAN traffic. The root cause was traced to Semtech’s HLR not receiving acknowledgment for the InvInsertSubscriberData message from Sparkle, resulting in HLR timeouts and subsequent system failure errors after a 14second delay. Current Status: Issue has been fixed. Root cause & remedy action awaited from Sparkle. Sparkle Ticket Number: CSFA00618981 Keynotes as Follows: Customer : Qotto SAS, ChargePoint, Networth etc Traffic Procedure: UL/ULGPRS Traffic type: SIGTRAN Roaming Partner: SPARKLE Device Type: ESSENTIALS1 VPLMN: TMobile USA, Inc , MTN Benin, Orange France, Bouygues Telecom ect Start Time: 10:24 UTC End Time: 12:30 UTC Duration: 2 hours 06 Minutes Sample ICCID: 8933240100001575322 Device Behaviour: GNOC took an example for a sample device with ICCID 8933240100001575322. GNOC observed that the device was sending multiple SAI messages, which were successful, but it was unable to start a session. . However, the device could not update location (UL), as it was throwing a system failure issue at that instance. GNOC observed that device could start a session with 4G network. Hence it seems devices switched traffic from 2G/3G to 4G. Customer Impact: No issues with existing connected devices, but there was a delay in new device registration. Degradation in Data/SMS/Voice/USSD was observed. Post Fix Snaps: Status Page Timeline (in UTC): 10:44 → Initial Status page Sent 12:46 → Identified Status page sent 13:46 → Monitoring Status page sent 15:45 → Resolved Status Page Sent

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: Per Sparkle, they identified an issue with a Carrier that managed multiple destinations. The carrier advertised routes on a single demarcation point for the signaling part without advertising the same routes on the backup. We notified the carrier, who restored the problem, and the service was restored. From:  Contact Center < contactcenter.tis@tisparkle.com > Sent:  Thursday, December 12, 2024 12:08 PM To:  Global NOC < globalnoc@semtech.com > Subject:  Case CSFA-00618981 Your Ref: GNOC-6829   Caution: This email originated outside of Semtech. Dear Customer, We identified a down with a Carrier that managed multiple destinations, the carrier advertised routes on a single demarcation point for the signaling part without advertising the same routes on the back up. We notified the carrier who restored the problem and the service was restored. We kindly ask you to check and confirm the TT closure. The TT is  set to an automatic closure If no reply is received from your side within the next 06 hours. Best Regards, We would like to update you on the Trouble Ticket Number CSFA-00618981 Status Update. Update Description: Symptom Category Found by Competence Center: Interruption Status: Resolved Fault Localization: Tis Network Fault Localization Description: TIS NETWORK/ORS PLATFORM/SW Your Reference: GNOC-6829 Account: SIERRA WIRELESS FRANCE SAS Product: Mobile Service D/T Ticket Open (UTC): 2024-12-12 12:11:02Z D/T Service Fault Found (UTC) :2024-12-12 12:07:38Z D/T Fault Clearing (UTC) :2024-12-12 12:30:00Z Real Time Flag :On Customer's Declared Trouble Ticket Details: __________________________________________________________ Customer's Symptom Category: Interruption Customer's Symptom Sub Category: Unable to Perform GSM LU Customer's Severity : Major Customer's Trouble Description : Hello, KPIs not yet back to normal.. Semtech has been experiencing an increase in system failure errors from Sparkle on SIGTRAN traffic since 10:10 UTC on 12 Dec. Would you kindly conduct a priority investigation and provide an update regarding the system failure's root cause? We are treating this issue in a Major Category. Based on an investigation using our inter tools, We found that it was caused by “ Semtech’s HLR not getting acknowledgment of the Inv_InsertSubscriberData message from Sparkle, and after 14 seconds, it has been sending HLR time out with a System failure error.” It indicates that you had the problem. Please refer to the Sample IMSI & PCAP that is attached. IMSI à 222013430089813 DPC: OPC: Thank you for contacting Telecom Italia Sparkle Contact Center. This is an automatic email, please do not reply. In case of need, please send your messages to  contactcenter.tis@tisparkle.com Telecom Italia Sparkle Contact Center Phone: +39 06 321000 Fax: +39 06 5274 4560 email:  contactcenter.tis@tisparkle.com web:  http://www.tisparkle.com

summary: Alert: Semtech - Sigtran STP-I FKT Traffic Processing Issue
description: Executive Summary STPI link Frankfurt was not handling traffic correctly. Restart was perform to mitigate the issue. Existing sessions were not impacted. Unable to establish new session. Detailed Summary Since 20250805 00:21 UTC, GNOC started to receive and observe an increase of traffic for multiple customer on Sigtran. Some STP link traffics are going down and shifting to other link. After checking kibana, a increase of System Failure is observed. GNOC believe this is some kind of signaling storm. SME has been contacted to assess on this matter. Meanwhile, StatusPage has been sent out. At 20250805 00:46 UTC, GNOC started to observe improvement. SME stated that he restarted STPI link Frankfurt due to it was not handling traffic correctly for unknown reason. summary: Sparkle Network Router Incident
description: GNOC RCA: GNOC6820: 12112024 Major Sparkle Network Router Incident Final RCA Executive Summary: On December 11, 2024, at 10:30 AM (Rome time), Sparkle experienced a hardware fault on the IP Seabone router in FRK, which led to network degradation. This fault resulted in an increase in Sigtran and Diameter traffic for customers using Essential1 SIMs. The issue was caused by an abnormal behavior between the main and backup components at the IP router logic level, preventing proper failover and routing at the network level. In response, Sparkle’s IP Competence team collaborated with the router vendor to restore IP router reachability within the backbone. Additionally, several application servers were restarted to clear the traffic queue. After these corrective actions, traffic levels returned to normal. Incident Overview: On December 11, 2024, at 09:33 UTC, GNOC received P2 & P3 alerts indicating a system failure and RNA issue toward Sparkle. Initial investigations, using OpenSearch and Viavi tools, identified elevated spikes in system failure affecting multiple VPLMNs for both Diameter and SIGTRAN traffic. The root cause was traced to Semtech’s HLR not receiving acknowledgment for the InvInsertSubscriberData message from Sparkle, resulting in HLR timeouts and subsequent system failure errors after a 14second delay. Current Status: Sparkle has resolved the issue and GNOC is currently monitoring. Impact Analysis: Essentials1 devices experienced the loss of current data sessions, although some sessions transitioned to other partners for Advanced Devices. Customers might have observed latency during network attachment or data session reestablishment. Root Cause: The hardware fault on the IP seabone router in FRK (10.30 Rome time) generated an abnormal behavior between the main and the backup part at the IP router logic level that was not given the possibility of switching at the routing level. Corrective Action: Sparkle’s IP Competence team worked with the Vendor's router

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: Greetings Team, Sparkle’s vendor ETR 2-4 hours. At 2024-12-11 15:58 UTC I spoke with Sparkle NOC Manager, Marco Marciante, and asked him if there was additional assistance needed from Semtech to expedite resolution efforts.  Marco mentioned the issue is isolated to Sparkle’s FKR (Frankfurt) router due to a hardware anomaly and there was nothing Semtech could do to resolve.  Sparkle currently has its vendor working on the FKR router issue and has given an ETR of 2–4 hours.   Other than that, he stated that there was not much more that he could provide outside their periodic incident updates.
