comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: Greetings Team, At 2024-12-11 21:20 UTC I spoke with Sparkle NOC Manager, Marco Marciante, following up with email and he mentioned their FKT router hardware issue was resolved.  He stated that there may be queued signaling on the Sparkle network that needs to purge.   See the email correspondence that I delivered to Sparkle management. As discussed, although it appears improvements have been made, we are still seeing an elevated number of System Failures.  You reiterated what was communicated in the Sparkle notification updates that hardware FKT router issue was resolved.   You mentioned that there may be queued signaling that needs to be purged. Please provide a trace from your side that shows Semtech traffic, we want to see if there is network congestion observed on the Sparkle network.     Please review the captured packet (IMSI 222013167778442) trace attached and the following snapshots below:         Public IMSI  222013167778442 Private IMSI 208243090778442     SCCP PEER                                    Count 393358840744406                           11 393358840744442                           2 393358840746781                           2 393358840603704                           1 393358840603720                           1 393358840603721                           1     SCCP PEER                                    Count 393358840705703                           186,996   393358840705701                           184,127   393358840705707                           181,024   393358840705705                           141,422   393358840690516                           93,062   393358840690520                           92,618   393358840690511                           91,483   393358840690504                           91,373   393358840690524                           89,960   393358840690519                           87,039

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: Summary:  Per Sparkle, The hardware fault on the IP seabone router in FRK (10.30 Rome time) generated an abnormal behaviour between the main and the backup part at IP router logic level that not given the possibility of switch at routing level. Our IP Competence team worked with the Vendor's router and restored the IP router recheability in our backbone around 18.47 Rome time but some Partner Peering Carrier (like Ibasis) was still down. After the restore of the connettivity with these peering at 21.10 Rome time we observed that major part of the service involved recovered but ORS platform was full of queue that needed to be elaborated and drained.At 22.40 Rome time due to long time passed we decided to restart some of the application server to clear the queue and after the traffic trend turn back to normal level. We have observed the levels have returned to normal and the GNOC is monitoring for stability at this time.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Executive Summary: On December 11, 2024, Sparkle experienced a hardware fault on the IP Seabone router in FRK at 10:30 AM (Rome time), which led to network degradation. This issue caused a significant increase in Sigtran and Diameter traffic for customers using Essential1 SIMs. The root cause of the degradation was an abnormal behavior between the main and backup components at the IP router logic level, preventing proper failover and routing at the network level. Sparkle’s IP Competence team worked in close collaboration with the router vendor to restore IP router reachability within the backbone. Additionally, application servers were restarted to clear the traffic queue. Following these corrective actions, traffic levels returned to normal, and the network performance was fully restored. Key Points: Incident Time:  December 11, 2024, 10:30 AM (Rome time) Impact:  Increased Sigtran and Diameter traffic for Essential1 SIM customers. Cause:  Hardware fault on the IP Seabone router in FRK, affecting failover functionality between main and backup routers. Resolution:  Collaboration with vendor to restore router reachability, followed by a restart of application servers to clear queues. Outcome:  Traffic returned to normal after corrective actions were taken.

FYI..

summary: HC : 4G Sessions Dropped/Shifted to 2G/3G via JT. (CSI)
description: On 18th August 2025, during GNOC’s regular Health check in ZENOSS, GNOC observed that there is decrease in over all JT DRA sessions. GNOC checked ZENOSS & observed some 4G Sessions Dropped/Shifted to 2G/3G via JT. GNOC checked in OpenSearch & confirmed bot EU & NAM region’s customers are impacted. Some sessions were shift to 2G/3G from 4G (Existing Sessions), but new sessions could not be established. Hence GNOC opened this ticket, informed SMEs in SW Production channel & reached out to JT for a solution. Status page was released as required. GNOC did not RECEIVE ANY ALERTS for the event. JT/Velos Ticket → IOT41384 (Incident Ticket) IOT41429 (RCA TICKET) Current Status → KPIs have returned to normal, feedback awaited from JT. More keynotes as follows, Keynotes as Follows: Customer : Premier Wireless Solutions, Matooma, Wilson Electronics, UHS Pty Ltd (KIDDE Global Solutions) US, Networth Telecom, Synox, ALEMCA, Nextivity, STOCK, WEEZEVENT SAS Error Type: NA (Decrease in Diameter traffic) Traffic Procedure: AIR/ULR Traffic type: Diameter Roaming Partner: JT Device Type: Essentials3 VPLMN: AT&T Mobility, Orange France, TMobile USA, Inc, Verizon Wireless, Bouygues Telecom Country: USA & France Start Time: 06:06 UTC End Time: 08:42 UTC Duration: 2 Hours 36 Minutes Sample ICCID: 8933240100001199164 , 8933240100001724128 & 8933240100001272457 . Device Behaviour: GNOC took 2 sample devices with ICCID 8933240100001199164 & 8933240100001724128 & observed that the existing sessions are not impacted. It seems these devices switched RATs to move from 4G to 2G/3G technologies. However, GNOC took another sample device with ICCID 8933240100001272457 & observed the device could not start a session after 06:05 UTC. (All snaps attached below in UTC) Customer Impact: All existing sessions of customers are not impacted, as from Zenoss it is evident that some sessions around 350~400 sessions moved from 4G to 2G/3G RATs. However creation of new sessions are impacted. Snaps attached below

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: The Impact Analysis is missing. Could we please complete that? What happened after the Lost services? are these devices trying to register via another available VPLMN? Why do we have system failures? What is the exact service impact?  We can pick a few sample ICC id and can see what exactly happened.

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: I picked examples below and tried to understand the Service Impact. Example1 .8933240100003259475 The devices attempted to initiate a data session by creating a PDP context; however, the pdp was immediately deleted. As a result, the devices experienced a loss of service.         Example 2  8933240100003618912 A similar pattern we can see     Following the restoration, there was a surge in SIGTRAN traffic due to devices re-registering on the network. In New Caledonia, OPT is the sole VPLMN available under our current agreement. Thales is operating ADV devices in the same region, which only has 3G coverage. As a result, these devices experienced continuous service loss. Due to issues with the VPLMN, re-registration attempts from the devices did not resolve the connectivity problem Thales has raised a ticket: 01880072 Around 191 devices are affected.   Service Impact :  Devices are unable to start a new data session. Also the old data session was dropped.

summary: Alert: AMM Site - amm10 (CSI)
description: At 18:27 UTC on August 19, 2025, GNOC received a P1 alert that AMM10 service was not reachable. I tried to reach out to the oncall but got no answer. I called Sanjay, and he was going to call the oncall. The Alert closed at 19:05 UTC with no intervention from SME. After the P1 alert cleared, another alert triggered a P3 alert shi03d55f08b2ef8bb6a: AMM AWS Database unavailable . Per Splunk, this is also part of AMM10. I waited to see if the alert would clear, but it has not. I have called the oncall tech and am getting no answer. I have called Sanjay and left a voicemail message. I have talked to Robert, and he suggested calling John Sit. According to John, he is seeing the ports are working and devices are reporting. He also stated earlier that he saw AMM10 down, and he had an engineer reboot AMM10, so that is how it returned to service. Per John and Ague, the site is working and we can wait for Suyash to troubleshoot during his business hours. Final Status → SME found that the CPU utilisation was very high during this time window, which led to the server's abnormal behaviour. After restarting the server, it worked fine

summary: Benin (MOOV Africa/MTN Benin) Service Outage – GT Mapping Incident
description: GNOC RCA: GNOC6748: 11302024 Benin (MOOV Africa/MTN Benin) Service Outage – GT Mapping Incident Final RCA 11302024 Benin (MOOV Africa) Service Outage – GT Mapping Incident INCIDENT SUMMARY : At 20241130 00:55 UTC Benin had a nationwide change of its GT range numbering plan from an 8digit (+229 XXXXXXXXX) to a 10digit format, which includes a “01” added to its +229 01XXXXXXXX prefix range. MOOV (formerly Etisalat Benin), the roaming partner, did not notify nor update its official IR.21 documentation to roaming sponsors to allow corresponding changes in networks to support the new global title (GTs). This caused a total blackout Outage for customer, Webb Fontaine Group, L3 NEO SMEs applied RZ changes from Semtech side of the network. Escalated tickets were raised with Roaming Sponsors, Sparkle, Jersey Telecom, BICS, and Orange requesting the required changes applied at their networks. Official RFO/RCA reports have been requested from the partner carriers. Start time: 20241130 00:55 UTC End time: 20241203 10:30 UTC Duration: 3 days, 9 hours, 35 minutes Sparkle Ticket : CSFA00617727 Velos/JT IOT34178 Orange S412527490 COFI: https://issues.sierrawireless.com/browse/COFI2784 See previous summary log notes below: As of November 30, 2024, Benin changed its MSISDN format from an 8digit (+229 XXXXXXXXX) to a 10digit format that includes a “01” in front (+229 01XXXXXXXX). Consequently, roaming sponsors and partner networks (including Semtech) must make corresponding changes in their networks to support the new global title (GTs). L3 SMEs (Minh and Amine) have applied RZ changes. Tickets raised with Roaming Sponsors to request and/or validate required changes applied at their networks. At 20241202 20:14 UTC Velos engineer, Duarte Santos, provided the latest Benin IR.21 updated in the Velos portal. See below. Since 20241130 1:17 AM UTC, GNOC started receiving multiple P3+P2+Kibana alerts regarding the PS/CS location update success ratio being too low and RNA issue for the single

summary: INT: Verizon Network Service Disruption (CSI)
description: Verizon Network Service Disruption (CSI) | No Customer Reported Tickets, No Alerts Any customer on the Verizon USA network may experience SMS, Voice, Data service failures and degradation in North America. We received from reports (online) via Downdetector and SME that there is a widespread Verizon service outage in various parts of North America. According to the Downdetector site, reports increased significantly at 20250830 16:22 UTC. At 20250830 19:00 UTC Semtech network connectivity KPIs began to degrade. GNOC escalated with carrier. At 20250830 23:15 UTC Verizon Network Service Improvements observed. At 20250830 18:51 UTC L3 Engineering asked if there as a Verizon issue. Verizon ticket# NRB000011910381; WB250830992498 (WFT1497266) Current Status: (Level 1 Escalation) Verizon USA experiencing network service disturbances (Priority 1 ticket active). GNOC engaged and escalated issue with Verizon Technical Support to investigate, address, and expedite recovery efforts. Verizon confirmed they are experiencing service outages in southern eastern states including, but not limited to most of Florida, Georgia and all the way through the US east coast from Virginia to Massachusetts. VERIZON ETR: 20250831 09:00 UTC Start Time: 20250830 16:22 UTC (Down Detector) Start Time: 20250830 19:00 UTC End Time: 20250830 23:15 UTC Duration: 4 hours, 15 minutes Impacted MNO: Verizon Country: United States (North America) Affected Customer(s): Chargepoint, UHS Pty Ltd (KIDDE Global Solutions) US, Gardner Denver, Wilson Electronics, Toyota Material Handling, Premier Wireless Solutions, The Chamberlain Group Device Behavior: Any customer on the Verizon USA network may be experiencing interruptions in SMS, Voice, and Data services and connectivity. comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Greetings Team, We have received the latest update, and they have requested that share some information with them:       kindly suggest to share the information with them. Update:  Good evening, In regards to ticket NRB000011910381 the network technician working on it had some observations and questions. Based off the single line that was provided it appears to have a static IP address and had an up time of 7 hours at the time of testing. Bi directional traffic is confirmed to the device, with no network abnormalities observed.  Do we have the mobile device numbers or phone numbers of other devices that are being affected? Did all your equipment go down at the same time?   Did you push any changes or updates recently?  We also attempted to reach out with a phone call but got busy signals. May we confirm a good number to reach you and generally available times? We will continue monitoring this ticket and provide updates when possible. Thank you.   Please reply to the email without editing the subject line if you need any further assistance with the issue you called in about.   Jacob M. Wireless Enterprise Helpdesk BGCO Verizon Business Group Contact Us: 800-922-0204

summary: Orange Roaming Service Disturbance | Increase System Failures (Invers most affected)
description: At 17:04 UTC, GNOC received a P3 alert for CS System Failures. After reviewing the KPIs, we were seeing an increase in System Failures on Orange901 for several customers, but mostly on Invers. Per the call traces, we are seeing Return Result loss from the HLR. We have referred the issue to the SMEs for review. Per the SME, he believes it’s the serving network's GT. It does not look correct and we do not have any GT like that. So my assumption is something was changed on Orange901 temporarily. Orange Ticket: S411516881 (Classic Domain) K ey Notes: Start Time: 26/11/2024 16:55 UTC End Time: 26/11/2024 17:31 UTC Duration: 36 Minutes Customer: Inverse (Mostly), ETrailer, Fifteen, Traffic type: Sigtran Roaming Partner: Orange901 VPLMN: Vodafone D2 GmbH, Telefonica Germany, Telefonica Italy, etc… Device Type: Essentials 5 ICCID: 8933240100001571095 https://vpcdatainsightbwk67b5airqgiro7xetpjrq7eq.eucentral1.es.amazonaws.com/dashboards/app/dashboards#/view/22639640040[…]GES,viewMode:view)

summary: HC: Orange_901-Lost Service Incident in NAM and EU(CSI)
description: RCA: GNOC9345: Orange Network Service Incident Final RCA Executive Summary: On September 02, 2025 , Semtech identified a significant increase in Lost Service on Orange901 , resulting in devices being unable to create new data sessions and drop the old session as well. This impacted customers using Essentials5 and Advanced SIMs , who may have experienced session drops and failures in establishing Data, SMS, and Voice services . Note: GNOC did not receive any alert related to this incident. Summary: Since Sep 02, 2025, 06:26 UTC , during a routine health check, GNOC observed a significant increase in lost service. Upon investigation in OpenSearch, it was observed that sessions were stopped due to Lost Service towards Orange901 , affecting multiple VPLMNs and customers. GNOC also identified that a planned maintenance activity ( GNOC9292: Orange — Operation with Risk N° T508205351 ) was ongoing at Orange, with potential network impact. The L2 SME confirmed a customer complaint related to the same issue. GNOC issued an initial status update and escalated the issue to Orange via the Orange Portal, followed by a direct call for urgent response. An email was also sent, and Orange acknowledged the issue. Current Status: Services were fully restored by 09:05 UTC on September 02, 2025. But the issue again restarted at 09:41 UTC & ended at 10:40 UTC. When the second instance started, GNOC created a Teams bridge & invited the Orange team for a call & resolution of the issue. The issue has been resolved & final RCA is pending from Orange. Orange Ticket: S509029589 COFI Ticket : COFI3529 Salesforce Ticket: 01882278 Keynotes: Customers Impacted: Invers GmbH (mostly), Toyota Material Handling, Solustop, Thales SIX GTS France – SAPHIR BAR 2023, Carrier Fire and Security B.V., Synox, ZEPLUG, CARRIER SCS, Wilson Electronics, Bambora Device AB, lxeptegisprod Traffic Procedure: Start / Stop / Interim Update Error: Lost Service Roaming Partner: Orange901 Device Types: Essentials5, Advance
searchable_text: HC: Orange_901-Lost Service Incident in NAM and EU(CSI) RCA: GNOC9345: Orange Network Service Incident Final RCA Executive Summary: On September 02, 2025 , Semtech identified a significant increase in Lost Service on Orange901 , resulting in devices being unable to create new data sessions and drop the old session as well. This impacted customers using Essentials5 and Advanced SIMs , who may have experienced session drops and failures in establishing Data, SMS, and Voice services . Note: GNOC did not receive any alert related to this incident. Summary: Since Sep 02, 2025, 06:26 UTC , during a routine health check, GNOC observed a significant increase in lost service. Upon investigation in OpenSearch, it was observed that sessions were stopped due to Lost Service towards Orange901 , affecting multiple VPLMNs and customers. GNOC also identified that a planned maintenance activity ( GNOC9292: Orange — Operation with Risk N° T508205351 ) was ongoing at Orange, with potential network impact. The L2 SME confirmed a customer complaint related to the same issue. GNOC issued an initial status update and escalated the issue to Orange via the Orange Portal, followed by a direct call for urgent response. An email was also sent, and Orange acknowledged the issue. Current Status: Services were fully restored by 09:05 UTC on September 02, 2025. But the issue again restarted at 09:41 UTC & ended at 10:40 UTC. When the second instance started, GNOC created a Teams bridge & invited the Orange team for a call & resolution of the issue. The issue has been resolved & final RCA is pending from Orange. Orange Ticket: S509029589 COFI Ticket : COFI3529 Salesforce Ticket: 01882278 Keynotes: Customers Impacted: Invers GmbH (mostly), Toyota Material Handling, Solustop, Thales SIX GTS France – SAPHIR BAR 2023, Carrier Fire and Security B.V., Synox, ZEPLUG, CARRIER SCS, Wilson Electronics, Bambora Device AB, lxeptegisprod Traffic Procedure: Start / Stop / Interim Update Error: Lost Service Roaming Partner: Orange901 Device Types: Essentials5, Advance

comments.author: Ahmad Yazeji
comments.body: Hello, Invers updated us that the issue disappeared from 09:00 until 09:40 UTC but came back a few minutes ago. The problem is that Devices repeatedly can no longer transmit or receive data within active sessions.

comments.author: Steven Lee
comments.body: Yes sir. Kibana KPI shows that the issue is back and GNOC has already re-engaged Orange for a explanation.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Greetings!! We are in communication with  Orange via email , and as per their latest update, they have carried out a  rollback . Let’s wait and monitor for improvement.     FYI…

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Greetings !! Orange STP started to increase; it looks like congestion is now increasing. We are also receiving alerts for the same. We are continuously taking follow-up with Orange via email.

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: GNOC had a call with Orange team for the resolution of the issue. As per Orange we should get an update in 30 minutes, but we already have started observing improvements.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Investigation and finding through the Sample IMSI: Sample IMSI:   8933240100002541768 / 208243091053410   OpenSearch The device initially established a session on the  LTE network . The ongoing LTE session was  terminated due to Lost Service . After termination, the device attempted to switch to  2G/3G networks , but was unable to establish a session.(Device may not be using 2G/3G Service) Subsequently, the device returned to the  LTE network  and successfully re-established the session after a short delay. These repeated session drops and reconnections are consistent with the  fluctuations in Lost Service  observed during the incident.     fyi..

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !! Orange has confirmed that the issue was because of the planned maintenance    they had (Firmware Upgrade) on M2M Servers.  For now this is the Root cause they have provided. Im order to fix the issue they rolled back the upgrade. A detailed RCA will be provided by Orange. For now GNOC is observing that lost service has returned to pre-level.   GNOC is completing this ticket.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Orange has updated that,  Hello,  Our roaming teams will contact your services to obtain more information about these service interruptions.  Regards,

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra

Then the device did usual data service after Orange_901 fixed issue at their end.  (Snap attached below) GNOC took sample  Essentials5 device with ICCID 8933240100001307420  & observed the after device got lost service at 10:03 UTC, but it could not start a session until 10:43 UTC . Then the device did usual data service after Orange_901 fixed issue at their end.  (Snap attached below)

comments.author: yjadhav

GNOC has asked Orange for an RCA , Orange replied -> Hello,   As mentioned in our last discussions.   Our ROAMING teams will contact you to find out why the maintenance on 02/09 had an impact on your side. At that point, we will decide whether or not to issue an RCA.   Thank you for your understanding.   Regards,

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: During the incident, we found advance devices were also impacted where their active data session were terminated and devices took more than 30 minutes to establish a new data session. Also we observed neither  mno  nor  mvno  was switched. Examples of Advanced4 Devices : ICCID – 8933240100003666287 We took example of device with ICCID 8933240100003666287 & observed it lost Service & could not re-establish connection during the incident. However the same device was doing successful interim update the day before.   We took example of device with ICCID 8933240100003294164 & observed it lost Service & could not re-establish connection during the incident (interim update happened after 26 minutes from last interim updates). However the same device was doing successful interim update the day before.    Examples of Advanced3 Devices : We took example of device with ICCID 8933240100003267531 & observed it lost Service & could not re-establish connection during the incident. It did sessions at 10:49 UTC. However the same device was doing successful interim update the day before.  We took example of device with ICCID 8933240100003044840 & observed it lost Service multiple times during the incident & was not having a stable session. However, the same device was doing successful interim update the day before.  Affected device Counts :  Consolidated  (All Supporting snaps attached below) Essentials5 Sept 2 (Affected Timeframe) Approximate device count ~ 50745 Sept 1 (Affected Timeframe) Approximate device count ~  1661 Approximate affected device count during incident  ~  Around 49,000    ADVANCED3 Sept 2 (Affected Timeframe) Approximate device count ~ 13135 Sept 1 (Affected Timeframe) Approximate device count ~  1152 Approximate affected device count during incident  ~  Around 12,000    ADVANCED4 Sept 2 (Affected Timeframe) Approximate device count ~  83490 Sept 1 (Affected Timeframe) Approximate device count ~   5312 Approximate affected device count during incident  ~  Around 78,000   Affected device counts : Essentials5 Sept 2 (Affected Timeframe) Approximate device count ~ 50745 Sept 1 (Affected Timeframe) Approximate device count ~  1661 Approximate affected device count during incident  ~  Around 49,000     ADVANCED3 Sept 2 (Affected Timeframe) Approximate device count ~ 13135 Sept 1 (Affected Timeframe) Approximate device count ~  1152 Approximate affected device count during incident  ~  Around 12,000   ADVANCED4 Sept 2 (Affected Timeframe) Approximate device count ~  83490 Sept 1 (Affected Timeframe) Approximate device count ~   5312 Approximate affected device count during incident  ~  Around 78,000 Active Session count dropped in ZenOS. The active data session degraded gradually from 169 K to 144 K  in 4 hours  (approximately 14% down) hence the alert was not triggered.

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: Hi   , if you remember, we discussed this case in last Weekly call with Richard. I have investigated by picking some examples and found that devices could not change/shift to MNO/MVNO. Can you help me with the reason on why and how it happened. 8933240100003666287 -- V5.2.2    Advanced4   Wilson Electronics 8933240100003044840 -- V5.2.2     Advanced3   Wilson Electronics As per the observation from Open search devices lost Service & could not re-establish data during the incident. Also, there was no MNO or MVNO switch during this time. The steering rank is 1 for TMO so the first connection should be with TMO- Orange_901, if due to some issue with Orange_901 if it choose second then it's BICS but for some reason if TMO is down it should choose best alternatives from AT&T and Verizon. However, devices never switched. 8933240100003294164 -- V5.2.2     Advanced4  Career Fire Security If there was some service degradation via Orange_901 then it should have chosen TELE2/Telenor  via BICS and HI3G via  mbqt. 8933240100003267531 -- V5.2.1       Advanced3   Synox If Orange_901 is down, it should have moved to BICS, then JT and Sparkle or would have chosen VPLMN/MNO Bouygues or Free  for awareness.

summary: System Failures due to  JT/Velos IPX interconnection link Failure
description: Summary : 20/11/2024, 23:21 UTC, GNOC received P2 & P3 alerts regarding system failure from JT. Upon investigation in Open Search and Viavi , our HLR is not getting acknowledgment of the InvInsertSubscriberData message from Jersey Telecom after 15 seconds. and has sent a System failure error. We have opened a ticket with Velos to investigate. JT/Velos Ticket: IOT33818 GNOC has received RCA from Velos. Root Cause → The root cause was the PW process (33376) to update the PGWs which resulted in the misconfiguration of the IP addresses where the GTP interfaces were not being correctly advertised in the global GRX table. The cause of the error was a new parameter switch in the Affirmed PGW that dropped the BGP advertised route. Start Time: 20241120 23:20 UTC End Time: 20241121 03:23 UTC Duration: 04 hours 03 Minutes Customers: Almost all customers Latched with JT (Corporate Systems Engineering, Premier Wireless Solutions, Webb Fontaine Group FZ LLC, Nextivity, International Centre for Tropical, UTC Fire & Security B.V, UTC Fire & Security America) Traffic Procedure: UL/ULGPRS, CL/CLR/CLRS6a Traffic type: Sigtran & Diameter Roaming Partner: JT Device Type: Essentials 3, Advanced(Minor) Affected VPLMN: For SigtranBouygues Telecom,Orange France, Telus Communications,Rogers Wireless,Bell Mobility Inc.Vodafone D2 GmbH,Etisalat Benin SA, Telekom Deutschland Gm,TMobile USA, Inc mostly but almost all VPLMN affected. For DiameterSierra Wireless France, Rogers Wireless mostly Initial Statuspage: 21/11/2024 00:04 UTC Identified Statuspage: 21/11/2024 01:12 UTC Final Status Page: 21/11/2024 10:03 UTC RCA Report from JT/Velos: During the Service Incident devices might have encountered challenges when connecting to roaming partners and initiating data sessions. Additionally, those devices already connected to the network could have faced potential disconnection. The root cause has been identified and isolated as an issue in the IoT Packet Gateway (PGW). comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: We are seeing improvement at this time.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: The Latest Update from JT: “ There is still a reduction in signalling quality on IPX interconnection links, but the service is showing improvements, and we anticipate the alarms will clear shortly.”

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: summar y: At 23:20 UTC on November 20, 2024., GNOC received multiple P2 & P3 alerts indicating an increase in system failure issues towards JT. GNOC investigated the issue in OpenSearch and observed that the system failure towards JT on Sigtran and Diameter traffic. Upon further investigation in Viavi with sample IMSI, we found that it was due to "SW’s HLR not getting acknowledgment of the Inv_InsertSubscriberData message from JT STP, and after 14 seconds, it has been sending HLR time out with a system failure error." GNOC has contacted JT through the Customer Portal and they also confirmed that Significant degradation on IPX interconnection links. GNOC sent the Status page.   Current Status:  After acknowledgment of the issue, JT Engineers were actively working on the issue on Priority, and on 2024-11-20 09:38 UTC they confirmed that the issue has been cleared but the Root cause is still awaited. GNOC sent the final resolved Status Page on at 2024-11-20 10:03 UTC and will continue to monitor the situation.   JT case ID:   IOT-33818   Important notes:   Start Time: 2024-11-20 23:20 UTC End Time:  2024-11-21 03:23 UTC Duration: 04 hours 03 Minutes Customers: Almost all customers Latched with JT (Corporate Systems Engineering, Premier Wireless Solutions, Webb Fontaine Group FZ LLC, Nextivity, International Centre for Tropical, UTC Fire & Security B.V, UTC Fire & Security America) Traffic Procedure: UL/UL_GPRS, CL/CLR/CLR_S6a Traffic type: Sigtran & Diameter Roaming Partner: JT Device Type:  Essentials 3, Advanced(Minor) Affected VPLMN: For Sigtran-Bouygues Telecom,Orange France, Telus Communications,Rogers Wireless,Bell Mobility Inc.Vodafone D2 GmbH,Etisalat Benin SA, Telekom Deutschland Gm,T-Mobile USA, Inc mostly but almost all VPLMN affected. For Diameter-Sierra Wireless France, Rogers Wireless mostly     Sample :ICCID: 8933240100002337419   Customer Impact:   No issues with existing connected devices, but there was a delay in new device registration. Degradation in Data/SMS/Voice/USSD was observed.  FYI…

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Update from Velos, Signalling and connectivity is back to pre-incident levels, pending complete investigations.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Greetings Team,   , Since the issue has been resolved and there is no reoccurrence, I am closing this ticket and will follow up with JT for the RFO.

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Initial Root Cause based on the email:  Significant degradation on JT/Velos IPX interconnection links.

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: Make sure to ask for RFO/RCA to JT before closing.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings !!! JT/Velos has provided update that →  The root cause has been identified and isolated as an issue in the IoT Packet Gateway (PGW).  The issue compromised the IPX interconnection Links.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !!! JT/Velos has provided RCA with Root Cause as  “The root cause was the PW process (33376) to update the PGWs which resulted in the misconfiguration of the IP addresses where the GTP interfaces were not being correctly advertised in the global GRX table. The cause of the error was a new parameter switch in the Affirmed PGW that dropped the BGP advertised route.”

summary: Sparkle - MTN Benin Roaming Service Degradation
description: While doing HC, GNOC observed an increase in system failures. After reviewing the KPIs, the increase started at 12:55 UTC over Sparkle for customers Qotto SAS and Webb Fontaine. GNOC has sent an email to Sparkle to investigate. No alerts were received. No SF or COFI tickets have been opened. Sparkle Ticket: CSFA00616647 Current Status: As checked in OpenSearch , System Failure for MTN Benin (SpaceTel) has reduced to Zero since 09:30 UTC. GNOC will keep monitoring the traffic. GNOC requested Sparkle for a RCA/RFO. But as per Sparkle’s statement there was no issue in their network, the issue was on MTN Benin’s network. So they will not be able to provide any RCA/RFO unless MTN Benin provides it. KeyNotes: 1. Start Time: 20 Nov 12:55 UTC 2. End Time: 28 Nov 09:30 UTC 3. Duration: 7 days 20 hours 35 minutes 4. Customer: Qotto, Webb Fontaine, Country → Benin & France 5. Traffic type: SIGTRAN 6. Roaming Partner: Sparkle/JT 7. VPLMN: MTN Benin (Spacetel) 8. Device Type: Advanced2, Advanced4 9. ICCID: 8933240100002026539 StatusPage Notifications: Initial Notification: 21/11/2024 13:06 UTC 2nd Notification 21/11/2024 14:19 UTC 3rd Notification: 21/11/2024 15:45 UTC 4th Notification: 21/11/2024 16:21 UTC Identified Notification: 21/11/2024 17:35 UTC Monitoring Notification: 21/11/2024 18:22 UTC Resolved Notification: 21/11/2024 19:42 UTC Issue with Sparkle: (OPENSEARCHLINK) GNOC took an example & observed that Swir HLR were not getting acknowledgment of the InvInsertSubscriberData message from Sparkle and after 14 seconds it has been sending HLR time out with a System failure error . This error sharply increased from 11:30 UTC on 21st Nov’2024 through Sparkle for Country Benin for VPLMN MTN Benin (SpaceTel). Issue with JT: (OPENSEARCHLINK) At the same time from 11:30 UTC System failure also observed sharply increasing for VPLMN Etisalat Benin SA (through JT) . Finding: (OpenSearchLink) Along with the original system failure issue with Sparkle which started from 20th Nov’2024

Issue with JT: (OPENSEARCHLINK) At the same time from 11:30 UTC System failure also observed sharply increasing for VPLMN Etisalat Benin SA (through JT) . Finding: (OpenSearchLink) Along with the original system failure issue with Sparkle which started from 20th Nov’2024

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: After sending an email to the carrier for an update and escalation request, I spoke with Luca, Sparkle engineer, with the Contact Center, at 2024-11-24 22:05 UTC.  He confirmed that the Case CSFA-00616647 investigation with the roaming partner was already at a 3rd level escalation.  At my request, he raised the escalation to the final 4th level. See snapshots of portions of the email correspondence below: From:  Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com >  Sent:  Sunday, November 24, 2024 5:05 PM To:  Marciante Marco < marco.marciante@tisparkle.com >; Sparkle Contact Center < SparkleContactCenter@tisparkle.com >; Forgione Vincenzo < vincenzo.forgione@tisparkle.com >; Paoletti Manuele (Guest) < manuele.paoletti@guest.telecomitalia.it > Cc:  Service Platform < serviceplatform@tisparkle.com >; Noc Mobile < noc_mobile@tisparkle.com >; Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com >; Asim Khamari < akhamari@semtech.com >; Rashied Lataillade < rlataillade@semtech.com >;  antonio.tuccillo@tisparkle.com Subject:  RE: [EXT] Case CSFA-00616647 Your Ref:   ++ Greetings Marco / Sparkle colleagues,   This regional (Benin) incident is ongoing… Since 2024-11-20 13:00 UTC we have been experiencing this elevated level of Sigtran (34) System Failures from Sparkle via visited network, MTN Benin.  Please check your GT Configuration tables.  It was mentioned earlier that Sparkle is not receiving responses from MTN Benin, was this issue escalated with the RP?   Please advise. Any issues with your SCCP provider? Are your GT configuration tables correct? What escalation level is this issue raised to with MTN Benin?   The following GT addresses for VLRs,  393358840609505  and  393358840609504 , seemed to be involved in this incident.  Please see fresh traces attached and snapshots below.  Semtech’s HLR is not getting acknowledgment of the Inv_InsertSubscriberData message from Sparkle, and after 14 seconds, it has been sending HLR time outs with System Failure errors.   See the following IMSIs and VLRs referenced in the traces attached: IMSIs                                  VLRs 208244530603830             393358840609504             208244530605976             393358840609504 208243095175333             393358840609505 208243094584399             393358840609505 From:  Sparkle Contact Center < SparkleContactCenter@tisparkle.com >  Sent:  Sunday, November 24, 2024 5:09 PM To:  Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com >; Marciante Marco < marco.marciante@tisparkle.com >; Forgione Vincenzo < vincenzo.forgione@tisparkle.com >; Paoletti Manuele (Guest) < manuele.paoletti@guest.telecomitalia.it > Cc:  Service Platform < serviceplatform@tisparkle.com >; Noc Mobile < noc_mobile@tisparkle.com >; Asim Khamari < akhamari@semtech.com >; Rashied Lataillade < rlataillade@semtech.com >; Tuccillo Antonio < antonio.tuccillo@tisparkle.com > Subject:  Re: [EXT] Case CSFA-00616647 Your Ref:    Dear Customer,   Upon your request your TT has been escalated to our Fourth Level of Escalation management.   Our Noc is doing the best effort to have the problem fixed asap and It will be our concern keeping you updated.   We appreciate your understanding.   Best Regards, Luca     _________________________________________ Sparkle Sparkle Contact Center Ensuring the best support to our Customers Telecom Italia Sparkle S.p.A. Via di Macchia Palocco, 223 – 00125 ROMA Phone: +39 06 32 1000 Fax: +39 06 5274 4560 Linkedin  –  Twitter  -  tisparkle.com

comments.author: Ajeet Kumar
comments.body: Hi Team, According to their latest update conversation with Sparkle, they are claiming that they are continuously seeking feedback from their roaming partner. However, with our most recent trend and observation, GNOC followed up with a sparkle once more. Awaiting their response. refer to the mail snap.

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: We have further escalated this issue with our leadership team internally.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: I called and spoke with NOC Mobile Team Leader, Manuele Paoletti, today at 2024-11-25 13:20 UTC, and he stated that their team is still awaiting feedback from the roaming partner.  He mentioned that this case# CSFA-00616647 investigation remains at 4th level escalation.  Manuele reiterated that Sparkle engaged the MTN Benin commercial team in an attempt expedite recovery, however the roaming partner is not responsive. Currently (at ~15:00 UTC) GNOC Leadership is currently on a call with Sparkle NOC Mobile leadership to discuss expediting resolution steps.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: GNOC set up and concluded a Teams call with Sparkle NOC Mobile leaderhip speaking with NOC Mobile Team Leader, Manuele Paoletti, and NOC Manager Service Platform, Marco Marciante, and Marco has committed to putting forth a concerted effort to resolve this issue with MTN Benin personally.  We asked him for an ETR for end-of-business today, however Marco stated that it will more likely be tomorrow.  If necessary, they will set up a technical bridge to expedite resolution. GNOC concluded Teams call with Sparkle NOC Mobile leaderhip GNOC Manager Service Platform, Marco Marciante NOC Mobile Team Leader, Manuele Paoletti Marco committed to resolving MTN Benin issue personally (by tomorrow, 11/26) . GNOC requested ETR for today; Sparkle estimated ETR for tomorrow (11/26) GNOC requested a joint-technical bridge; Sparkle is moving forward with its own joint Sparkle-MTN Benin call.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2024-11-25 15:42 UTC NOC Mobile Team Leader, Manuele Paoletti replied to GNOC Leadership with the following email response: Dear Robert,   As for conversation with Marco, we have just reached our contacts at the Sponsor Network TIM by phone to request a technical call with the roaming partner MTN Benin as soon as possible. The commercial contacts of TIM and TIS have also been activated to speed up the request as much as possible.   In the meantime, to ensure maximum transparency, I share with you the feedback from the MTN Benin carrier BICS which confirmed the problem coming from the operator who does not provide a response to the ISDs.     We will share updates as soon as possible.   Thank you for your patience and cooperation.    Best Regards, Manuele Paoletti _________________________________________ Sparkle   NOC Mobile   Team Leader Network Operations Service Platform Telecom Italia Sparkle S.p.A. Via di Macchia Palocco, 223 – 00125 ROMA +39 06 52744821

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: I tried to take  a feed back from the Sparkle team. via email. Waiting for their reply.

comments.author: Ajeet Kumar
comments.body: Hello Team , Manuele Paoletti from Sparkle has confirmed that they are continuously contacting the Roaming Partner to obtain a response, but they have not received any response. They have escalated the matter to the highest level. Meanwhile, GNOC did follow-up via five9 . An executive from Sparkle NOC assured me that the matter has already been escalated and that the next update will be communicated via mail. no further update on it.

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: Hi    , as discussed Sparkle is not accepting the issue at their end. Asking me to check the devices whether it is working or not.  The devices are getting registered via other roaming partner in MTN Benin(2G/3G), however the problem is only with Sparkle with MTN Benin(2G/3G). Can you also double check. Meantime I have asked them to share the traces from their side.

comments.author: Sébastien Larue
comments.body: have you tried a sigos test, this would exclude any device issue?

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: Hi   , not yet.

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: Hi    we have performed SIGOS test with the scenario. The results are as expected. Via  Sparkle devices LU are failed in 2G/3G however success in 4G. MTN Benin is rejecting.   Similarly with JT it is successfully registered in 2G/3G. I am attaching all scenarios PCAP trace.         I took help of Ashay to conduct these tests. FYI.

comments.author: Sébastien Larue
comments.body: Hi   this test shows that there is no device issue as claimed by Sparkle, MNO is rejecting the UL.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !!! As the leadership team is engaged with Sparkle for a fix, just posting an update here, that the issue still continues.  OpenSearch_Link

However the Teams application showed that the meeting was actually scheduled for 15:30 UTC / 16:30 CET (UTC +1).  Sparkle leadership mentioned that MTN Benin is currently troubleshooting and is the bottleneck in resolving issue.  NOC Manager Service Platform, Marco Marciante, has agreed to reconvene or reschedule the joint troubleshooting session for tomorrow (11/27/2024). See snapshots below. comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Greetings Team, Since 27 November 20:48 UTC,  GNOC has been receiving intermittently P2+P3 alerts regarding the  CS Cancel Location success ratio too low /  CS System Failure,  per the  Zenoss Chart , it is hovering around the threshold. GNOC also checked the System failure in Zenoss and found that  cs_loc-update_system-failure  fluctuated below 1K. After further investigation, we found that the CS Cancel Location error was gradually increasing for the VPLMN  MTN Benin (SpaceTel) with Sparkle. OpenSearch Link:

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Greetings Team,  As checked in  OpenSearch , System Failure for MTN Benin (SpaceTel) has reduced to Zero since 09:30 UTC. GNOC will keep monitoring the traffic.   FYI..

summary: Verizon Outage reported by Broadband Access Customers
description: L2 NAM Technical Support team reported that L1 and L2/Broadband Access teams received reports of a Verizon outage. GNOC engaged with the L2 NAM Technical Support team to investigate the issue. A total peak of 424 Broadband Access circuits (devices) affected. This only affected some Broadband Access customers on Verizon Wireless. L2 Engineers only saw the problem with circuits using the regional APN, so01.vzwstatic. Smart Connectivity and ECC devices were unaffected. Start Time: 20241120 16:05 UTC End Time: 20241120 20:27 UTC Duration: 4 hours, 22 minutes LMNO: Verizon Wireless Regional APN: so01.vzwstatic Country: United States 35211 Macon GA 35208 Inland CA 35209 Raleigh NC 35177 South Bend IN Service type: Broadband Access / formerly MNS (Managed Network Services) Data service and connectivity. Customers Affected: Vonage Business, Ten4, Telesystems (Honey Baked Ham) 01838403 | Case | Salesforce Verizon ticket# NRB000011737652 Additional Log Details: At 20241120 17:54 UTC, the L2 NAM Technical Support team reported that there were 424 Broadband Access circuits offline on VZW, and there was no reason to suspect ECC to be affected, ECC customers are not using a regional APN with VZW. “We are only seeing the problem with circuits using the so01.vzwstatic regional APN” There were No iMaestro Fleet alerts received, however GNOC saw a surge in Verizon Access Failures between 20241120 16:20 20:27 UTC. As of 20241120 17:20 UTC there were no current problems reported on Downdetector: There were no detectable issues with Smart Connectivity or ECC devices:

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade

The L2 team will find out if there is an individual case raised with Verizon or if there was a master ticket with already generated with them for a known network event or service interruption.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade

The L2 engineer will reach out to Verizon to inform them of the observed recovery and will request an official RCA report. 2024-11-20 19:34 UTC:  Broadband Access circuits down to 72.

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: Per the L2 team they have requested an RCA from Verizon and they should provide something in 24hrs.

summary: CS/PS system failure towards Sparkle observed
description: Summary: Since 20241106 15:52 UTC GNOC observed multiple P2 & P3 alerts indicating an increase in system failure issues towards Sparkle. GNOC investigated the issue in OpenSearch and observed that the system failure towards Sparkle on SIGTRAN/Radius traffic. Upon further investigation in Viavi with sample IMSI, we found that it was due to " SW’s HLR not getting acknowledgment of the InvInsertSubscriberData message from Sparkle STP, and after 14 seconds, it has been sending HLR time out with a system failure error ." GNOC has contacted Sparkle through email . Current Status : Issue Still persists. SPARKLE feedback awaited. Sparkle case ID : CSFA00615003 Important notes: Start Time: 15:43 UTC, 06 Nov’2024 End Time: 18:30 UTC, 06 Nov’2024 Duration : 2 Hours 47 Minutes Customers: Almost all customers Latched with Sparkle (ChargePoint Inc, Networth Telecom, Matooma, Franke & Werner GmbH, Nextivity, QOTTO SAS, Synox, Solustop, Connected Cycle Traffic Procedure: UL/ULGPRS Traffic type: SIGTRAN & Radius Roaming Partner: Sparkle Device Type: Essentials 1, Essentials 3, Advanced & Lite Affected VPLMNs: Almost all VPLMN TMobile USA, Telia Sverige AB, , Bouygues Telecom, Orange France, Telia Sverige AB,Telefónica Germany Gm, Rogers Wireless, Telekom Deutschland GmbH, etc. Sample Device : doc IMSI: 208243094755977 ICCID : 8933240100002645173 MSISDN: 337000023927929 Public IMSI: 222013430155977 StatusPage Timeline: (in UTC) 16:11 – Initial Status Page sent 17:30 Investigation Status Page sent 19:00 Monitoring Status Page sent 21:01 Resolved Status Page sent Device Behavior : GNOC took an example for a sample device with ICCID 8933240100002645173. GNOC observed that the device was sending multiple SAI & AIR messages, which were successful, but it was unable to start a session. . However, the device could not update location (UL), as it was throwing a system failure issue at that instance. GNOC did not observe any increase in diameter traffic; hence, devices did not switch RAT but

summary: AirVantage DB1 Provisioning Degradation + SMS and USSD failures
description: GNOC RCA: GNOC6543: 11042024 DB1 Database Incident AV Provisioning, SMS and USSD Service Outage Final RCA Executive Summary Issue on DB1 (Storage full) and SMPP account with mbqt API can't send SMS and USSD. Customers may have experienced delays in provisioning operations and failures in SMS and USSD services (Essential 1, Essential 2, Essential 3, Essential 4, Essential 5, and Advance). Total Outage Time 4 hours 38 minutes (278 minutes) Start Time 20241104 08:27 UTC End Time 20241104 13:05 UTC Duration 4 hours 38 minutes Customer Complaint MYOTIS, Matooma, MyAngel, Synox Related COFI Ticket COFI2717 , COFI2718 SFDC → 01835547, 01835527, 01835525, 01835521, 01835514, 01835520, 01835523, 01835524 Initial StatusPage (Investigating) 20241104 10:40 UTC (Degraded Performance) Second StatusPage (Investigating) 20241104 11:21 UTC (Partial Outage) Third StatusPage (Identified) 20241104 12:10 UTC (Partial Outage) Fourth StatusPage (Monitoring) 20241104 13:12 UTC Fifth StatusPage (Monitoring) – 20241104 13:51 UTC Sixth StatusPage (Resolved) – 20241104 14:46 UTC NOTE → GNOC did not receive any opsGenie alerts related to this incident. Detailed Summary Since 20241104 8:27 UTC, anomaly started. No alerts were received through OpsGenie. SME later informed that there is a issue on DB1 and SMPP account with mbqt API can't send SMS and USSD. SME has extended storage capacity and also performed a restart of db1 vty. After the restart, Loic waited for Mikes go ahead notice before performing the same action on FKT. Mike also engaged DataBene for additional support to understand the issue. Upon further confirmation with DataBene, Loic performed the same actions for FKT. Mike later confirmed with DataBene that everything is back up running and statuspage was sent out to notify customer. Impact: During the incident customers may have experienced delays in provisioning operations and failures in SMS and USSD services. SMS Error code 4 is another sign that SMS is not being delivered. Customers may have experienced delays in provisioning operations and failures in SMS and USSD services (Essential 1, Essential 2, Essential 3, Essential 4, Essential 5, and Advance). Total Outage Time 4 hours 38 minutes (278 minutes) Start Time 20241104 08:27 UTC End Time 20241104 13:05 UTC Duration 4 hours 38 minutes Customer Complaint MYOTIS, Matooma, MyAngel, Synox Related COFI Ticket COFI2717 , COFI2718 SFDC → 01835547, 01835527, 01835525, 01835521, 01835514, 01835520, 01835523, 01835524 Initial StatusPage (Investigating) 20241104 10:40 UTC (Degraded Performance) Second StatusPage (Investigating) 20241104 11:21 UTC (Partial Outage) Third StatusPage (Identified) 20241104 12:10 UTC (Partial Outage) Fourth StatusPage (Monitoring) 20241104 13:12 UTC Fifth StatusPage (Monitoring) – 20241104 13:51 UTC Sixth StatusPage (Resolved) – 20241104 14:46 UTC NOTE → GNOC did not receive any opsGenie alerts related to this incident. Detailed Summary Since 20241104 8:27 UTC, anomaly started. No alerts were received through OpsGenie. SME later informed that there is a issue on DB1 and SMPP account with mbqt API can't send SMS and USSD. SME has extended storage capacity and also performed a restart of db1 vty. After the restart, Loic waited for Mikes go ahead notice before performing the same action on FKT. Mike also engaged DataBene for additional support to understand the issue. Upon further confirmation with DataBene, Loic performed the same actions for FKT. Mike later confirmed with DataBene that everything is back up running and statuspage was sent out to notify customer. Impact: During the incident customers may have experienced delays in provisioning operations and failures in SMS and USSD services. SMS Error code 4 is another sign that SMS is not being delivered. summary: Alert: Connectivity  issue on Telia Sverige AB, Sweden(CSI)
description: RCA : GNOC9603: Telia MNO Sweden Incident Final RCA Executive Summary : On September 22, 2025, Telia Sverige AB (240 01) experienced a NFVI/Cloud outage between 11:15–11:53 UTC, leading to a signaling storm and platform overload. This impacted Essentials1 (7,770 devices), Advanced2 (9,366 devices), and Advanced4 (7,383 devices), along with Sparkleconnected Advanced devices. Major customers affected included AddSecure Group Services AB, Eskilstuna Strängnäs Energi, and Carrier Fire and Security B.V. While Advanced2 and Advanced4 devices recovered by switching IMSIs to alternate networks (Tele2, Hi3G), Essentials1 and Sparkleconnected Advanced2 devices faced extended service loss. Telia disconnected their FW (HSS NSlookup), and by 16:26 UTC, initial improvements were observed. Our SMEs unblocked Telia Sverige AB on September 23, 2025, 07:54 UTC, leading to further recovery and restoration of normal KPIs with no further recurrence. Incident Summary: On September 22, 2025, at 11:17 UTC , GNOC started receiving multiple P3 and P2 alerts indicating a signaling issue with multiple roaming partners. GNOC performed the initial investigation and identified an issue in Sweden affecting multiple roaming partners. After a detailed investigation, it was confirmed that VPLMN Telia Sverige AB was experiencing an issue where connected devices were unable to start sessions after dropping the session due to “Lost Service”. GNOC informed in the SW Production channel , including Maingate SME (since P2 alerts indicated session deductions for Maingate). Maingate SME confirmed that Telia nodes were impacted , but traffic was being handled by other nodes. Out of 8K dropped sessions from Telia, 6K sessions had already recovered , and there was no major impact on Maingate customers . For Smart Connectivity customers using Essentials and Advanced devices , session drops occurred due to service lost in VPLMN Telia Sverige AB. L2 SME confirmed that Invers was also impacted, and a ticket was rais

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Incident Investigation Report Incident:  Telia Sweden – Essentials1 & Advanced Devices Date:  September 22, 2025 Investigation & Findings Essentials1 Devices GNOC investigated the issue observed on  Essentials1 devices in Sweden . Below are the key observations: Devices were initially connected through  TELIA SONERA MOBILE NETWORKS AB (240 01) . After losing the ongoing session, devices were  unable to establish new sessions  and continuously retried attaching to the network (240 01). After sending the  Create PDP Request , devices received  Lost Service immediately . Following the  SME block on 240 01 , devices attempted to switch  RATs  but still could not establish sessions, experiencing  RNA  in both  UL and ULR . During this period, devices also tried registering via  240 02 (Hi3G) , but experienced RNA as well (since  240 02 is blocked by Semtech  in Sweden for Essentials1 devices). After the issue was resolved and  TELIA SONERA MOBILE NETWORKS AB (240 01) was unblocked by SME , devices were able to successfully start sessions again. Sample IMSI:   8933240100001807878 / 208243093427526 Essentials1 Device Count: Device Count: Total Count:   7770  (SIMv 05.00, 05.01, 5.2) Major Impacted Customer:   AddSecure Group Services AB Pre Duration:  Sep 21, 2025 @ 11:10 – Sep 22, 2025 @ 07:54 UTC Post Duration:  Sep 22, 2025 @ 11:10 – Sep 23, 2025 @ 07:54 UTC Advanced2 Devices Advanced2 devices  initially latched to  TELIA SWERIGE (240 01)  and MVNO  BICS . After the issue occurred, these devices experienced  Lost Service via TELIA SWERIGE (240 01) . Unlike Essentials1, the  Advanced2 devices automatically switched IMSI profiles  and successfully latched with: Hi3G Access AB (240 02) TELE2 SWERIGE AB (240 07) Advanced Device Count: Device Count: Total Count:   9366  (SIMv 4.4.1) Major Impacted Customer:   Eskilstuna Strängnäs Energi Pre Duration:  Sep 21, 2025 @ 11:10 – Sep 22, 2025 @ 07:54 UTC Post Duration:  Sep 22, 2025 @ 11:10 – Sep 23, 2025 @ 07:54 UTC Advanced4 Devices Initially latched to  TELIA SWERIGE (240 01) . Devices experienced Lost Service but switched IMSI successfully to alternate MNOs. Device Count: Total Count:   7383  (SIMv 5.2) Major Impacted Customer:   Carrier Fire and Security B.V. Pre Duration:  Sep 21, 2025 @ 11:10 – Sep 22, 2025 @ 07:54 UTC Post Duration:  Sep 22, 2025 @ 11:10 – Sep 23, 2025 @ 07:54 UTC Advanced Devices with Sparkle GNOC analyzed a  sample SIM (IMSI: 8933240100001435914) . The device was initially connected via  TELIA SONERA MOBILE NETWORKS AB (240 01) . During the issue, the device was  unable to switch to another MNO or roaming partner  and completely stopped the session. Once the issue was resolved, the device successfully resumed sessions again via  240 01 . Sample IMSI: 8933240100001807878/8933240100001807878-- Essentials1 Devices--->>Could not able to make a session until the issue was resolved. 8933240100001435914--->>>Advanced2 with Sparkle IMSI--->>> Could not able to make a session until the issue was resolved. For the Other Advanced IMSI, the devices could switch the MNVO and VPLMN and maintain the session. Below is the screenshot of devcies moved from BICS to MBQT and from  Telia Sverige AB to other VPLMN  Tele2 Sverige AB , Telenor Sverige AB and  Hi3G Access AB Conclusion The incident was caused by session failures on  TELIA SONERA MOBILE NETWORKS AB (240 01) . Essentials1 devices  could not establish sessions and were impacted until SME unblocked 240 01. Advanced2 devices  showed resilience by switching IMSI profiles to alternative MNOs ( 240 02, 240 07 ) and maintained service continuity. Advanced devices with Sparkle  were impacted similarly to Essentials1 and could not switch MNOs, only resuming once 240 01 was restored. RNA Impact Analysis – Sweden Essentials1:  Impacted most severely due to only Telia (240 01) and Hi3G (240 02) agreements; with 240 02 blocked, fallback was impossible. Advanced2 & Advanced4:  More resilient; switched IMSIs to alternative MNOs such as Tele2 and Hi3G, reducing impact. Sparkle Advanced Devices:  Impacted like Essentials1 since fallback networks were blocked/unavailable.   Current Coverage Analysis – Semtech (Sweden) Advanced SIM v4.4.1   Roaming Sponsor TELIA SWERIGE (240 01) TELE2 SWERIGE AB (240 07)  Hi3G Access AB  (240 02) Telenor Sverige AB (240 08) Sparkle ✕ ✕           (Ranking -1) ✕ ✕           (Ranking -1) Orange ✕ ✕ ✕ ✕ BICS ✕ ✓            (Ranking -2) ✕ ✓            (Ranking -2) JT ✕ ✓            (Ranking -3) ✓            (Ranking -3)        MBQT ✕ ✓              Advanced SIM v5.0:   Roaming Sponsor TELIA SWERIGE (240 01) TELE2 SWERIGE AB (240 07)  Hi3G Access AB  (240 02) Telenor Sverige AB (240 08) Sparkle ✕ ✕           (Ranking -1) ✕ ✕           (Ranking -1) Orange ✕ ✕ ✕ ✕ BICS ✕ ✓            (Ranking -2) ✕ ✓            (Ranking -2) JT ✕ Data not found ✓            (Ranking -3)        MBQT ✕ ✓ ✓ Data not found     For Essentials1:   Product version Network mcc mnc Roaming Sponsors Available sponsors rank sms voice volte 2G 3G 4G Essentials 1 HI3G ACCESS AB SWEDEN 240 2 Sparkle TRUE (Blocked by Semtech) 1 No No Yes Yes Yes Yes Essentials 1 TELIA SONERA MOBILE NETWORKS AB 240 1 Sparkle TRUE 1 Yes Yes Yes Yes Yes Yes     Investigation and Findings: Essentials1 devices (majorly impacted) had roaming agreements with 24001 (Telia Sonera) and 24002 (Hi3G Access). However, 24002 was blocked by Semtech prior to the incident. When 24001 experienced issues, devices were unable to switch to an alternate MNO, continuously receiving RNA attempts through non-agreement networks like Hi3G Access AB and Tele2 Sverige AB (in Diameter and Sigtran). Even after SME blocked 24001, devices still observed RNA from 24001 and 24002 (blocked earlier). Only after SME reopened 24001, devices resumed sessions and registrations.   For Advanced Devices ·   BICS Advanced Devices  had roaming agreements with  Tele2 Sverige AB  and  Telenor Sverige AB . ·  After  Telia outage , these devices successfully switched to  Tele2  and  Telenor , ensuring better continuity. ·  For  Advanced Devices via Sparkle , all available MNOs in Sweden were either  blocked  or had  no coverage . As  Telia Sverige AB  was blocked from HLR, RNA was expected. Devices attempted fallback to  Hi3G  and  Telenor , but due to lack of Sparkle coverage, they again experienced RNA.   Conclusion Root Cause:  Outage on  Telia Sverige AB (240 01)  NFVI/Cloud platform caused session failures and RNA. Essentials1 Devices:  Heavily impacted, unable to switch MNOs, only recovered once 240 01 was reopened. Advanced2 Devices:  Impacted (9366 devices, major customer Eskilstuna Strängnäs Energi) but switched IMSI to maintain partial continuity. Advanced4 Devices:  Impacted (7383 devices, major customer Carrier Fire and Security B.V.) but recovered via IMSI switch. Essentials1 Devices:  Impacted (7770 devices, major customer AddSecure Group Services AB) until SME reopened Telia. Sparkle Advanced Devices:  Unable to switch → full session stop until Telia recovered.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: @14:02 UTC Sparkle (CSFA-00651167) did transfer over RCA from Telia Sweden for Monday, 9/22 Incident, which Paoletti Manuele ( manuele.paoletti@guest.tisparkle.com ) sent to us: We had outage(human mistake person did incorrect work) in Sweden with Site for  NFVI / Cloud  at 11:15-11:53 , (graphs shows 13:15 UTC). Then signalling storm , IOT/DCP imsi range 2382087* platform had overload situation until few hour ago.. Disconnect FW (HSS NSlookup) From approximal 16:26 then improvement is seen. (From 16:26 outbound Data started improving )

summary: CS-system failure towards Sparkle observed.
description: summary: At 12:21 UTC on November 1st, 2024., we observed multiple P2 & P3 alerts indicating an increase in system failure issues towards Sparkle. GNOC investigated the issue in OpenSearch and observed that the system failure towards Sparkle on Sigtran traffic. Upon further investigation in Viavi with sample IMSI, we found that it was due to " SW’s HLR not getting acknowledgment of the InvInsertSubscriberData message from Sparkle STP, and after 14 seconds, it has been sending HLR time out with a system failure error ." GNOC has contacted Sparkle through email and five9. Current Status : After acknowledging the mail, Sparkle's NOC responded to us, saying they are still working on the issue. GNOC has asked for this problem to be escalated. The issue was finally fixed at 17:50 UTC. Sparkle case ID : CSFA00614457 Important notes: Start Time: 12:15 UTC, 1st Nov' 2024 End Time: 17:50 UTC, 1st Nov' 2024 Duration : 5 hours 35 Minutes Customers: Almost all customers Latched with Sparkle (ChargePoint Inc, Networth Telecom, Matooma, Franke & Werner GmbH, Nextivity, QOTTO SAS, Synox, Solustop, Connected Cycle Traffic Procedure: UL/ULGPRS , CL Traffic type: SIGTRAN & Diameter Roaming Partner: Sparkle Device Type: Essentials 1, Essentials 3, Advanced & Lite Affected VPLMNs: Almost all VPLMN TMobile USA, Telia Sverige AB, , Bouygues Telecom, Orange France, Telia Sverige AB,Telefónica Germany Gm, Rogers Wireless, Telekom Deutschland GmbH, etc. Sample : Public IMSI: 222013430000172 ICCID : 8933240100002442586 MSISDN: 337000020800333 Internal IMSI : 208243094600172 Statuspage : The first notification of the status page was sent at 12:55 UTC on November 1, 2024. Under investigation, a second status page notification was sent at 14:30 UTC on November 1, 2024. Still investigating status page notification sent at 16:07 UTC on November 1, 2024 Identified status page notification sent at 18:06 UTC on November 1, 2024 Monitoring status page notification sent at 19:03 UTC on November 1, 2024

comments.author: Ajeet Kumar
comments.body: Hi Team, kindly refer to the following observation about the impact on customers and device behavior. OpenSearch  :  Device Behavior : GNOC took an example for a sample device with ICCID 8933240100002395589. GNOC observed that the device was sending multiple SAI & AIR messages, which were successful, but it was unable to start a session. . However, the device could not update location (UL), as it was throwing a system failure issue at that instance. GNOC did not observe any increase in diameter traffic; hence, devices did not switch RAT but they switched their VPLMN. It seems there was some temporary link degradation from Sparkle or some congestion happened in STP Links. Customer Impact :  No issues with existing connected devices, but there was a delay in new device registration. Degradation in Data/SMS/Voice/USSD was observed.

summary: AV issue : EU AirVantage Display and Alerting Degradation
description: Summary : The AV team notified GNOC via Slack on 20241029 at 9:30 UTC that they were observing a decrease in traffic from the MBQT Radius, upstream AirVantage/avusage. GNOC contacted the Radius/Network team for additional verification. The CPU/memory pattern has elevated but within normal ranges, according to the L3 SME of Radius. Per the AV team, they don't have any consumption latency. All messages ready in the queue are ingested directly. Further analysis is ongoing to determine the root cause. Note : GNOC did not receive any alerts related to this issue. Key Notes : Start time: 20241028 at 05:30 UTC End Time: 20241029 at 10:50 UTC Duration: 29 hours 20 min Customer Reported Issue: NA Impacted Region: EU region Impacted Customer: EU Region Impacted services: API, alerts, cloud connectors. Impact : Affected: Delays in data push for connectivity usages and alerts based on connectivity data. Unaffected: Provisioning operations, traffic, and device data management remain fully operational. Current Status: The issue is resolved but SME is waiting for the reconciliation. Status page : Initial Statuspage notification has been sent on 20241029, 10:10 UTC. Monitoring Status page: 20241029, 12:58 UTC. Timeline in 24 Hours (All Time in UTC): 20241029 09:30 → The AV team communicated the problem to GNOC via Slack. 09:35 → GNOC Started the incident management process (Opened JSM Incident Ticket) 09:40 → GNOC engaged the L3 team ( AV team ). 09:42 → L3 (Radius / Network team) has acknowledged the Issue & started an investigation. 09:50 → Dedicated Slack Channel & Teams Bridge was opened to discuss the issue. 10:10 → Initial Status page Notification was sent with impact clarification. 10:28 → AV SME has confirmed the severity of the incident. 10:38 → L3 SME of radius confirmed that he saw the lag increasing just like the last incident. 12:42 → L3 SME Joseph confirmed that the traffic has come back but has not been reconciled yet, he also confirmed that there is realtime traffic

summary: ALERT:  Jersey Telecom LTE Network Degradation (primarily in North America) (CSI)
description: GNOC RCA: GNOC9798: 10092025 Jersey Telecom LTE Network Congestion Incident Final RCA Jersey Telecom LTE Network Degradation (primarily in North America) | No Customer Reported Cases Decrease Jersey Telecom LTE Network Connectivity JT’s LTE traffic dropped 50% Impact: Essentials 3 customers may have experienced some issues (delays or intermittent failures) establishing LTE Data services and network connectivity primarily in the North America market. Approximately a total of 9.8K LTE Data Sessions were dropped. Most existing Data Sessions were unaffected. Cause: One of JT’s customers experienced failures on their primary IMSI provider causing congestion overloading JT’s IPX links. Velos (JT) ticket# IOT42932 Final Update: Services Restored. Jersey Telecom (JT) had experienced network degradation in LTE Network Service primarily in North America. JT experienced a Device Connectivity Issue due to congestion on one of its partner’s core network. The Incident was raised to a Level 3 Escalation. An official Velos (JT) RCA will be requested. GNOC engaged the partner via the Velos IoT Support team pushing the carrier to address and resolve. At 20251009 15:38 UTC there was a significant drop in Jersey Telecom LTE Network Traffic and Connectivity. At least half of the normal traffic levels are being observed. GNOC engaged the carrier Velos IoT Support (Jersey Telecom) to investigate the possible issue or unexpected network changes on its network. Start Time: 20251009 15:38 UTC End Time: 20251009 22:05 UTC Duration: 6 hours, 27 minutes RCA Statsu: Tue 10/14/2025 19:22 UTC – Charles.Austin@velosiot.com advsd they are still working with their partners to nail down the final RCA and committed to sharing one with us as soon as they finalize it. Impacted Customer(s): Connected Cycle, Premier Wireless Solutions, NextCentury Submetering Systems, LLCCorporate Systems Engineering Connectivity, Premier Wireless Solutions, UHS Pty Ltd (KIDDE Global Solutions) US, Wilson Electronics ICCID

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2025-10-09 16:54 I spoke with Saja with Velos IoT Support following an email correspondence requesting a carrier investigation with GNOC’s findings.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: Service Restored – (GNOC-9798) Jersey Telecom LTE Network Degradation (primarily in North America)  Decrease Jersey Telecom LTE Network Connectivity - JT’s LTE traffic dropped 50% No Customer Reported Cases Velos (JT) ticket# IOT-42932 Semtech’s Essentials 3 customers may have experienced some issues (delays or intermittent failures) establishing LTE Data services and network connectivity primarily in the North America market.  A total of 6K LTE Data Sessions were dropped before recovering.  Most existing Data Sessions were unaffected.   Services Restored.   Jersey Telecom (JT) experienced network degradation in LTE Network Service primarily in North America.  JT experienced a Device Connectivity Issue due to congestion on one of its partner’s core networks.  The Incident was raised to a Level 3 Escalation. GNOC engaged the partner via the Velos IoT Support team pushing the carrier to address and resolve.   Start Time:  2025-10-09 15:39 UTC End Time:  2025-10-09 22:05 UTC Duration:  6 hours, 26 minutes   10/09/2025 (All Times 24Hr UTC) ·        15:38: Incident Started.   There was significant drop in Jersey Telecom LTE Data service and network connectivity. ·        15:46:  A P2 DEA alert was triggered indicating low JT diameter traffic rates.  GNOC began investigating… ·        16:03:  GNOC initiated the Incident Management Process creating an internal ticket for tracking. ·        16:16:  GNOC sent initial customer notification. ·        16:24:  GNOC notified internal teams of the JT incident ·        16:54:  GNOC engaged the Velos IoT Support to begin a Jersey Telecom carrier investigation. ·        17:00:  GNOC initiated an internal slack channel to keep teams informed and to assist in expediting any recovery efforts. ·        17:07:  JT sent an official email incident notification confirming a Device Connectivity Issue due to congestion on one of their partner carrier's core networks. ·        18:40:  Velos reported seeing services stabilize.  GNOC did not concur. ·        20:13:  GNOC continued to show Velos GTPv2 services were affected mostly in NAM. ·        20:28:  GNOC sent additional proof with snapshots of call flows, charts, and detailed captured packet traces. ·        21:42:  GNOC noticed and reported to Velos that services remained impacted, but Authentication (AIR) traffic began skyrocketing.  Escalation was requested. ·        22:00:   GNOC Leadership called into account manager, Mona Schnell, raising the carrier investigation to a Level 2 Escalation. ·        22:07:  Velos confirmed that the incident was already escalated. ·        22:07:  GNOC sent additional traces of the observed Authentication surge and a cluster of MMEs to analyze. ·        21:51:  GNOC Leadership spoke with the Velos Incident Manager, Charles Austin, he was actively engaged with JT’s Tier3 engineers.  Charles reported that one of JT’s customers experienced failures on their primary IMSI provider causing congestion overloading JT’s IPX links. ·        22:05: Incident Ended.  Service Restored. ·        23:35:  GNOC and Velos IoT Support placed the incident in a “Monitoring” state to ensure stability.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: GNOC checked the approximate number of sessions that may have moved in NAM PGWs from 4G to 2G/3G & arrived at below conclusions.  Sessions Count/Shift during the Incident :   4G LSP --  Dropped  sessions 2680 Approx 2G/3G LSP -- Increased by 200 Approx   4G MSN --  Dropped  sessions 2680 Approx 2G/3G MSN -- Increased by 200 Approx   So it seems  some sessions were lost  during the incident. However  the Device LTE Data Sessions  returned to LTE as normal level post incident.

I took a device with sample ICCID 8933240100002140924 & observed that the existing sessions were not impacted (snaps attached below)  I took a device with sample ICCID  8933240100002155073  & observed that the session got timed out & after a minor delay it started session. We may say devices almost maintained their sessions active with short disruptions.

summary: ALERT IMPROVEMENT: Create Voice Service Impairment Alert based on learnings from COFI-3516
description: This ticket will be used for tracking activities associated with creating and implementing an alert in response to lessons learned following the 08/27/2025 Voice Service Impairment incident. Voice service impairment was reported by customer Thales on August 27, 2025. L2 Support tracked the investigation via COFI3516 . https://issues.sierrawireless.com/browse/COFI3156 . L3 found the issue was triggered by the planned shutdown of Telco nodes in the FKT data center. Further analysis revealed the VTY HSS/HLR did/could not route PRN to external networks after receiving SRI; sending System Failure error messages instead.

Voice service impairment was reported by customer Thales on August 27, 2025. L2 Support tracked the investigation via COFI3516 . https://issues.sierrawireless.com/browse/COFI3156 . L3 found the issue was triggered by the planned shutdown of Telco nodes in the FKT data center. Further analysis revealed the VTY HSS/HLR did/could not route PRN to external networks after receiving SRI; sending System Failure error messages instead.

summary: HC: Lost Services Increasing for Orange_901 (CSI)
description: On October 07, 2025, at 07:35 UTC, during a routine health check, GNOC observed a significant increase in lost service. We have investigated the issue and found the following observations: Investigation & Findings: OpenSearch analysis indicated sessions were stopped due to Lost Service towards Orange901 , impacting multiple VPLMNs and customers. Main VPLMNs appearing in Data Insights where the Lost Service radius transactions appeared were: Orange France Telenor Sweden Vodafone Germany Devices were unable to establish new data sessions for a short time, and some ongoing sessions were dropped. The Lost Service error was fluctuating, and the overall session drop remained limited. GNOC raised a JSM ticket and escalated the issue to Orange via the Orange Portal. Filtering at [OFFICIAL] Device Data session MVNO (Radius Based) The following Orange SGW IP addresses cropped up with the bursts of Lost Service at the following timestamps: Main apns showing up during these bursts were: ultrasync.swir inversprivate.mbqt.net 80.10.181.87 – 8:33 UTC 80.10.181.88 – 8:48 UTC 80.10.181.177 – 9:04 UTC 80.10.181.178 – 9:16 UTC 80.10.181.179 – 9:27 UTC 80.10.181.180 – 9:39 UTc 80.10.181.182 – 10:04 UTC 80.10.181.183 – 10:13 UTC 80.10.181.184 – 10:29 UTC Whereas our PGW IP address pulled up as 46.23.241.7 Current Status: Services were fully restored by 10:35 UTC on October 07, 2025 . No further recurrences have been observed Orange Ticket: S510090764 Keynotes: Customers Impacted: Invers GmbH (mostly), Toyota Material Handling, Solustop, Thales SIX GTS France – SAPHIR BAR 2023, Carrier Fire and Security B.V., Synox, ZEPLUG, CARRIER SCS, Wilson Electronics., Solustop,Networth Telecom , ZEPLUG Traffic Procedure: Start / Stop / Interim Update Error: Lost Service Roaming Partner: Orange901 Device Types: Essentials5, Advanced3, Advanced4 VPLMNs: Orange France TMobile USA, Inc Telenor Sverige AB Telia Sverige AB KPN B.V. Sample IMSIs: 8933240100001561443/208243091012657 Regions Impacted: EU an

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Impact Analysis: During the incident, GNOC observed 10 intermittent spikes, each lasting less than 20 minutes, after which the service returned to normal. These intermittent Lost Service errors and subsequent recoveries created a shark-tooth pattern on the graphs. Further investigation of one such occurrence revealed that approximately 13,000 devices experienced session drops due to Lost Service, impacting Data, Voice, and SMS services. However, the degradation was short-lived, and the affected devices were able to reattach to the network within a few minutes of each disturbance/spike. Filters & Timeline Used: Timeline:  Oct 7, 2025 @ 10:28:02.433→Oct 7, 2025 @ 10:33:40.496 Roaming Sponsor:  Orange_901 Termination Cause:  Lost Service   FYI…

summary: Alert : ECC LOST SERVICE via AT&T (CSI)
description: On 23rd oct 6:45 UTC , During our regular HC, GNOC observed a Stop due to a lost of services in ECC Radius Accounting Records via AAZ At&T, The spike was for 50min(6:45 to 7:34 UTC) and is back to normal now h , After a deep investigation in OpenSearch we found that the ECC Billing Data session was stopped due to lost of services and this was impacting our multiple customers including Sentinel most impacted. GNOC has raised the issue and reached out to NAM L2 and SME on SW Channel , the error has been back to normal at 23rd oct 7:34 UTC GNOC will monitor it closely and will close the ticket if there is no reoccurrence. L2 SME has confirmed No customer cases were raised . GNOC also received 3 P1 alerts that closed within 1 or 2 min at the start of the incident . GNOC also checked the Imeastro platform , The access Failure was also less for ATT (7.4%) GNOC has reached to ATT and asked for the root cause ATT Ticket ID: CS2332502 Final Status The levels are back to normal and ATT said it occurred in the middle of the usual maintenance period for AT&T it was most likely due a scheduled work. Current Status → GNOC checked OpenSearch & observed the KPI is back to normal for the customer. Start/Stop is back to normal threshold alongwith 2way sessions. GNOC continues to monitor . Start time: 23rd oct 6:45 UTC End time: 23rd oct 7:34 UTC Duration : 49 Minutes Impacted customers: Sentinal Impacted VPLMNs: AT&T Mobility Affected services: ECC Affected Roaming Partner: AAZ AT&T Device Type: ECC
