summary: JT/Velos Maintenance: Network vendor will be performing a Software Upgrade and maintenance of Mobile Core PCFW-2
description: From: noreply@velosiot.com < noreply@velosiot.com > Sent: Monday, October 20, 2025 12:03 PM To: Global NOC < globalnoc@semtech.com > Subject: Velos IoT Service Update Maintenance Velos IoT Planned Work Notification PW67824 23 October 2025 Caution: This email originated outside of Semtech. Scheduled 1 component affected Reason: Our Network vendor will be... ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ Velos IoT Planned Work Notification PW67824 Upcoming scheduled maintenance notice Reason : Our Network vendor will be performing a Software Upgrade and maintenance of Mobile Core PCFW2 Service Impacted : Mobile Core PCFWs2 Gi/sGi traffic, carried directly towards the internet or to Model 3 customer LAN via VPN. No customerfacing impact is expected. Change Summary : Our Network vendor has a responsibility to maintain the infrastructure on the latest and most stable software releases. Our Network vendor currently operate two highavailability cluster pairs (1 & 2) of firewalls, deployed on a virtualised chassis. These are the PCFWs, that carry Gi/sGi traffic, traffic reaches these FWs from the GGSN/PGWs and is routed either directly to the internet or to a 'Model 3' customers LAN via VPN. This PW pertains to upgrading the software on PCFWs in the cluster2. The upgrade is planned to have a zerodowntime approach by upgrading the standby unit first and leveraging ASA high availability (HA) failover. comments.author: akoffi
comments.body: Greetings, The alerts mentioned are being reviewed internally.  We suspect that the problem stems from messages originating from the SMSC. We have therefore opened ticket  COFI-3644  to identify the root cause and determine whether adjustments are necessary to avoid unnecessary notifications.  For now, we recommend monitoring the situation as the alerts have auto-closed, and the system appears stable. Any updates or findings from the investigation will be shared to guide GNOC on handling similar scenarios in the future. Thank you for your patience. Best regards,   Akoffi

summary: ALERT IMPROVEMENT REQUEST - Lost Service Spike in Radius Activity
description: Due to recent Orange901 issue on 9/2 We are hoping to get an alert created for when Lost Service termination cause codes come in at an excessive amount within a given fifteen minute block of time. For this issue, Invers did experience service level impact. https://vpcdatainsightbwk67b5airqgiro7xetpjrq7eq.eucentral1.es.amazonaws.com/dashboards/app/dashboards#/view/4dfcdeb0426211ee99b12d677c0af4d8?g=h@c472417&a=h@d53844b Would it be possible to implement an alert based on spikes in this terminate cause code type of Lost Service, let’s say, starting with a volume above 250 or maybe 500 within a 15 or 30 minute time window? Could we start off by activating such an alert for Invers , one for ChargePoint (for IDing issues peculiar for users depending on Sparkle R.S.), and a third for NEAT users (for spotting data session impact for JT traffic)? Maybe, this initial trio could expand down the road for other VIP customers.

summary: Alert Improvement: Lower Nutanix Alarm Severity - Protection domain Jumphosts snapshot
description: Greetings Monitoring Team, Infrastructure SME, David Adams, confirmed that this Nutanix : Protection domain Jumphosts snapshot ‘1236937’ failed. No entities snapshotted, skipped 1 P1 Alarm needs to be deemed “ Informational .” Please reduce this alarm severity from P1 to P4 or lower. David pointed out that there is some sort of alerting remapping that moves these alert types from P5 to P1 for some reason: Note: This Nutanix service is designed to allow Jumphost redundant availability. If one Jumphost is unavailable, Nutanix allows for the switching of the next available Jumphost.

If one Jumphost is unavailable, Nutanix allows for the switching of the next available Jumphost. Awareness:

comments.author: Julien Poisson
comments.body: Hello, we will check to remove this alert accordingly. However there is no mechanism in place that remapped the alert from P5 to P1 (it’s normal behavior from ZenOss/Opsgenie: simple mapping between systems having different alert priority naming). It’s most likely just an alert that was never sent.

comments.author: Julien Poisson
comments.body: this alert seems to be generated via trap, can you please if this should be instead removed from the Nutanix itself (usually we avoid altering the trap configuration)

comments.author: David Adams
comments.body: Julien there seems to be some confusion, as it is very clear from this and other alerts, that low severity alerts are indeed being remapped to high priority. On the Nutanix side, this was only 1 step above an informational log entry. As mentioned, this is not the only alert that has come across with a significantly increased priority.

comments.author: Julien Poisson
comments.body: can you provide the comprehensive list of alerts that is configured from Nutanix with their priority please ? Can you also please share the different severity you can implement in the Nutanix alerting ? As I mentionned in my previous message, the priority mapping that you see in Opsgenie is 100% normal it’s mapping priority between ZenOss and Opsgenie (Severity 5 in Zenoss corresponds to Error/P1 when Severity 1 corresponds to Logs/P5. Hence, we need to re-check in ZenOss itself if there was no mapping implementing to change trap priorities.   in parallel could you please confirm if there is any mapping that was implemented on Nutanix side ?

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: I queried for this alert type from 7/18 up through today, the 10th of October, and there doesn’t seem to be any more occurrences after this single one on 7/20. Can anyone confirm if adjustments were made shortly before July 20th?  Either way, the GNOC doesn’t seem to be receiving these alerts for the past three 2 1/2 months..

summary: BICS Maintenance: software upgrade in DWDM node in Marseille
description: From: BICS Planned Maintenance NOREPLY < plannedmaintenance.noreply@bics.com > Sent: Monday, October 6, 2025 11:23 AM To: Global NOC < globalnoc@sierrawireless.com > Subject: BICS Planned Maintenance [MTT 20254759479] Wed 22 October 2025 Announcement Caution: This email originated outside of Semtech. Planned Maintenance Announcement We would like to inform you about the following Planned Maintenance: Our reference MTT 20254759479 Start time of the event (UTC) Wed 22 October 2025 21:00 End time of the event (UTC) Thu 23 October 2025 03:00 Description of the Planned Maintenance: As part of our continued commitment in providing the best in class services to our valued customers worldwide, BICS is planning a software upgrade in DWDM node in Marseille. This software upgrade is expected to be no service affecting, however we have noticed some rare traffic hit during our lab trials to validate the new software. summary: Alert : Increase in Lost service for MNO Etisalat Benin SA (CSI)
description: GNOC Received a P3 kibana alert , for ETISALAT Benin SA , VPLMN DOWN Etisalat Benin SA [MVNOEU LostService] Radius Terminated sessions increase | The number of radiu , GNOC Checked in opensearch and observed a minor increase in lost service , GNOC investigated this issue in OpenSearch & observed that the increase in Lost Service started at 11:05 UTC More details as follows. Keynotes as Follows: Customer Webb Fontaine Group FZ LLC Error Type: Lost Service Traffic Procedure: STOP Traffic type: Radius Roaming Partner: , JT, Sparkle , , BICS . Device Type: ADVANCED4 VPLMN: ETISALAT Benin SA Country: Benin Start Time: 11:05 UTC End Time: Continuing Duration: Continuing Sample ICCID: 8933240100003072628 Device Behaviour: GNOC Checked a sample ICCID 8933240100003072628 It is a ADV Device , THe devices quickly recovered the session after getting lost service , it got multiple lost service in a go but reregistered to the same MNO and started the session Customer Impact: The Customer might experienced delay in Data session establishment , The devies after Experiencing Lost service and gradually recovered the session experiencing a delay in registration GNOC Checked a sample ICCID 8933240100003072628 It is a ADV Device , THe devices quickly recovered the session after getting lost service , it got multiple lost service in a go but reregistered to the same MNO and started the session

summary: Update EKS Cluster Authentication Mechanism to ConfigMap+EKS_API to eu, na and connect-apps production clusters
description: Since Dec 18, 2023 EKS clusters support a new "EKS API" authentication mode. This model superseded the old ConfigMap option that is not available for new cluster nowadays. This MOP is to switch old production clusters (eu, na, euconnectapps) to the transition mode "ConfigMap + EKSAPI" authentication mode to unify the configuration and prepare migration of rules.
searchable_text: Update EKS Cluster Authentication Mechanism to ConfigMap+EKS_API to eu, na and connect-apps production clusters Since Dec 18, 2023 EKS clusters support a new "EKS API" authentication mode. This model superseded the old ConfigMap option that is not available for new cluster nowadays. This MOP is to switch old production clusters (eu, na, euconnectapps) to the transition mode "ConfigMap + EKSAPI" authentication mode to unify the configuration and prepare migration of rules.

summary: Skylo:  Planned Network (RAN) Maintenance - Restore System Redundancy | S-band US
description: From: Skylo NOC <nocteam@skylo.tech> Sent: Friday, October 17, 2025 3:38 PM Cc: Skylo NOC Notifications <snocnotifications@skylo.tech> Subject: Planned Network Maintenance Notification Oct 22 2025; 07:00 UTC. Dear Team, This is to notify you that our Network Operations Management team has approved a Change Request to restore system redundancy by reverting the secondary base stations to its primary status. Summary of Impact: Devices within the mentioned regions may face intermittent impact in their ability to send and receive messages during the activity window. The Skylo NOC will issue a closure notification for the scheduled activity once the maintenance window has concluded. For further details, please refer to the Change notification below. summary: Syniverse Initial Planned Maintenance (CN:108974-1) -MES IMN, HYPERSCALE COMMUNICATIONS PLATFORM (HCP), MES IMN INTERNATIONAL, SYNIVERSE COMMUNICATION GATEWAY (SCG)
description: From: notification@syniverse.com < notification@syniverse.com > Sent: Monday, October 13, 2025 12:50 PM Subject: Syniverse Initial Planned Maintenance (CN:1089741) MES IMN, HYPERSCALE COMMUNICATIONS PLATFORM (HCP), MES IMN INTERNATIONAL, SYNIVERSE COMMUNICATION GATEWAY (SCG) Planned Maintenance : CN:1089741 Customer Impact There will be no impact to message delivery during the activity window since we are failing over our services to the Chicago site. Impacted Products MES IMN, HYPERSCALE COMMUNICATIONS PLATFORM (HCP),MES IMN INTERNATIONAL,SYNIVERSE COMMUNICATION GATEWAY (SCG) Notification ID CN:1089741 Planned Maintenance Source Syniverse Notes Syniverse CRQ000000291579 Initial: Syniverse will be performing network maintenance in Dallas site. Prior to this activity, the operations team will be doing a traffic failover from Dallas to Chicago Datacenter to avoid any impact. For SMPP and SMPP SSL customers specifically, please make the necessary changes to your application in advance if applicable, to ensure you are connected to the secondary site to prevent any message loss during the maintenance window. For HTTPS/Web services/Bulk protocol customers there is no need to make any changes. As part of the balancing of the SMPP connections across the sites, customers over the SMPP protocol will observe a disconnect and reconnect. Timestamp Notification Date/Time (EST): 20251013 12:38 Start Date/Time (EST): 20251020 22:00 End Date/Time (EST): 20251021 06:00 Expected End Date/Time (EST): 20251021 06:00 Expected Duration 8 hrs, 0 mins

summary: Migrate av-accounts to istio service mesh
description: To add the service into service mesh, it should be deployed with service mesh enabled in helm chart values which adds istioproxy sidecar container to the application Pods and creates VirtualService resource with default configuration. Then, DNS name needs to be migrated from the existing ALB to the new istioingress ALB.
searchable_text: Migrate av-accounts to istio service mesh To add the service into service mesh, it should be deployed with service mesh enabled in helm chart values which adds istioproxy sidecar container to the application Pods and creates VirtualService resource with default configuration. Then, DNS name needs to be migrated from the existing ALB to the new istioingress ALB.

summary: Alert: SC - Bambora Device AB all traffic increase (NCI)
description: Since 20251021 01:03 UTC, a increase of Sigtran and Diameter traffic was observed for single customer Bambora Device AB. Customer is using VPLMN Nuuday A/S over Roaming Partner Orange901. Upon investigating deeper into kibana, GNOC observed that the devices were constantly performing SAI/AIR and then UL/ULR. GNOC also checked AV and observed no provisioning action being performed. Hence, GNOC believes that this anomaly increase is due to customer devices. GNOC will continue to monitor this increase. Session count for Orange remains stable. At 20251021 02:25 UTC , increase started to drop back down to normal pattern. Start time: 20251021 01:03 UTC End time: 20251021 02:25 UTC Duration: 2 hour 22 min Impacted customers: Bambora Device AB Impacted VPLMNs: Nuuday A/S Affected services: Sigtran, Diameter, Vssf, Radius Affected Roaming Partner: Orange901 Device Type: Essential5 Sample ICCID: 8933240100002597543 ICCID Unique Count 3,776

summary: Comfone: Maintenance Announcement: SS7 signalling in Zurich STP -20251020
description: Dear Customer/Partner, This is a Comfone maintenance notification. An upgrade of the STP software and operating system in Zurich needs to be carried out. The work will impact your SS7 signalling service as per the table below. Location Zurich Maintenance start: 20 th October 2025, 21:00 UTC Maintenance end: 21 th October 2025, 01:00 UTC Impact: Interruptions of the Signalling links to Comfone Zurich STP will occur. Location Network SPC (383 or 888) SPC (dec.) Zurich ITU International 72281 16161 ITU International Spare 02495 1997 ANSI National 510961 355645 Not service affecting as Comfone STPs listed below will be in service during the maintenance work and process all SS7 signaling traffic. Location Network SPC (383 or 888) SPC (dec.) Lausanne ITU International 72280 16160 ITU International Spare 02494 1996 ANSI National 510962 355646 Zurich ITU International 2617 4591 ITU International Spare 02452 1962 ANSI National 510967 355651 Lausanne ITU International 2616 4590 ITU International Spare 02451 1961 ANSI National 510966 355650 Please forward this email to all concerned parties within your organization. If you have any questions, feel free to contact Comfone NOC. Regards, NOC Network Operation Centre Comfone AG Mingerstrasse 12 3014 Bern Switzerland Phone: +41 31 341 11 00 Email: noc@comfone.com Web: www.comfone.com

summary: EXT: Syniverse - Reporting on a Decommissioned PC Connection  (NSI)
description: EXT: Syniverse Reporting on a Decommissioned PC Connection (NSI) Final Status Update: This point code connection between 005066011 (Semtech/Numerex) and 235042000 (Syniverse) was removed over a year ago. GNOC reached out to the Syniverse Command Center to have its monitoring decommissioned. Also reference: At 20251018 10:04 UTC , GNOC received an email from the Syniverse Command Center informing that they are observing a connectivity issue between Syniverse Point Code 235042000 and Semtech Point Code 005066011 on Wentzville . GNOC has raised a ticket and reached out to the IP Network SME on Slack for confirmation and further investigation. This point code connection between 005066011 (Semtech/Numerex) and 235042000 (Syniverse) was removed over a year ago. GNOC reached out to the Syniverse Command Center to have its monitoring decommissioned. Email Description: Hello Team, We want to inform you that we are seeing a problem between our point codes on Wentzville, can you please check and let us know if you are seeing a problem or having a maintenance on your side that can affect this connection: Your Point Code : 005066011 Our Point Code : 235042000

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2025-10-18 14:07 UTC GNOC Leadership responded to the Syniverse Command Center with the following request for decommissioning: Greetings Ajaydas / Syniverse colleagues, Can the Syniverse Command Center designate a team or an individual who can initiate the decommissioning process for monitoring this point code connection? As previously communicated, the connection between point codes 005-066-011 (Semtech/Numerex) and 235-042-000 (Syniverse) was removed over a year ago.  See emails attached referencing Syniverse tickets: #INC000001717514 and #INC-1386206. This would help prevent any future unnecessary engagement related to this retired connection. Thank you for your support.

summary: EXT: Syniverse Fiber Circuit Down - Provider Reported BGP Peer Down (NSI)
description: Syniverse Fiber Circuit Down Provider Reported BGP Peer Down (NSI) | No Impact (Due to Redundancy 4 Syniverse connections) At 20251016 22:02 UTC the Syniverse Command Center reported observing Semtech’s BGP Peer Down. Although, there are No active Alarms, GNOC reached out to the L3 IP Data Network team to assess and confirmed that Syniverse will need to open a ticket with their thirdparty fiber circuit (probably Windstream). Syniverse ticket# INC000001719506 Circuit Vendor Windstream Master TT# 06128729 Current Status: Syniverse IP/KSXX/066061/001/WCI circuit is down. Awaiting SCC (Syniverse Command Center) Feedback… Initial Syniverse (Vendor) ETA approx. 20251017 20:00 UTC. FYI Alarms temporarily disabled (due to Syniverse circuit project). GNOC will continue to follow up with Syniverse. Start Time: Approx. 20251016 21:36 UTC (Alerting is Down; Awaiting SCC Confirmation) End Time: N/A Duration: N/A Additional Notes: At 20251016 22:02 UTC the Syniverse Command Center reported observing Semtech’s BGP Peer Down. Although, there are No active Alarms, GNOC reached out to the L3 IP Data Network team to assess and confirmed that Syniverse will need to open a ticket with their thirdparty fiber circuit (probably Windstream). (GNOC consulted IP Data Network team; Syniverse opened a ticket with its thirdparty fiber circuit vendor (Windstream). The IP Data Network team comfirmed that the Syniverse IP/KSXX/066061/001/WCI circuit is down, and due to the recent the Syniverse circuit augment project the alerts were disabled (temporarily). SMEs stated that “now we have four different connections to them two of the new connections will alert correctly but the old connections won't. Once we get done with the work everything will be working properly but like I said that probably won't be for a few weeks as syniverse is slow.” Most current update10/17 12:35 UTC –from Syniverse…Master TT Number: 06128729 Wisconsin Region they advised US signal should be back on site around 14 UTC today.

summary: HC : Lost Service Spike for MNO Bouygues Telecom (CSI)
description: On 15th October 2025, from 1:33 UTC, GNOC observeda Lost service 1:33 to 1:37 via single VPLMN (Bouygues Telecom) in France . Through Multiple Roaming Sponsor, GNOC observed that Session count were dropped Gradually , It was a Four Minute Spike impacting 13K Devices and Approx 10K Sessions drop Zenoss Link GNOC investigated this issue in OpenSearch & observed that the increase in Lost Service started at 01:33UTC & returned to normal level at 01:37 UTC. Current Status → Levels are Back to Normal BICS Ticket SC4778822 More details as follows. Keynotes as Follows: Customer Synox , FIFTEEN , ZEPLUG , Matooma , Networth Telecom , Ingenico France , LOOMIS France Connectivity , UTC Fire & Security B.V ,Thales SIX GTS France – SAPHIR BAR 2023 , Wilson Electronics Error Type: Lost Service Traffic Procedure: STOP Traffic type: Radius Roaming Partner: , JT, Sparkle , , BICS . Device Type: ESSENTIALS1 , ADVANCED3 , ADVANCED4 , ADVANCED2 , ADVANCED , LITE VPLMN: Bouygues Telecom Country: France Start Time: 1:33 UTC End Time: 01:37 UTC Duration: 4 Minutes Sample ICCID: 8933240100003855065, 8933240100003580308 Device Behaviour: GNOC Checked a sample ICCID 8933240100003580308 It is a ADV Device , THe devices quickly recovered the session after getting lost service , it got multiple lost service in a go but reregistered to the same MNO and started the session Customer Impact: The Customer might experienced delay in Data session establishment , The devies after Experiencing Lost service and gradually recovered the session experiencing a delay in registration GNOC Checked a sample ICCID 8933240100003580308 It is a ADV Device , THe devices quickly recovered the session after getting lost service , it got multiple lost service in a go but reregistered to the same MNO and started the session

comments.author: yjadhav
comments.body: Greetings!    ,    Today we observed a 4min Lost Service spike For Bouygues Telecom in France , the lost service impacted 13k Devices and almost 10k session drop was observed in Zenoss , The sessions Gradually Restored After the lost service until 8:05 UTC , could you please check and suggest if we should engage the Roaming Sponsor ?? FYI

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: can you pick the iccid and try to capture and show how we have received lost service. As I explained today check the number of seconds of  data  establishment and compare.

comments.author: yjadhav
comments.body: this is what we Discussed -> ICCID →  8933240100003580308 The Device was doing Interim Update Continously every 15mins , The Input and output octet has a significant Difference during normal interim update and stop procedure also the session time was existing (3589 seconds , 2702 seconds)     But During the Incident time -> no Input output octet existed and session time was 0 , the session was not able to be Handled and was dropping continously leading the Device to try Multiple Times

summary: Migrate av-backoffice to istio service mesh
description: To add the service into service mesh, it should be deployed with service mesh enabled in helm chart values which adds istioproxy sidecar container to the application Pods and creates VirtualService resource with default configuration. Then, DNS name needs to be migrated from the existing ALB to the new istioingress ALB.

summary: HC SC: Invers increase in RNA in Hungary(CSI)
description: During the HC, GNOC observed an increase in RNA for Invers devices in Hungary over the VPLMN Magyar Telekom Nyrt. After reviewing the Roaming Zone spreadsheet, it appears that these Invers devices are not permitted to roam using this VPLMN. GNOC will refer the case to the L2 team for further review. Invers has not opened any SF tickets. Final Update → Based on the 7day data and Coverage File analysis , since the devices are not permitted to roam in Hungary via BICS and MNO Magyar Telekom Nyrt , the observed RNA is expected and may be a temporary customergenerated issue .

summary: NAM - Syniverse GRX redundancy project
description: CAB is to enable the additional 10 gig fibers we have with Syniverse. This is a fortification of our Syniverse connectivity and gives us much greater redundancy. No MOP has been added as all the work is on the Syniverse side to open traffic; we just need to monitor traffic (PGW sessions, and SS7 links to TMM). No impact is expected but as always we can’t guarantee anything. GNOC should monitor NAM PGW traffic during this migration. should also monitor SS7 links to SMSC in NAM to ensure they remain operational. The CAB is for NAM region only and will not impact traffic to EU PGW / EU SS7 / EU Diameter Links.

summary: HC: Single Customer : WEEZEVENT SAS || RNA increased in Morocco(NSI)
description: During GNOC’s health check, RNA errors were observed for WEEZEVENT SAS devices ( Advanced3/4 ) in Morocco since 20251003 08:30 UTC . The issue is expected as Morocco is not included in the allowed roaming list (RZT – EU + Switzerland). Investigation and Findings: Based on a detailed investigation across multiple OpenSearch Dashboards, it was found that Advanced3 and Advanced4 devices were receiving Roaming Not Allowed (RNA) errors in Morocco . These devices are not permitted to roam in Morocco as per the current RZT Template (RZT – EU + Switzerland) . A validation of roaming capabilities with sample devices confirmed that Morocco is not included in the allowed roaming list, hence RNA is expected. The impacted Advanced devices were attempting to roam with partners Sparkle, JT, and Orange901 across different VPLMNs: Orange Morocco Itissalat AlMaghrib S.A. (IAM) Wana Corporate S.A. – Inwi (only for UL) Errors were observed for UL and ULR procedures . Devices attempted to change IMSI and VPLMN but failed due to the absence of a roaming agreement. This aligns with historical behavior and current configuration. Note: GNOC has not received any alerts related to this issue. Current Status: As we investigated earlier, there was no traffic in Morocco for the WEEZEVENT SAS customer using the RZT Template (RZT – EU + Switzerland). We also checked the current device count, which is only 144. Since the count is not too high and this may be a temporary issue (due to devices moving into a nonroaming agreement zone), and as NOC lead confirmed, it is part of the Signaling Reduction Project, We are closing this case. Keynotes as Follows: Customer: WEEZEVENT SAS Traffic Procedure: UL, ULR, ULGPRS Traffic type: Diameter/ SIGTRAN Error Type: Roaming not Allowed Roaming Partner: JT, Sparkle and Orange901 Device Type: ADVANCED4 / ADVANCED3 VPLMN: Orange Morocco, Itissalat AlMaghrib S.A. IAM, Wana Corporate S.A. Inwi(Only for UL) Start Time: 20251003 08:30 UTC End Time: Duration: Country: M

comments.author: Preston Crump
comments.body: GNOC is seeing that RNA errors were observed for WEEZEVENT SAS devices (Advanced3/4) in Morocco since 2025-10-03 08:30 UTC. They are broadcasting aggressively on the network in Morocco causing RNA Roaming Not Allowed. These devices are not permitted to roam in Morocco as per the current RZT Template (RZT – EU + Switzerland). We are wondering if an L2 can reach out to the customer during normal business hours to find out what is causing this event

comments.author: Asim Khamari
comments.body: Weezevent devices offers IoT-enabled event management solutions, integrating ticketing, access control, and cashless payments through connected devices primarily in France , Mexico , and South Africa .( https://confluence.sierrawireless.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=442173464 ). When we found that devices were trying to register in Morocco, these are the questions we should ask ourselves: Is there any Weezevent device registered in Morocco ? Have we ever seen any traffic for Weesevent. If we are seeing RNA , how many devices are getting RNA? This could be related to the signalling reduction project(  )?

comments.author: Rajan Yadav
comments.body: Thanks,   , for the suggestions. As we investigated earlier, there was no traffic in Morocco for the  WEEZEVENT SAS  customer using the RZT Template (RZT – EU + Switzerland). I also checked the current device count, which is only 144. Since the count is not too high and this may be a temporary issue (due to devices moving into a non-roaming agreement zone), and as you confirmed, it is part of the Signaling Reduction Project, I am closing this case. Thanks!!

summary: HC:  Internal: Zenoss Dashboard_Traffic Sync issue(NSI)
description: Since 20251006 06:00 UTC, during our regular health check, GNOC has noticed that the RNA has increased significantly in the Zenoss Dashboard, along with CSPS location update request drops. The node view looks fine, and GNOC couldn’t find any anomalies or traffic variations in OpenSearch. We also saw that LTEauthsuccess Ratios have increased to 100%. We also could not see any abnormalities in Viavi, either with 7 days of data.GNOC has informed Network SME on the Slack Channel . Per SME Amin, the values do not seem in line with SCARF and could be an issue related to Monitoring. Monitoring SME has acknolodge the issue and they are working on the same. Current Status: The traffic has been restored and is running smoothly. GNOC will close this case. Root cause: SME rolled back the previous HLR ZenPack; there was an issue with the new ZenOss pushed. comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: Incident Management Process Services Restored.  Jersey Telecom (JT) experienced network degradation in LTE Network Service primarily in North America.  JT experienced a Device Connectivity Issue due to congestion on one of its partner’s core networks.  The Incident was raised to a Level 3 Escalation.  Semtech’s Essentials 3 customers may have experienced some issues (delays or intermittent failures) establishing LTE Data services and network connectivity primarily in the North America market.   (GNOC-9798) Jersey Telecom LTE Network Degradation (primarily in North America)  Decrease Jersey Telecom LTE Network Connectivity - JT’s LTE traffic dropped 50% No Customer Reported Cases Velos (JT) ticket# IOT-42932   ~6 hours, 30 minutes

summary: All External emails blocked to GlobalNOC mail ID and GNOC members (NSI)
description: All external emails blocked to GlobaNoc and it's members Incident Summary Issue: Email delivery failures to globalnoc@semtech.com mailbox and its members due to Mimecast email filtering system. Root Cause: Emails were being held/blocked by Mimecast's new email filtering system. External and internal senders are currently being screened as part of postimplementation review. No senders are whitelisted at this time while the email security team reviews and organizes the new filtering system. Current Stats – 10/15: Based on the followup call from Scott Critchfield from IT in California, Semtech IT has no plans on relaxing inbox restrictions which might impose themselves upon various inbox addresses from external domains. IT advised the GNOC will need to frequently follow up within Mimecast, as shown in the link below and make sure we release the emails and domains which appear as withheld from us. Due to what was communicated over the phone regarding 296145, I am resolving out this particular GNOC ticket. Our current (individual) workaround, is a manual release of held emails after logging into Mimecast, https://login.mimecast.com/m/portal/login/#/login The team received only one general notification email from Mimecast last month , informing them about the new email filtering system implementation and asking them to review the Mimecast portal. However, this was merely a broad announcement about the system rollout rather than specific alerts about blocked emails. Impact: Multiple emails were held in quarantine and not delivered to recipients Priority customer service emails were delayed, affecting realtime response capabilities No notification emails were received by affected users when messages were held Resolution Steps Taken: All held emails were manually released by the Email Admin team Emails were released specifically for globalnoc@semtech.com shared mailbox and its members Users were informed about the Mimecast portal ( https://loginusb.mimecast.com/ ) where they

Working with IT team to fix the issue.     Some examples of the email IDs that are blocked :   support@velosiot.com   Velos/JT orange.support@orange.com   Orange jira@sierrawireless.atlassian.net   Jira Updates ticketing_noreply@lumen.com    Lumen communication.

They had, in fact, confirmed all the reported domains, provided from Asim & Arabinda have been allowed. Scott advised there are not further plans from IT to open up conventionally accepted domains due to a recent breach within the past couple of months (he referenced an intrusive breach via MS teams)… best advice offered was for us to go into Mimecast pretty regularly (maybe a weekly basis) to make sure no necessary domains are getting blocked. summary: HC:SC - QOTTO SAS Sigtran Traffic Increased (CSI)
description: Since 20251015 05:20 GNOC observed an increase of Sigtran traffic for single customer QOTTO SAS. Upon further investigation, GNOC observed that Success/RNA/SAI traffic increased for this customer through VPLMN Orange Burkina Faso over JT and Sparkle network, and is primarily impacted Advanced 4 devices. Traffic started to decrease from 05:50 UTC, GNOC will monitor closely for any further anomaly or recurrence Start time: 20251015 00:50 End time: 20251015 06:00 Duration: 5 Hours 10 Minutes Impacted customers: QOTTO SAS Impacted VPLMNs: Orange Burkina Faso Affected services: Sigtran Affected Roaming Partner: JT, Sparkle Device Type: Advanced 4 Sample ICCID: 8933240100003119000 Investigation Summary : It seems the issue of increased RNA & subsequently increase in SAI traffic was because of Retired & Suspended SIMs of the customer. When only ACTIVE SIMs were selected in OpenSearch no issues with traffic was observed. Hence GNOC is completing this ticket.
searchable_text: HC:SC - QOTTO SAS Sigtran Traffic Increased (CSI) Since 20251015 05:20 GNOC observed an increase of Sigtran traffic for single customer QOTTO SAS. Upon further investigation, GNOC observed that Success/RNA/SAI traffic increased for this customer through VPLMN Orange Burkina Faso over JT and Sparkle network, and is primarily impacted Advanced 4 devices. Traffic started to decrease from 05:50 UTC, GNOC will monitor closely for any further anomaly or recurrence Start time: 20251015 00:50 End time: 20251015 06:00 Duration: 5 Hours 10 Minutes Impacted customers: QOTTO SAS Impacted VPLMNs: Orange Burkina Faso Affected services: Sigtran Affected Roaming Partner: JT, Sparkle Device Type: Advanced 4 Sample ICCID: 8933240100003119000 Investigation Summary : It seems the issue of increased RNA & subsequently increase in SAI traffic was because of Retired & Suspended SIMs of the customer. When only ACTIVE SIMs were selected in OpenSearch no issues with traffic was observed. Hence GNOC is completing this ticket.

summary: HC : Temporary Lost Service for Single VPLMN ATT affecting ECC & MVNO Service (CSI)
description: During GNOC’s regular HC on 15th October, GNOC observed temporary increase in Lost Service for single VPLMN ATT from 06:10 UTC to 07:10 UTC. GNOC investigated this in OpenSearch & observed both MVNO & ECC offerings were impacted. More keynotes as follows, No Customer tickets received. Keynotes as Follows: Customer : SentinelPROD, AED Smart Monitoring LLC, Arcoro Holdings Corp, Neurio Technology Inc, UplinkPROD, Nextivity, Aperia Technologies, ChargePoint Inc, The Chamberlain Group, Premier Wireless Solutions, Toyota Material Handling, Wilson Electronics etc Error Type: LOST Service Traffic Procedure: STOP Traffic type: Lost Service Roaming Partner: ECC, Sparkle, Orange, JT Device Type: ECC & Essentials1 VPLMN: ATT Country: USA Start Time: 06:10 UTC End Time: 07:10 UTC Duration: 1 Hour Sample ICCID: 8933240100003127607 Device Behaviour: GNOC took a device with sample ICCID 8933240100003127607 & observed the device dropped off from ATT network with Lost Service but reconnected with the same network with delay. Device STARTED Sessions with a minor delay. Customer Impact: Customer devices must have dropped off from the network momentarily & would have started session with a delay .

summary: ALERT:  High Memory usage on FWL-FKT-FRT-Node0 Firewall (NSI)
description: Summary: On 29th September at 15:46 UTC, GNOC received a P3 ALERT related to “High Memory usage on FWLFKTFRTNode0 Firewall”. GNOC opened this ticket & reached out to SME for a fix. SME Hafedh investigated & found that, the misconfiguration on DNS Side is generating hundreds of denied DNS queries per hour , resulting in, Log buffer saturation , Sustained memory elevation , & ACL processing overhead . Hence the System Team, from Juniper needs to rectify the DNS settings to fix the issue. Currently alert has autoclosed, however the memory usage is still high. Current Memory Usage: 79% (baseline: 5657%) . Current Status→ System team needs to correct DNS configuration on 10.62.55.22 ( SME Hafedh will give feedback when he has a confirmation from system team , as per him there is no urgencies, he is monitoring the FWL) There is no ETR received from SME I just emailed SME (Hafedh Essay), requesting any possible ETR he may have from System Team on rectifying DNS configuration. Analysis from SME: Required Action: System team must reconfigure DNS settings on server 10.62.55.22 to use authorized DNS servers: Primary: 10.62.30.100 Secondary: 10.62.30.101 Expected outcome: Memory should return to baseline (5657%) within 24 hours after DNS correction. Next Steps: System team corrects DNS configuration on 10.62.55.22 NOC monitors firewall memory levels If memory remains elevated after 4 hours, secondary investigation required (SNMP polling load or other factors) Current Status 10/10 Just pinged SME via this JSM ticket since the Memory Usage levels have decreased down to 66% as of 1:00 CST/6:00 UTC today, the 10th. Requested confirmation that DNS config was rectified. GNOC Observation: GNOC checked in ZENOSS & observed below percent of memory usage again different days. Before 17th Sept 06:00 UTC – 56% On 17th Sept 12:00 UTC ~ 62% 18th September to 29th September ~ 59% From 29th September 18:00 UTC till date (8th October 07:00 UTC) ~ 79% (Alert is Closed)

comments.author: Hafedh Essayeh
comments.body: FYI - I received a call from 01 80 53 89 28 84 but there was no one speaking when I answered!!! Firewall Memory Issue - Root Cause Identified: DNS Misconfiguration on 10.62.55.22 Firewall:  fwl-fktfrt-prod Current Memory Usage:  79% (baseline: 56-57%) Issue Duration:  possibly since September 29th memory spike Server  10.62.55.22  (network: fkt_mvno_cdr_bgw_prd) is configured to use DNS server  10.61.30.100 , which is not included in the authorized DNS object-groups for this network segment. Authorized DNS servers for fkt_mvno_cdr_bgw_prd: mbqt_dns_servers: 10.60.30.15 fkt_prod_dns_servers: 10.62.30.100, 10.62.30.101 Impact: The misconfiguration is generating  hundreds of denied DNS queries per hour , resulting in: Log buffer saturation  - 29,007 messages currently logged Sustained memory elevation  - Memory cannot be released due to continuous packet processing and logging ACL processing overhead  - Each denied packet must be evaluated, logged, and tracked Log evidence: %ASA-4-106023: Deny udp src fkt_mvno_cdr_bgw_prd:10.62.55.22/[varying ports] dst interco_vprn214:10.61.30.100/53 by access-group "fkt_mvno_cdr_bgw_prd" Rate limiting was triggered at 05:04:52 UTC on Oct 3: %ASA-4-733100: [Scanning] drop rate-1 exceeded. Current burst rate is 16 per second Required Action: System team must reconfigure DNS settings on server 10.62.55.22 to use authorized DNS servers: Primary: 10.62.30.100 Secondary: 10.62.30.101 Expected outcome:  Memory should return to baseline (56-57%) within 2-4 hours after DNS correction. Next Steps: System team corrects DNS configuration on 10.62.55.22 NOC monitors firewall memory levels If memory remains elevated after 4 hours, secondary investigation required (SNMP polling load or other factors)

summary: HC:  VIAVI PA - SS7 UDTS - (SCCP) - NO TRANSLATIONS FOR THIS ADDRESS (NSI)
description: While analyzing the SS7 traffic within Explore QoS, an exorbitant amount of UDTSNo translations for this address Cause code rejects surfaced over the past 48 hours. No ULs are coming in from these particular GTs listed below ; however , Cancel Location Invokes are going out to these GTs associated with BICs, with both BICS MAR22 & BIS STR21 sending back SCCP: No translations for this address The most recent capture shows the repeat offenders are the following BICS GTs: 324750004129047 324750004129038 Recent captures on 8/31 indicate rise in UDTS for CLR invokes going out to 22995651082 (Moov Benin), while no fresh ULs come in for this GT from this particular VPLMN via JT. Could you please assist us in determining why we would be sending the Cancel Location Invokes to these GTs if we are not receiving any ULs or UL GPRSs for them?

No ULs are coming in from these particular GTs listed below ; however , Cancel Location Invokes are going out to these GTs associated with BICs, with both BICS MAR22 & BIS STR21 sending back SCCP: No translations for this address The most recent capture shows the repeat offenders are the following BICS GTs: 324750004129047 324750004129038 Recent captures on 8/31 indicate rise in UDTS for CLR invokes going out to 22995651082 (Moov Benin), while no fresh ULs come in for this GT from this particular VPLMN via JT. Could you please assist us in determining why we would be sending the Cancel Location Invokes to these GTs if we are not receiving any ULs or UL GPRSs for them?

comments.body: @ 14:49 UTC HLR platform manager inquired on obtaining a list of IMSIs showing up in capture… and advsd Anas that a query within the HLr to determine if quantity is small, a basic reprovision could stop the occurences of this cause code.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Just sent the following over to the manager over HLRs That does sound like how the network normally would behave.  What is baffling about these recent PA captures for this reject cause is that these three main GTs, when querying them as the calling address for Uls, don’t show any traffic coming in from them.  324750004129038 ( BICS ) 324750004129047 ( BICS ) 22995651082 –  Moov Benin  (coming in through JT) SCCP - UDTS- no translations for this address  However, when I plug in a different GT associated with BICS (324750004061704), pulled from a generic PA capture for Uls, the search does pull up recent activity, as show in the second screenshot below.  Do you think these two BICS GTs could be removed sometime today or this week?   @Abinash Panda Also, would we need to engage JT to confirm the status of the  Moov GT , or would you need to approach JT regarding this odd behavior?

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: HLR platform manager did update via email at aruond 16:24 UTC The problem is we will just send out system failures now for these same devices.  Let me take a look and do some testing.  And if we cannot figure out a solution, we can remove it from the HLR.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Was advised that we are not able to delete the two BICS GTs only showing up for the erroneous CL invokes provoking UDTS errors…. Since the HLR is configured for the range belonging to BICS. Was advised that we need to “identify all subs that had these 2 bad GTs as an old network. Once we remove them from their individual subscription, technically they would never register there again because it does not exist from BICs.” The most recent downloaded spreadsheet revealed around 220 to 235 individual IMSIs.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Yesterday, did continue querying within Viavi based on the spotted GTs:   BICS - 324750004129038               324750004129047   Moov Benin 22995651082 (CLR going out to JT) SCCP - UDTS- no translations for this address and tried a wildcard query based on the four digits at the end of the BICS range…ending in 9XXX found plenty of successful ULs come in for the following GTs, listed down below.  Did inform HLR platform manager of this distinction and sent over the list of IMSIs showing up within past 24 hrs per GT  324750004129087 324750004129079 324750004129076 324750004129073 324750004129072 324750004129064 324750004129063 324750004129062 324750004129061 Total counts per IMSI are not reaching into the thousands, since some of the IMSIs are generating repeat cancel locate invokes.. total quantity of failing transactions are reaching much higher due to the repeats.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Was informed at 18:38 UTC that some work had to be done from node SME level to figure out a workaround since there are other functional GTs within the 9XXX last portion of the GT range.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: On Tuesday, Anas did state the following via MS teams chat Hello Lewie Spearman can you check if any of below imsi get the error Bics GTs any more? 208240004891508  208244500302327  208240005048902  208240005050098  208240002360164  208240002361400  208244500371201  208244500641589  208240002360405  208240004681803  they are from your list, and I prevent them to have success UL since last night.. lets see if this workaround can help!   Today, Thursday, the 18th  I sent over a fresh capture from a PA within Vaivi today…over to Minh and Anas.    I did just rerun a PA in Viavi, and the counts, after removing duplicates, has gone down some for these 3 specific GTs, but there are still some of these stragglers showing up.  Thanks for taking another peek when things free you up to do so.     324750004129047 - 50 IMSIs showing up for CLR invokes out to this GT 9/18 05:00 UTC to14:50 UTC   324750004129038 - 80 IMSIs showing up for CLR invokes out to this GT 9/18 05:00 UTC to14:50 UTC   22995651082 - 24  IMSIs showing up for CLR invokes out to  this GT 9/18 05:00 UTC to14:50 UTC

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: @4:26 CST/9:26 UTC…Anas advsd that he revisited same subset of IMSIs and preformed an unblocking for them….also, advsd to go in and see if these are users are no longer sending CLRs to the three spotted GTS.. that would be indicative of the workaround’s success.  Have pasted them in below and will rerun a PA to see any of these IMSIs pull up.  208240004891508 208244500302327 208240005048902 208240005050098 208240002360164 208240002361400 208244500371201 208244500641589 208240002360405 208240004681803

Will the PA again on 9/20 & 9/21 and advse SMEs of results.  208240005050098 13:37 UTC CLR-UDTS 324750004129047 208240002361400 13:54 UTC CLR-UDTS 324750004129047

comments.author: Lewie Spearman

summary: Alert : Increase in Lost Service via single VPLMN Telekom Deutschland GmbH (T-Mobile) (NSI)
description: On 14th October 2025 from 03:24 UTC, GNOC received an alert for increase in Lost Service via single VPLMN Telekom Deutschland GmbH (TMobile) . GNOC investigated this issue in OpenSearch & observed that the increase in Lost Service via this single VPLMN started at 03:00 UTC & returned to normal level at 06:50 UTC. More details as follows. Current Status → Lost Service via VPLMN Telekom Deutschland GmbH has returned to normal level. JT Ticket → IOT43101 Keynotes as Follows: Customer : Franke & Werner GmbH, NEAT Electronics AB, VTron Electronics Corp, SAVVY Telematic Systems AG, Innotas Produktions GmbH, Invers GmbH, Eco Compteur, Nordic Gamekeeper AB, AssistMe GmbH, CGM Lauer etc Error Type: Lost Service Traffic Procedure: STOP Traffic type: Radius Roaming Partner: MBQT, JT, Sparkle Device Type: ADVANCEDNODATA, ESSENTIALS3, ADVANCED3, ADVANCED2NODATA, ADVANCED4 VPLMN: Telekom Deutschland GmbH Country: Germany Start Time: 03:00 UTC End Time: 06:50 UTC Duration: 3 Hours 50 Minutes Sample ICCID: 8933240100001522747 Device Behaviour: GNOC checked single Device Behavior ,ICCID 8933240100001522747 , Initially latched with Telecom Deutschland , The device got lost service and retried with other MNO (Telefonica Germany ) The device after multiple retries re registered with Telefonica germany and started the session , the device Experienced Delay in starting the session , and was unable to do interim updates during Lost service Customer Impact: Customer might have experienced Delay in Session registration , the sessions were dropped from Telekom Deutschland GmbH and took a while in reregistering to other MNO MVNO GNOC checked single Device Behavior ,ICCID 8933240100001522747 , Initially latched with Telecom Deutschland , The device got lost service and retried with other MNO (Telefonica Germany ) The device after multiple retries re registered with Telefonica germany and started the session , the device Experienced Delay in starting the session , and was unable to do interim u

Also the impacted devices are switching MNOs to maintain connectivity.  We are requesting JT to conduct an investigation with MNO for the same. We will keep adding updates here.     FYIP .

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !! The Lost Service has reduced its normal level.  GNOC continues to monitor.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Greetings All !! JT has responded that they do not see any issue. After few hours of monitoring it seems the issue has been fixed. GNOC is completing this ticket.

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: Per email from Velos. they stated that their partner investigated and the issue was from our side or the devices. I have informed them that We believe that this issue originated on the VPLMN side. It impacted multiple customers, who were able to connect over other VPLMNs. Please re-engage them to check their side. From:  Velos IoT Customer Support Portal < support@velosiot.com > Sent:  Tuesday, October 14, 2025 9:07 AM To:  Global NOC < globalnoc@semtech.com > Subject:  IOT-43101 Connectivty issue with Telekom Deutschland GmbH (T-Mobile) Network // CS0128857 Caution:  This email originated outside of Semtech. —-—-—-— Reply above this line. Hello GNOC, We have news about your ticket IOT-43101: Hello. Our partners indicate that the issue was either on your side or on the client devices. Do you have any localization of the issue on the client or device side? Viktor. Kind Regards,

comments.author: Ed Guigou
comments.body: Per Velos, They are not seeing any issue. I requested they close their ticket. Hello. We have reviewed the data and have not observed any drops in this network on the overall chart. Our partners requested information about what you are seeing on your side to confirm. If there are no new findings and the issue is considered resolved, we can proceed to close this ticket. Viktor.

summary: Maingate: Swap end of life router BBO-C3925-1/2 to bbo-c8300-1/2
description: The aim of project is to swap the end of life and support router(s) used on Maingate network EndofSale Date : 20111101 EndofSupport Date : 2016110 We will shutdown the network interfaces of the old router and activate the interfaces on the new router, the traffic will switch automatically since we have the same configuration (with minor changes). The routers being replaced are used for internal communication (such as Backend and DMZ) in maingate. The expected impact will be arround 10/15 minutes each. Maingate services are mainly used in the EU / Sweden area so this change will only impact the ECC type services for users in those geo regions.The expected impact will be 24 network hits lasting ~5 minutes each. We should mark all services at Maingate impacted during this time (SMS, GPRS, Voice, Maingate manager, etc). Since these routers are internal to the network monitoring will be impacted also.

summary: EXT: Syniverse - SS7 | 005-066-029 | Numerex DPC bouncing (NSI)
description: Syniverse is observing multiple SS7 links and loop alerts. Syniverse SS7 | 005066029 | Numerex DPC bouncing GNOC received an email from Syniverse stating that Syniverse detected multiple alarms for the next SS7 links and asked us to check and verify the same from our side. They also requested to reset the following link. Current Status: Syniverse is checking with their Network Support SS7 team. The provider is still observing bouncing links after L3 SME restarted process from the Semtech side. SME advised provider that the issue is likely on the Syniverse end. SME reported, “We have very low traffic and we restarted all process. Last time this occurred, it was on the syniverse end.“ SME reported that this issue is most likely on the Syniverse side and there is relatively no impact. The connection(s) were for old Airadigm SIMs that are barely taking on any traffic. As we know, we are using the Syniverse SS7 link between NAM and EU, hence we reached out to SME for the validation and reset the link. Note: We checked the KPI and did not find any major variation in the traffic. Below needs to be reset: Please also try performing a reset on these links. Customer Name : Numerex Customer Site Location : Madison WI Customer Point Code : 005066029 Linkset : M3UA ASP AM361081 Customer Name : Numerex Customer Site Location : Madison WI Customer Point Code : 005066029 Linkset : M3UA ASP AM360081 Next Action Item: GNOC has sent the initial acknolodge email to Syniverse and need to take followup from SME.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: GNOC reached out to the SME again and is still awaiting feedback from the L3 NEO team. At 2025-05-02 15:10 UTC Syniverse Command Center requested an update and escalation.  The provider also reported multiple loop alerts observed.  See email correspondence below. From:  Syniverse Command Center < scc@syniverse.com >  Sent:  Friday, May 2, 2025 11:10 AM To:  Global NOC < GlobalNOC@semtech.com >; Syniverse Command Center < scc@syniverse.com >; Minh Tran < minh.tran@semtech.com >; Ed Guigou < eguigou@semtech.com >; Robert Brumbeloe < rbrumbeloe@semtech.com > Cc:  CSEmails < CSEmails@syniverse.com >; Gregory Selig < gselig@semtech.com >; Kevin Wohlrabe < kwohlrabe@semtech.com > Subject:  RE: SS7 | 005-066-029 | Numerex DPC bouncing | INC000001632644||GNOC-8012   Hello Numerex team,   Do you have any updates regarding this ticket?   Also please note that we have noticed multiple loop alerts coming from the same point codes (see attached)   A ticket has been created for a proper follow-up of the loop case.   SS7 | Looping detected on range 31064 / 19207 |7522-0-0/7521-32-0 TO 168-67-5 Numerex| INC000001632832   Please escalate the case and let us know what the status is, we think both the loop and the bouncing alerts are related and we need you to fix it.   Let us know if you need any assistance from our SS7 team.   Regards, Mario Sanchez NOC Technician T +1 813.233.7870 mario.sanchez@syniverse.com syniverse ® The world’s most connected company™ Website  /  Twitter  /  LinkedIn

comments.body: Greetings   !!  seems the issue is not fixed yet, Syniverse has replied that the links are bouncing, and the looping alerts are triggering every few minutes. They requested us to investigate further!!  Attaching email for reference:

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: At 2025-05-03 13:38 UTC Minh Tran explained to Syniverse via email that, “We have very low traffic and we restarted all process. Last time this occurred, it was on the syniverse end.“ At 2025-05-03 13:43 UTC GNOC attached an older email thread that referred to the last time 005-066-029 point code links had an issue, i.e., INC000001526510,  .  Syniverse resolved that issue last year, November 6th, 2024, with a session reset on their Firewall connection. At 2025-05-03 13:58 UTC the Syniverse team mentioned that,  “ We are going to involve our Network Support - SS7 team to check further.” Next Steps:  Awaiting feedback from the Syniverse SCC/Network Support teams.

comments.author: dram

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: called up  813.233.7870  just now…for an update for INC000001632644… Individual who answered queried ticket number within their database and found a later update on 5/3.. once their SS7 support team got involved…. their records show an update internally, stating that the links later on showed as stabilized; the person on the phone explained that they didn’t document updating Durga or anyone else from the GNOC.  They advised that if we saw the links down again that we should call them back.  But, as of 5/3 15:31 EST/19:31 they were seeing the connection back to normal between them and us.

comments.author: Arabinda Mishra
comments.body: Hello All, Update from Syniverse that they are observing the link to be stable. Although we have observed link is stable from our end,    would you like to double check the same as requested by Syniverse. Syniverse----------- Dear Colleague,               We are observing the links to be stable for now. Can you please check and confirm if the issue is still being observed at your end,             Let us know if you have any questions.  Tripurari Sharan Sr. Network Engineer Semtech ----------- Thank you Tripurari for the Feedback,   We will check the status internally & will get back to you if we still see any issues.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: Syniverse continues to pursue Semtech to investigate this ticket when the Syniverse team has not yet provided their findings from the Syniverse Network Support - SS7 and Firewall teams.  Semtech cleared its network on May 3 rd  and Minh reset the SS7 links from the Semtech side.  Once Tripurari Sharan, Sr. Network Engineer, with Syniverse confirmed that Syniverse was stable, GNOC should have asked them for the cause/resolution and the closing their ticket. Last time (Oct.-Nov. ’24) Syniverse had to reset its Firewall links.  Thus, in this case, Syniverse should be confirming their investigation findings to GNOC, not the other way around. In the meantime Minh and I have responded to Syniverse and are awaiting their feedback, see email correspondence below. Awareness:

summary: Alert: SC - CARRIER SCS Sigtran Traffic Increase (CSI)
description: Since 20251012 03:30 UTC, singe customer CARRIERSCS started to show increase of traffic for Sigtran, Diameter, Radius and Vssf. Upon investigating into Kibana, GNOC saw that the some devices were stuck in RNA, others are constantly connecting and dropped off the network, and some were reregistering back on to the network successfully. GNOC checked AV and saw that the customer has been performing a lot provisioning work, hence, GNOC believe the anomaly increase is due to the customer. GNOC will continue to monitor this new pattern. Start time: 20251012 03:30 UTC End time: 20251012 04:00 UTC Duration: 30 min Impacted customers: CARRIER SCS Impacted VPLMNs: Polkomtel Sp. z o.o. summary: ALERT IMPROVEMENT REQUEST - MSU CHANGE FOR JT SIGNALING. raising max number to 65 for jtstp2
description: While we did receive a spate of JT signaling alerts both for STP links and for connection to the DRA on 7/13, Sunday, which led to a GNOC ticket (8812), & did receive 36 P2 alerts over the course of Week 28, coupled to MSU counts for thresholds between 60 to 65. This points to a need to improve the regular MSU count alerts to above the 60 count. either stop notifying us of counts below 75 , or raise it up to 65+ at least? Current max threshold is set at 60 Example alert below f84567a910ba4805a58683b2417e18601752141671173 Event Details... current 61.7666666667 description High Linkset Throughput detected escalationcount 9 how exceeded isarchive true manager 2c8cb0d3a516 max 60 zenoss.device.deviceclass /Server/Linux/Ouroboros zenoss.device.ipaddress 46.23.241.244 zenoss.device.priority 3

This points to a need to improve the regular MSU count alerts to above the 60 count. either stop notifying us of counts below 75 , or raise it up to 65+ at least? Current max threshold is set at 60 Example alert below f84567a910ba4805a58683b2417e18601752141671173 Event Details... current 61.7666666667 description High Linkset Throughput detected escalationcount 9 how exceeded isarchive true manager 2c8cb0d3a516 max 60 zenoss.device.deviceclass /Server/Linux/Ouroboros zenoss.device.ipaddress 46.23.241.244 zenoss.device.priority 3

comments.author: Mohamed Amine Elbahi
comments.body: : I don’t understand the requirement here. For STP-J on 13/07 there was some issue of links that why we had alerting there was interruption of link diameter with JT as well on 13/07 Could you please clarify what’s the request in this ticket?  for awareness

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Amine, Yes, we are aware of the alerts for the July 13th flare up.    However , the alerts on other dates alerting for counts above the current threshold of  60, were fairly numerous.  f84567a9-10ba-4805-a586-83b2417e1860-1752141671173   vma-telfrt-05.mobiquithings.com : Linkset stp_j_m1_vty_ jstp2  speed too high: 64.7 MSU/s  This alert came in on 7/10,  And saw no discernible network fault occurring at the time.   vma-telfrt-05.mobiquithings.com : Linkset stp_j_m1_vty_jstp2 speed too high: MSU/s   There are about 15 some odd alerts for the year for JTSTP1, with the max threshold set at 90 Event Details for a jstp1 alert current 92.9322033898 description High Linkset Throughput detected max 90 These alerts come in not nearly as frequently for JSTP1,  and when they do so ,  their MSU counts are much higher than what we are getting for JSTP2 For jstp2, we would be better off if the threshold was raised to anything above 75 MSU. Below, is a sampling of the jstp1 alerts which are more indicative of a network issue/

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Based on the descriptions and comparisons of the JT stp linksets below, can we make stp2 match the max threshold of jstp1, which is currently set at 90?  An alert for jstp2, the max is currently set at 60, thus we’re receiving too many alerts.  5b61dfab-7218-497e-96f9-b72902b4f790-1749651791023  vma-telfrt-05.mobiquithings.com : Linkset stp_j_m1_vty_jstp2 speed too high: 61.4 MSU/s For jstp1 the max is set to 90 currently, and these breaches of the thresholds do seem worth alerting on. d3f32c77-06b5-456f-987f-284f886931bd-1752575411478 vma-telfrt-05.mobiquithings.com : Linkset stp_j_m1_vty_jstp1 speed too high: 113.3 MSU/s

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Gentlemen, although we aren’t receiving a huge amount of alerts for JT signaling surges exceeding MSU counts at stp2, the most recent one that showed up on 9/1, was for where it exceeded the max of 60 by a quantity of 61.5, which doesn’t look to be a remarkable breach. f3029975-bfc7-4f3a-92c0-eb2b8ecac927-1756725535430 vma-telfrt-05.mobiquithings.com : Linkset stp_j_m1_vty_jstp2 speed too high: 61.5 MSU/s The example below from 8/27 seems more noteworthy, and worth alerting us on, since the MSU count breached at 95.6. Could we confirm whether the alerting threshold has moved up to the neighborhood of 65 to 70? 8c880ca7-89e4-4c4b-9ef3-ac845353d528-1756282855856 vma-telfrt-05.mobiquithings.com : Linkset stp_j_m1_vty_jstp2 speed too high: 95.6 MSU/s Thanks.

summary: Alert Improvement :increase alert priority for VIP customer for KIBANA observability Alert
description: Greetings !! In some scenarios, GNOC receives KIBANA observability Alert for different customers for different scenarios. Sometimes the impacted customer name is missing from the alert & it populates as “Others”. It would be really helpful for GNOC if we can have all the impacted customer names in the alert . If we have a VIP customer impacted then the severity of the alert can be changed to P2 & the alert can have the word VIP to have more attention on it from GNOC monitoring perspective. Kindly review the request.

summary: Alert Improvement : Location Update thresholds for Each Roaming Partner
description: Greetings !!! Many times GNOC receive different P2 alerts , which subsequently ends up with Service degradation for our customers in most of the cases. The opsGenie alert we get shows overall combined KPIs for different Roaming partner. It does not specify which Roaming partner has issues or thresholds not met. So from GNOC perspective we tend to check all Roaming partner KPIs when we receive such alert. If we can have Individual Roaming partner’s Location update threshold set & if we get opsGenie alert specifying which Roaming Partner has issues/threshold not met then it will save us a lot of time. Kindly review the request. ZENOSSLink Example of alerts → PS Update location Ratio too low CS Update location Ratio too low Cancel location update ratio too low etc.

summary: ALERT IMPROVEMENT REQUEST - Kibana Observability for Invers
description: Greetings Julien and co: We recently had an issue get reported to us for Invers, where they experienced data connectivity issues on Telefonica Germany. COFI 2862 was brought to our attention from the L2s via a Slack channel. We queried recent OpsGenie Alerts from the 6th through the 8th with Invers and/or kibanaobservability in the message row. No results back in which specified Invers . However, there was one for Synox. Could we configure OpsGenie to alert us specifically for Invers with kibanaobservability at the beginning of the message? Thank you for your help.

summary: NAM: Syniverse 10-gig fibers - enable for traffic
description: Cab is to enable the additional 10 gig fibers we have with Syniverse. No customer impact is expected but incase of issues we thought it was best to notify customers. This is a fortification of our Syniverse connectivity and gives us much greater redundancy. No cab has been added as all the work is on the Synivese side to open traffic. GNOC should monitor NAM PGW traffic during this migration. should also monitor SS7 links to SMSC in NAM to ensure they remain operational. The CAB is for NAM region only and will not impact traffic to EU PGW / EU SS7 / EU Diameter Links.

comments.author: Rashied Lataillade
comments.body: Greetings  , So this activity is actually an ordered circuit augment from Syniverse that is being turned-up on the provider side (for additional redundancy), is that correct?  Initially, I thought this activity was an internal Semtech CR CAB, if impacting, would require enough customer notice lead time, per the process 10 business days if critical, and 5 business days if otherwise.  If possible, could your team consider rescheduling the activity to allow sufficient lead time?  That way, we (CAB team) can review (discuss) and vote on it for approval prior to any status page notification. If there is no way to reschedule this activitiy (or turn-up rather) due to provider coordination, please let us know as soon as possible.  If this is critical anf the date is manadatory, you can resubmit or change this activity as an ECR (which has a shorter lead time), once you get EAP approval, GNOC can move forward sending out a statuspage notice as an Emergency Change in notifying customers.  Did Syniverse submit a provider MOP or is there some work order that can be reviewed, if a MOP is not fromally submitted? Awareness:

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Minh did ping Harvey Jordan at Verizon at 18:53 UTC for some clarification on what changes they made as of 9/11 for the Verizon Radius servers.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: spoke to Jordan HArvey within the past 45 minutes…he understood the recent upgrades between semtech and their side, could have potential radius visibility impact, but was not aware of any direct service impact.. spoke to jovon at NAM and Robert James afterwards, and Innovative FOTO and Toyota material handling have both experienced 0 byte on outbound direction after the 11th.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: there is actually a call slated for 13:00 CST…with Verizon….we will treat the Innovative FOTO users as the top, initial sampling of users experiencing 0 byte session and then get an answer to the larger issue of Data insights feeds showing a surge of 0 byte session.  Goal is to determine if this is simply a visibility issue.  Just sent over the email with 4 ICCIDs from the SF ticket that Jovon has.  89148000007172194959 89148000007172196749 89148000007172198109 89148000007172197069 @12:12 CST, Jordan Harvey had responded with the following Can you provide some MDN examples that you are seeing with the 0-byte session that you are receiving. That way our vMPN Proxy team can do some traces.  There was an update last Thursday to update the rest of Branchburg nodes but that should not have impacted what you are seeing. So the team wants to check what is being sent from the Gateway and forwarded to you via the vMPN Proxy.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: last outbound transaction of bytes was on 9/11 at around 2:00 EST

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: on the bridge, we broached the subject of 4 new tunnels recently created as of 9/10, possibility of prepends could be sending traffic incorrectly.. Kevin Worhlabe is in Frankfurt for Nutanix issue… proposed turning down the 4 recently created tunnels… Verizon is reaching out to Jen (VZW), employee/engineer to tear down these newer tunnels and we’ll see if this improves the up and downlink of bytes… Kevin is not able to pull up the ip routes immediately while on the call.  it would take him an hour to two hours to pull up semtech’s view.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Kevin has gotten confirmation of the tunnels getting taken down from our side (had reached out to Adam)… however, the surge in our radius feeds for 0 bytes could be due to the Branchburg change made on 9/11.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: spoke to jovon just now on the phone via MS teams, and for the SIM card 89148000007172196749 shows a recent increase in outbound traffic in thingspace after the tunnels were turned down.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: While Innovative FOTO is showing improvement… DNP Imagingcomm America Corporation …. Jordan responded at 4:16 pm  “Afternoon Minh and Lewie,   Our vMPN team is still looking into the traces, one thing we have noticeds is with the new update to the interim feed the Start record normally would have a "null" in the value for Acct-Input-Octets & Acct-Output-Octets but after the update it starts with a 0.Since it is the start of the accounting session there should not be any usage yet, but it does show on your report as part of the 0-Byte sessions. I will keep you posted as I receive any additional updates.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Summary of Two separate ECC issues 9/15 to 9/16   Issue (1) – 9/11 customer impacting issue reported from Innovative FOTO SF (01884417)                  Issue (2)–   9/11 Radius records within Data Insights displaying surge in 0-bytes        (GNOC-9529)                 ·         (1) Innovative FOTO (an ECC customer) reported inability for their devices to communicate POS updates to their main server(s). ·         (2) Queries within the Z_DRE - ECC SIM RADIUS dashboard did point to a severe increase in 0-byte sessions starting on 9/11 ·         (2) While at the same time, there didn’t seem to be a loss of 2-way traffic over the past five days. ·         (1) 9/16– Conference call with Verizon and Semtech attendees ·         (1) Verizon brought up recently activated tunnels between Verizon and Semtech, which had prepends adjusted between 9/9 & 9/11. ·         (1) Semtech contacted Kevin Worhlabe, who had Adam deactivate the tunnels. ·         (1) Jordan at Verizon requested their engineer (Jenn) do the same. ·         (1) NAM team confirmed Innovative FOTO uploads were processing fine. ·         (2) Post-call, Verizon advised their vPMN group would continue researching how recent updates for the remainder of their Branchburg nodes could be causing our feeds to show up as 0-byte sessions. ·         (2) Most recent update from Verizon – their new updates to Interim feed, show Start records are now inserting  0  instead of  null  as the value for input-octets and output-octets.  The information we receive is flagging this as 0-byte sessions.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Email sent over to us on 9/16 at23:55 UTC  Update on Radius Accounting Messages We’ve resolved the issue in Radius interim and stop messages where  Acct-Output-Octets  was incorrectly set to  0 . Additionally, we’ve verified that when multiple  Diameter Accounting-Input-Octets  or  Accounting-Output-Octets  are received in a single message, the MPN-Proxy now correctly aggregates these values and forwards them as  Radius Acct-Input-Octets  and  Acct-Output-Octets . However, this enhancement introduced a side effect: the MPN-Proxy now sends  Radius Acct-Input-Octets  and  Acct-Output-Octets  with a value of  0  in cases where previously no values were sent at all. Verizon’s current analysis of Radius SNAFU Start records are still receiving  0  values. According to specifications, start messages should  not  include  Radius Acct-Input-Octets  or  Acct-Output-Octets , so suppressing these fields will require additional design work. Question Posed to Semtech from Verizon  Would it be possible for your reporting dashboard to ignore  0-byte  values specifically for start records? This could help minimize any impact on your reporting accuracy. Also, are you seeing any other downstream effects from start records containing  0  values? Your feedback will help us assess whether further changes are needed.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: Later on at17:04 UTC, Jordan Harvey advsd Minh That with both feeds being active from their side, they still need to update their other VMPN sites (colorado springs and salt lake) with the idential sw version. Interim accounting stream from their Branchburg location is currently including cumulative data usage for the session getting totaled in each interim record.   Start records will include0 within the acc-input-octet and acct-output-octet (if the vMPN team quires failover to Colorado or SL before the sw upgrade, the interim accting records would only show usage during interim session….  Radius accounting feed with start/stops also includes total usage within stop records.. after colorado and Salt Lake are upgraded, their MPN team will remove start/stop acct feed

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: within the past 20 to 30 minutes, I sent an email to Jordan Harvey @ jordan.harvey@verizonwireless.com  to how they were progressing in rectifying the radius feeds over to us.. to where we no longer see spurious quantities of 0-byte session.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: I just emailed Jordan Harvey at Verizon the following: Jordan, I queried the feed for 0-bytes sessions, and since the 10 th  through today, the 22 nd , we still show elevated levels, per the display feed. Do you have any news on those Colorado and Salt Lake connections getting updated?, and if so, do you have an estimated time of the feeds getting back to normal?

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: tried calling Jordan at 9196340759 via 8 by 8 and had difficulty making a call out; called him from my cell and left him a vm, describing the issue of 0 bytes feeds showing up still as elevated on our side, although we don’t believe is CSI.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: at the  Z_DRE - ECC SIM RADIUS  dashboard  I required the 0 byte session counts from 9/10 up the current day, the 29th and the feeds claim 0 byte sessions remain elevated. While 2 way traffic is still coming in at a decent flow.

comments.author: Lewie Spearman
comments.body: I just called and left a voicemail withmy personal cell number, advising we are still seeing elevated feeds for 0 byte sessions.. although this looks to be non customer impacting.

summary: ALERT: Lumen Technologies LSP-MSN Inter-site 443976315 Circuit Down, because of Maintenance (NSI)
description: Lumen Technologies LSP < > MSN Intersite 443976315 Circuit Down. No Impact due to Redundancy. At 20251009 17:09 UTC the Lumen Technologies 443976315 intersite circuit was Down between Semtech’s Madison and Lithia Springs data centers. GNOC engaged the provider to investigate to confirm any change activities or issues that need to be address on the Lumen network. GNOC got feedback from Lumen that the circuit is down is because of a planned maintenance . (Maintenance time 05:00 UTC to 11:00 UTC) Lumen Maintenance Ticket Number → P270B002 GNOC found that, email was shared from Lumen but we did not received it because of issues with Mimecast email filtering system. FINAL Update: Circuit is UP, Lumen updated that the issue was because of a planned maintenance which GNOC was not aware off. But later it was found that GNOC did not receive the corresponding email from Lumen because of issues with Mimecast email filtering system Start Time: October 9, 2025 17:09 UTC Stop Time: October 9, 2025 17:53 UTC.
